title: Not ready for prime time: Too many motion events, terrible battery life, no resolution for problems reported.
body: Update Jan 2018:I'm giving up on these. I had two of them (this and a Ring 2) and they both miserably fail to do their job properly. Frequent disconnects from the network (when I know the network is fine), several minute delay of relaying a ring to the chime and phones, not announcing that batteries are about to go (so they don't work for days until someone knocks and I know the doorbell is no longer online because of the battery), and I can't use them at all to detect motion due to its inferior motion detection system, abysmal interface and total lack of options for mounting and software knobs to achieve a somewhat sane surveillance mode, so it's just an expensive doorbell, nothing else. Doesn't even work half of the time. Two different models! I have to say that customer service, once I escalated the original issue, was superb in that they tried replacing the original one and then were nice enough to send me a Ring 2 without charge. Sadly, the product is not on par with this. I can't wait for the Blink version to come out to try that, but for now I'll go back to my simple, dumb 20 bucks doorbell set. This one never failed me, once: [[ASIN:B00J4FVZU6 SadoTech Model CX Wireless Doorbell with 1 Receiver Plugin and 2 Remote Buttons Operating at over 500-feet Range with Over 50 Chimes, No Batteries Required for Receiver, (White), Fixed Code C Series]]June 2017:When I first installed the door bell it worked somewhat great, with all the motion detection bells and whistles turned on. I also have a Chime so I can hear if someone rings the doorbell without having to rely on my phone all the time, and added the 30 bucks a year subscription to save motion/ring events in the cloud.After a few months the nightmare of constant noise/notifications from motion alerts started. Nothing had changed. Neither the position of the device, the settings, or frequency of traffic. I emailed support, which was an utterly disappointing experience.They first tried to get me to change the settings to make it less chatty. When I questioned that, they tried to explain how the motion detection works and that the range control doesn't actually control the range but size of heat signatures picked up by the motion sensor. Say what? Fine. Don't care, just tell me how to fix it. They recommended pointing it down a bit more (by buying one of their wedge kits), which I did. No change. Change settings again, no change. I recommended to add a still image behind the range settings screen at some point so I don't have to keep printing out screenshots of the range settings view and a live view screen to overlay them (which apparently, since the range doesn't do what it says on the tin, is utterly useless anyway) - ignored (this is of course reserved for the more premium model).Multiple back and forth emails later and I still can't use motion detection (which now also seems to eat up all the battery every 2-3 weeks - because for whatever reason motion detection and silent reporting still happens even if it's "turned off"). They continuously fail to address my concern that nothing has changed on my end and it has to be a camera/doorbell issue. When I cancelled my subscription and asked if I can get a partial refund, they stopped responding altogether.I'm about to ditch this thing, go back to my cheap wireless doorbell and add a SmartThings integrated camera to watch over the front porch. This product is utterly useless for this purpose, and I don't need a brick as a doorbell that needs to be charged every three weeks.For a device that costs 200 bucks (I include the cost for the video storage subscription here) I expect more - it should work, I shouldn't have to learn or understand the inner workings of the motion detection mechanism, the UX should be intuitive, I expect decent battery life, proper and working functionality, and customer service should be stellar. None of that applies, unfortunately.Update from June 2017: After I could not find any setting to quiet down the motion notifications, customer service kindly sent a replacement and I returned the original. Unfortunately the same issues are still happening.I just can't seem to find any setting that's not going to make the doorbell go after every car going by even with the motion detection turned off. I have it set to the least sensitive settings, purchased a wedge kit to point it downward to avoid the street, and changed motion zones to all conceivable options, all to no avail. All points to software faults at this point (and perhaps systemic hardware design issues as well).The device is therefore useless for me for the following points:* I can not turn off the motion detection completely, even if I turn it off on the app it still triggers. It's ridiculous that turning it off doesn't work or is just per app suppression of notifications!* My partner's phone app which I have shared the door bell with also has the motion detection turned off but still gets the notifications.* The constant reacting to motion drains the battery - I don't have a regular doorbell outlet and am not willing to invest in custom wiring to feed it with power. That's the whole reason I've bought this device so I can be independent of a power supply!I am extremely unhappy with this.

title: There's PROS, and then there's CONS (plenty of them).
body: I didn't buy this on Amazon, but I do have it. I have the Ring Doorbell 2. It's been 2 months, and for the price, this is (well, I won't say it), but it definitely is not worth the price; not even half the price.Many of these items are kudos and/or complaints from other customers too. So I'm not the only one!PROS - It's easy to install (physically) - No problems connecting to home wi-fi, if you're in a good range. * If you're a sucker like me, and bought it, then please read the instructions. - Video quality is only good, if you have the perfect lighting, and the short range. * Basically, best video is only in the middle of the day. - Works with your phone, after installing app. Slow, but it does work. * If you are someone with little patience, then this might be a CON. - It's a great quick intercom system for your door, and the 'live' video feed works. * Did I mention it is slow, so while someone is ringing at your door, maybe 30 seconds later, you get the video feed, and see who it is. - 1 month free of video logging online - You can 'manually' save a 30-sec video clip to your phone. - Neighbor Feed - you can see what a neighbor (5 mile max radius) in the area has posted online on Ring * Great for detecting if some mysterious person might be in your neighborhood * You get a 'general' area of where the incident occurred (say, within a 1000 foot area; not exact on this #)CONS - 30 second videos only. If something happens, you only get 30 seconds. - Monthly service fee. (OK... it's only $3 a month to save your 30 second video files online) * But why wouldn't Ring provide other methods to save your info? Perhaps in version 5 or later. - Motion Detection is SLOW to start. * By the time the doorbell does detect a person, 5 to 10 seconds may have already passed before it starts recording, so you miss the initial stuff. - Motion Detection triggers massive amount of false alarms. * There is configuration for this to minimize this (which works a little), but it's not great. * You can shut off any Motion Alerts completely, but then... WHY DID YOU BUY THIS FOR? * Things like tree leaves shadows trigger motion, so it's NOT a thermal detection (well, completely by itself anyways), it is a video image detection too. - Video quality is mostly bad. Anything less than perfect lighting makes video almost useless. * Excess lighting creates glare, and not sharpness. * Not enough lighting creates dark (or) jumps to black/white night time mode, which is grainy * Any distance beyond 5 feet is fuzzy, even though... this is a "high res" camera. - Initial set up is horrible. * Prepare to yet, create another new account with Ring. To use it, (and it doesn't say this), you have to reboot the app. Log off and back on. It shouldn't require you to have to call Ring to set this up, but they are available. - Help support via the App is horrible. * CHAT starts up without ability to search through their Knowledge Base. Just have to start typing. * When CHAT is started, it (Ring) asks what the problem is. It's an automated chat initial questionnaire. (That's not the bad part). When someone finally does get your case, they ask you all the same questions again. (If you've ever experienced that, you know how frustrating it is... and then to have to type it in again, through your phone's keyboard just adds to the frustration... but maybe that's just me, having to use my phone's keyboard to type in a conversation is not a preferred method of communications; although texting is fine with a sentence here or there) - Neighborhood Feed (I know... this was a PRO, but...) * Viewing the neighbor's 30-sec clip might work; or it might not. Some of the videos are just still images, and not necessarily playable. * I tried to work with Ring Help, to see why the posts were not videos, or was it my phone, or was it the post by the Neighbor themselves. Either way, working with Ring support was a fiasco. - Did I mention the price? Comparable to other products out there, I'm not sure I got a good deal.Uggh, I gotta go. There's a few more, but I think I got the major ones.

title: Cool idea, fails miserably.
body: Buyer beware - really cool idea, but it's not ready for prime time. I installed my Ring and was excited to put it to use... but within minutes we had MAJOR issues.When you press the doorbell button, my phone would notify me (yay!), my doorbell would ring (yay!)... and then my doorbell would KEEP ringing (boo!)... for the full 30 seconds that Ring tries to reach you on your phone (double boo!).ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong...I contacted Ring support and they asked me switch the red/white wires and the diode directions. (Keep in mind, my install EXACTLY matched their instructions: power to the right terminal, diode facing the doorbell, power exits the left terminal). Eventually I was able to find a combination of diode direction and wire combinations that made the doorbell ring only once. (yay!)Then I enabled motion detection. Whenever someone would walk in front of my door, my phone would notify me (yay!) .... and then my doorbell would ring (strange, but tolerable)... and then my doorbell would keep ringing (boo!)... for the full 30 seconds that Ring tries to reach you on your phone (double boo!)...ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong...I contact Ring support again and they said it must be my transformer or my doorbell. So I went out to my local Home Depot and purchased a brand new doorbell and a brand new transformer. At this point the only thing "old" in the system was the wires. STILL HAPPENED.ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong...I tested all of the voltages across all of the wires and triple checked them against the Ring specifications / install guide. Everything was within spec.ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong...I contacted Ring support again and they said I must have bought an incompatible doorbell. They linked me off to some hidden web page that shows a couple dozen doorbell models and whether or not they are compatible. Both my old doorbell and my new doorbell were not listed on either list. I figured I would try a THIRD doorbell, specifically one of the compatible models. Unfortunately Home Depot doesn't sell ANY of the compatible models, so I had to order one from a large online retailer. Two days later the new doorbell arrived... I installed it. STILL HAPPENED.ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong...I contacted Ring support again and they offered to replace the unit with a new one... maybe mine was defective. A week later, the new one arrived... I installed it... STILL HAPPENED.ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong... ding-dong...I contacted Ring support again and they said: "Gee I don't know, your install isn't compatible I guess. You can try buying this new Ring Pro, or you can disconnect your doorbell and buy this WiFi doorbell that you have to plug in somewhere." My install IS compatible, since it exactly matches their installation diagram. I don't WANT another doorbell taking up another square foot of wallspace... I already have one.I threw my Ring in the trash and went back to a regular doorbell button. You can't say I didn't try... I tried EVERYTHING.Buyer beware, this thing just isn't ready for prime time. If you ask me, the repeating doorbell is a software issue but they refuse to acknowledge it or fix it.

title: Not quite there yet.
body: I purchased this at a local big box store using a discount that Ring promoted in our neighborhood…turns out I didn't need their discount as it was far less expensive there. So far, I've had several chats with tech support and all of them have been odd. In one instance, I was told I'd need to have a faster internet speed for this thing to work well…the tech person checked and then told me THEY had my speed set too slow, and fixed it. Nope. No fix. Then they said I needed a wifi boost, but at the last call had said I actually had a good internet speed. My wifi router is less than 12 feet from the doorbell!My LARGEST issues with Ring so far are:1. You can't see a person when they walk up. Five times I've missed people coming to the door because either the doorbell didn't work or the motion detection didn't work. And a larger problem than this, is the delay in recording. I NEVER see people's faces. I get a real good shot of them walking away. Not helpful in crime detection. In a couple instances, people came and went and I received no recordings and no messages.2. It won't pick up motion of a person, but it picks up plants quite nicely blowing in the wind.3. Because you can't really control the brightness on this thing, if it's under the eves of the house, as mine is, and facing out to the street on a sunny day, it's BLINDINGLY bright. Pretty much completely blasted with brightness to the point that I can't see past about two feet in front of my door. It's all white. I was told by tech that they were working on having this be adjustable to deal with this in a future firmware update. We shall see.4. I can't change the annoying and silly ring. Mine is hardwired, so I hear both MY doorbell AND the ring doorbell. Can't I choose what my bell sounds like, oh say, like a bell maybe? I can't stand, and I get it that this is totally personal preference, the silly shrilly whirly whoosh that the bell creates when run or when motion occurs. It's silly.5. On line, while researching at their site, I see these brilliantly clear videos..wide angles….visually stunning cinematography…. In reality, it's a fish eye lens that distorts anyone into a vowel shape who stands still long enough for the recording to kick in, and it's hurky-jerky video at best.6. I paid up front for their year of cloud, resentfully because I'm sick of this cloud and that cloud and those clouds and these clouds out there now that sees people paying for things they buy in perpetuity. Couldn't ALL of these clouds get together and just be one glorious super cloud?? Couldn't ring come up with a say to save things locally so that if I don't want Mr. Nosy Cloud Puss staring at my blinding white front yard all day I could, oh say, record to my own device??I could rant onward. But I'm hopeful. I'm hopeful that Ring gets it together. I want to like this thing===for the money I spent even at Big Box land, it ought to work flawlessly…but right now, it does not. And it's interesting. I wrote a review locally on our neighborhood watch site at a point where only problem one existed for me…and in almost dizzying speed, a Ring person emailed me---wanting to help. That's great, but there was really no help than could fix the issues. I believe most of the issues like with Ring's firmware. Right now, I'm hopeful that they will fix the glitches that plague so many people now and get this Ring thing working properly. I'm pretty much resolved that it's better than nothing, but honestly if Blink came out with something similar that could be placed outdoors…I'd rip this thing down in a heartbeat.UPDATE: I've had to downgrade this to one star. With each new update, and I think I've had this now a couple years, the doorbell grows more sloth like. Just now, I had a delivery placed on the doorstep. The guy rang the bell, but all it caught of him was the little bit of his heel as he walked away, and it took well over a minute for the ring app. to connect. How on earth is this ueseful? Will someone PLEASE make a better product? I feel like I've tossed my money away. It catches trucks, squirrels, random dogs, even birds at times, but it does NOT catch people at all.

title: First 1 was dead on arrival. 2nd one needed to be less than 10' from the router to work
body: I bought 2 of these and neither worked correctly.Some help for people having trouble. If it's not working correctly:Hard reset: Hold the orange button on the back for 30 secondsIf it's still not working correctly:Factory Reset: Hold the orange button and the front button for 30 secondsIf it's still not working plug it into your computer with the supplied USB cord. If it doesn't light up any lights it's completely dead and only good for a paperweight.If these do not work do not waste your time with Ring's customer support. Simply return it to Amazon.The first device I purchased wasn't working when it came out of the box. The front doorbell button was very stiff and barely moved when you pressed it. The device didn't ring when I pressed the button either. I called customer support and was happy to find their customer support is based in the US. Unfortunately, their customer service knows virtually nothing about their product. Through many calls to their customer support we finally figured out it was broken straight from the factory. They said they'd send a replacement right away. After a week and a half without hearing anything from Ring I called to follow up. Ring had entered my email information completely incorrectly and was waiting to hear back from me with what color I wanted. I told them we verified silver, the only color they had in stock, over the phone when they first told me they'd send a replacement. I waited 9 more days without hearing anything from Ring and then called back again to inquire about my replacement. They told me it had been shipped the same day I called but couldn't tell me the tracking number. She told me I'd need to talk with a tech to get that information. Seemed strange but she told me a tech would call me back within an hour. I waited a full day before calling back again. I again asked for a tracking number and was again told I'd need to speak to a tech and told to wait for a call back. I told him instead to cancel my order and further that I didn't believe it had even been shipped because neither UPS, FedEx, or USPS even have a shipping method that could take as long as they claim to have taken. Here we are another week later and I still haven't received the supposedly shipped item.So, I contacted Amazon for a replacement. Obviously the route I should have taken in the first place. Amazon sent a replacement 2 days later. I charged it and connected it without issue to my network. I tested it before installing and it worked OK. The person using the ring has to be pretty patient as it takes 30 - 45 seconds to send a message to the phone. The person using the ring also has to speak loudly and clearly to be picked up by the ring's microphone. Finally, the person using the ring has to put up with a very noticeable echo of their own voice being picked up by the phone's microphone and sent back to the ring.I installed it on the wall at my front door and the call took over a minute to connect to the phone. The video was blocky and spotty, delivering a stop motion style video. Audio was so broken up it was not usable from either end. I ended the call and tried again. It took nearly 2 minutes to connect and had the same video and audio issues. I tried a third time and it failed to connect entirely. A fourth time and after approximately 2 minutes it again connected with terrible video and audio. I removed the Ring from the wall and walked it towards the router while testing audio and video. Not until I was less than 10' from my router could the ring deliver satisfactory video or audio. It also couldn't deliver satisfactory video or audio when on the other side of a wall but only a few feet from the router. The router is 802.11n and 300mbps. My other devices show full bars where the ring was mounted at the front door. The router also has no trouble streaming HD video and sound to multiple devices throughout the house, several much further away than the front door.I really wanted to love this but unless I'm willing to install my router outside right next to the ring at the front door it's just not ready for prime-time.

title: NOT READY FOR PRIME(Time). I REALLY wanted this to work well!
body: I purchased the Ring video doorbell directly from Ring.com (on March 3, 2016) because the "Ring Stick Up" cam was not yet available at Amazon (see attached copy of my email receipt". Also, I ordered the Ring Chime which acts like a tradational doorbell when someone rings the bell or motion is detected, the chime rings. The chime is necassary because otherwise, the only notifications you will receive will be on your smartphone, tablet or Windows10 computer.Total spent $456:- $199 - Ring Video Doorbell- $199 - Ring Stick Up Cam- $29 - Wireless Chime- $29 - Stick Up Cam - SOLAR CHARGING PANEL (Pre-order)- Problem 1 (Original Video Doorbell):- Started when we received the Video Doorbell, motion didn't seem to work consistently. No matter what the settings were, most sensitive (approximately 30 feet according to app) and least sensitive (approximately 5 feet according to app), standing in front of the door waving my hand in front off the camera and you could flip a could to guess whether or not it would work. I called support and we went through updating the firmware, resetting and same result.- Support said they would ship out a replacement. Something that should be noted here, when I received the replacement doorbell, it came in a PRE-PRINTED BOX INDICATING, HERE IS YOUR REPLACEMENT. PLEASE PLACE YOUR OLD UNIT IN THE BOX AND RETURN.- If you think about this for a second, how many companies have RETURN CUSTOM PRINTED PACKAGING with return instructions on the box. This box can only be used for returns of defective product which would make one believe, there most be a LOT OF RETURNS if the had this printed up.- Sensitivity set to approximately 5 ft (least sensitive) you can set it to, cars, buses, garbage truck all set off an alert even though they are 35-40 feet from the Video Doorbell.- Problem 2 (Stick Up Cam):- Went through the setup and mounted the camera, NOT MORE than 10 feet from my Asus AS1900 Wireless Router in my office but approximately 20 feet off the ground and had similar issues; inconsistent motion triggers and activations.- Got on the phone and support said to reposition so it wasn't "so far off the ground"....HUH!!!??? Why would this matter? Did it anyway.- Moved the cam directly over the back door and yes it now picks up motion but also picks up motion WHEN THERE IS NONE!!!- Condensation on the lens the first morning after install made video completely unviewable and it seemed as the day warmed and the condensation dissipated, that would trigger the recording and of course an alert on my phone.- ALMOST FORGOT about the VOICE interaction!!! HAHAHAHA!!! My cellphone has a louder speaker and there isn't anyway to change a setting.- So now if the motion does work and does send you a notification that there is someone in your backyard, well you might want to ask them what they are doing....well, they WILL NOT HEAR YOU!Problem 3 (Replacement Video Doorbell):- Same exact issue as with the first cam.For anyone reading this thinking it must be my Wifi, NOPE!! I positioned a repeater not 8 feet from the doorbell on the inside wall. My signal strength is great but this device just isn't doing what it says it will.- My Swann cameras picked up my mailman but the Ring Doorbell DID NOT????- Audio communication while it seems better than the Stick Up Cam, it is of course where you would have a doorbell and the person ringing the bell will only be maybe 4-10 feet away. With the doorbell it might be better but of course you have to get a notification before UPS/FedEx or the mailman leave with your package.This is a GREAT IDEA but the execution is REALLY POOR! This just isn't ready for Primetime!! I was willing to forgive the FIRST Video Doorbell having issues but when the second exhibited exactly the same issues.Don't say I didn't tell you so!

title: Save your money and the headache
body: [[VIDEOID:837af69a3c79d611f710bd8fb59a9e70]] Debated a very long time on what type to go with and ultimately chose Ring over Nest BUT only because we are Alexa house. Started with the Ring Alarm panel and door sensors. Worked great and bought the Ring door bell...works terrible.During my 30 day free trial here are my challenges:1. ANSWERING THE RING ALERT: My locked iPhone detects the doorbell, I select the alert, my phone prompts me for my password and the Ring app opens automatically and I answer HOWEVER when the same occurs on my hub's Samsung, he unlocks his phone, the app DOES NOT open automatically, he gets to the app, opens it and by the time he's done all that the he's missed the person.2. MISSING THE RING ALERT: a. During the trial period or with a paid Ring subscription: No problem, you just playback the clip of the Ring and see who it was. b. AFTER the trial period, if you don't have a paid subscription: You can no longer look at the clip...(you have to buy their subscription) c. What you can do after trial end IF you miss the doorbell and you didn't pay for the subscription you CAN look at the camera's live feed...IF they are still standing there.3. MOTION DETECTING SENSOR: a. During trial or with paid subscription: I got messages alerting me "Motion detected at your front door" open the app, play the clip and only hear the door closing and missed the person coming in or out (yes I tried all the recommended senor adjustments with no ease or luck) b. After trial with no subscription, you get the alerts and again cannot access any clips (money money money)4. PRICE AND FEATURES:For what it costs, it has too many challenges, has expensive extras that you most likely need (attachment that allows you to hear the ring in the house without having to carry your phone, extenders to boost signal), and the fact that it loses most of the features you want in the video doorbell without a paid subscription... It is NOT worth it.Becuase I don't like to complain without offering solutions, here is what worked for me...What did I buy INSTEAD OF RING?:Frustrated and still in need of a temp solution I bought a Wyze pan camera for 36ish bucks and a mini SD card based on reviews (and it's Alexa compatible) to try it and I LOVE it. Now it's NOT a doorbell but heck I don't answer my doorbell anyways (my giant mastiff provides free alerts to the entire neighborhood when some tries to get close to the front of the house and he looks mean, see attached video) I just want to replay the coming and going activities or watch live feed if I see fit so this Wyze device is perfect!!! It detects motion and/or sound and sends alerts, it motion tracks, motion tags, pan tracks, offers continuous recording that can play back days of time in addition to sending you motion clips based on motion or person detection settings, you can watch the live feed with sound (what you hear is based on distance) AND it has two way selections to listen and talk through it decently, you can create shortcuts to do quick activities (such as one click to pause notifications per your schedule exp: I stayed home sick and I have it set to normally send alerts during work hours so I press my pause shortcut so my hub's doesn't get alerts while I move around the house AND without having to temporarily adust my weekday settings), you can add multiple cameras easily, play back entire periods YOU select in time lapse speed, you can take pics of what you are seeing, take videos live or from the playback (use to black mail your kids when you catch them singing or picking a nose), the app is easy to use, you can buy inexpensive accessories to mount it on an outlet or mount it to a wall, even hang it upside down bcz you can flip the view, and the best.... NO PAID SUBSCRIPTION required.Long story long, Ring was underwhelming and expensive. Save your dollars. Hope this helped.

title: Defective product & Bad Customer service
body: I'm very disappointed with customer service at Ring.com.The product feels solid but mine is having a problem. Note to everyone else out there who bought this or looking to buy it; if you have a problem with your hardware after a few months of using it then you are in the long run to try very hard to get Ring.com to replace the unit.I bought this in April of this year, did not use or install it for around 5 months because I sold my house and then after moving to the new house I installed it a few weeks ago. It worked fine until yesterday.I was getting notification about motion at the door but wasn't able to connect to see the video feed nor was the history saving to the cloud. I called customer service while I was at work thinking they can help remotely but it was a waste of time.So I decided I'll call them again at night when I get home. But before I called them, I did a reboot/reset (yes reset) of my home wireless router and modem in case it was a network issue. That's the first thing we (myself) IT guys tell people to do...My problem is the doorbell pics up motion but when I connect to it the live feed only last for 10-20 seconds before it freezes. And it's pretty darn consistent that I'm able to replicate that problem. Live view would not connect. Anyways In short I have a hardware problem and this is how I know.In addition to doing the above, I also did a orange button reset by holding it down for 30 seconds, removed it from my account and re-added it back.I did all that before calling customer service. While I was on the phone with the customer service agent last night, she had me take the doorbell and put it on top of my router to do a motion test and ring test; she saw the problem that I described above and then she told me to take the door bell out of the house and do a test there. Basically she was trying to isolate the problem to blame my network. If the problem was with my network/connection strength then doing a close proximity test would not show any problem but it show the problem so I didn't understand why I asked to do the same test outside of the out. Anyways that's not the point....still a problem with the hardware to me.I told her I did a test with my phone hotspot since you suspect it might be my home internet connection although all my smart devices are working just fine except the ring but that was ignored.My problem was not solved last night but instead she told me that she will escalate to level 2 support and I would have to wait 24-48 hours for a reply. I'm standing their thinking are you serious. It's going to take that long for level 2 to reply to me when a device like this is my first line of defense and contact outside my house. So right now my house has no doorbell or outside camera. I guess no priority is really put into getting customers up and going.Today I decided to take the camera to work and connect it to out unrestricted IT wireless to see if the same problem persist and low and behold it does the same thing. I purchased another Ring for my work so I compared the two on the same network but the work one is working just fine however mine doesn't.I called Ring support again but as expect was given the same answer "it's with level 2, just wait for them to get back to you"...I'm not impressed with the product and most of all the support. They don't put customer first. If you have a problem they will try to blame your network or user negligence. That's not the way to do business.I purchase a Ring doorbell for my company recently but I am going to return that due to my experience and since I'm the IT guy at my company, I don't want to have to deal with this again.All i'm asking for is a replacement to be sent out to me ASAP and don't have to wait 24-48 hours for a reply. That's ridiculous considering it's a security device as well.

title: Motion detection is complete garbage
body: I bought the original Ring device a little over a month ago and I both love and hate it so very much. The motion detection is so terrible to the point it's unusable - it detects cars 30ft+ away but won't detect people till they are 3ft away. It doesn't matter what sensitivity or motion detection settings I choose. I have contacted support and they've been like "let me fix the settings for you"...and so far it's just made things worse. I read all the tutorials about the motion detection and since it's heat based then I thought it might be related to the cold weather we were having in the area making it very sensitive to the warm cars. But it's been about 80 degrees out the past couple weeks and the thing is picking up dozens of cars every day but it's missed 2 different package deliveries. And speaking of which, do you know how frustrating it is that to make settings changes to even attempt calibrating this garbage motion detection it requires me to press the doorbell button physically for any changes to be picked up? Really?? They didn't think it would be a good idea to have the doorbell periodically connect to the servers and download settings changes or even to check for updates? Also, there's no way for me to see what's going on at my door even when I know the device is alive and on my wi-fi sending data to the servers (I know because I monitor these things very carefully on my router) and yet I still can't connect to it unless I "answer" the notification. And as I mentioned earlier the motion detection is pure garbage, so I had to turn that notification off, so the only time I can actually view what's going on at my door is when the button is pushed, and you miss the notification even a second too late then you're stuck waiting around for the video to upload to the server before you ever know who was at your door. But seriously why does it have to disconnect when it's idle, even though it's hardwired - that doesn't make any sense! I could understand when it's running on battery you might want it to operate this way, but when it's connected to power it should operate differently. You have enough power to keep the LED ring around the button lit 24/7, so clearly you had power to spare.Of course now I come to find out the reason none of these things have been addressed on the Ring is because Ring Pro was in development...which appears to fix all these problems, is better in every way, and it's smaller! That would have been good to know 2 months ago - I would have waited for the Ring Pro device because the old Ring was so huge I couldn't put it on my doorjam where my old doorbell was located, so I had to mount it to the side of my house. Which is fibercement siding, so I had to use concrete anchors and now you're telling me I could have have just waited a couple months to buy the new one and that wouldn't have been a problem. And to replace it with this new one means I'm going to have to patch all that up because it's smaller and won't cover up the holes from the old.The timing of the release of Ring Pro certainly explains why so many of the issues with the original Ring were never addressed - all along the plan was to make a new device. That's fine, I understand how these things work, and sometimes you need to improve the hardware to resolve the issues. But you need to be upfront about your limitations and what your plan is to fix them, not pretend like nothing is wrong and there's nothing in the works. I explained to Ring support all the issues I'm having with motion sensitivity and they've yet to offer a solution that actually fixes the issue - I kind of get the feeling that they don't have a solution.It really it sad that I have to write this review. The truth is I love the idea of Ring and when it works it's fantastic (also I love the integration with Wink!). But the shortcomings are just too much for me to give this a good rating.

title: Bell works fine but motion and latency of cloud are slow
body: the main function of answering door bell and talking to visitor through my devices worked fine. I did not like motion sensors and UX to retrieve video from cloud. The motion sense setting is extremely difficult and the speed of activation is very slow. Since it detects heat and movement together, it is too sensitive to cars to pass by. When I set the motion range less than 15 feet, it activated when cars passed by more than 50 feet every single time. The response time of motion detector is very slow. Most of cases, I can see only back of UPS drivers who was leaving products already in my door way.The latency to retrieve video from cloud is annoyingly slow. When you combined with unnecessary motion recording and slow latency in cloud interaction becomes very problematic. There is function to edit or delete bulky recording, it becomes almost full time task to clean up recording.I contacted the founder of the company and this is the response I got. I thought he would be more apologetic - Photo attached.Jamie Siminoff, Chief Inventor and Founder1523 26th StSanta Monica, CA 90404j@ring.com917.412.4747Jamie Siminoff, Chief Inventor and Founder1523 26th StSanta Monica, CA 90404j@ring.com917.412.4747As seen on:Fortune Magazine- Meet Ring, the connected doorbell company that just scored Richard Branson as an investorTime Magazine's Top 10 GadgetsThe Truth About Daytime BurglariesTechCrunch Product ReviewABC's Shark Tank (password: doorbot)On Tue, May 24, 2016 at 6:53 AM, Dong Kim <coop2008@outlook.com> wrote:Hi Jamie,I am reaching out to tell you why I am giving up on a Ring I purchased less than an year ago. The product has stopped working and I got no where with customer support. So far the customer support has been really not thinking my perspective. I was going to forget about the product and move on and the last email really made me unhappy. If you read the sting, when I contacted first time, the suggestion was to reset (pushing the orange button) and remove device from the app and add it back. It failed in the stage to connect to my wifi multiple times. Since then, customer support continue to suggest a variations of the same solution. Now, the person is asking me if I removed the device? Is Eddie following even what happened of history? Do you think if his customer support is adequate?I had bad experience with your product and the low satisfaction with customer support is the last step to keep me away from your product forever. To list issues with the product;• Generally slow latency – either motion or ring react slow. By the time when I reacted to it, all gone.• Motion detector is so slow – I always see the back of people’s head than when they approach• Motion detector is hard to set – it picks up all cars passing by from far far away but it takes so long to pick up people approaching really close.• As a result, I have unusable video’s in the cloud and the cloud is so slow, it becomes a full time job to clean up all videos. There is no function to bulk edit to delete at once. Then I need to go each video one by one – play -waiting– click delete –waiting- are you sure you want delete – yes – waiting. Think if you have 20-30 of passing by car video and you go through this step 20-30 times a day. It is not a good experience.This product did not turn out to be what I thought it would be.Thank you,DongUpdate after about 10 month of usage: The unit stopped to work with wifi and could not reconnect. It became $200 door bell just ring a bell.

title: This model of Ring just isn’t ready for prime time.
body: Update: This doorbell worked perfectly until I cancelled my monthly subscription. Now it constantly fails to pull up live video.This doorbell is a piece of garbage, just like Jeff Bezos.I’ve now evicted all my Alexa devices from my home, cancelled Prime, and I’ll be replacing the Ring with a HomeKit Netatmo and Ring Alarm with Abode. I can’t wait to NEVER do business with Amazon again! This version of the doorbell is a great idea, but the execution falls a bit short.I’ve lived with this doorbell for almost 3 years, and here are my thoughts about ownership.If using in battery mode, you only get about half the advertised time. So, you’ll be charging the Ring 2-4 times a year, depending on how often you get visitors.Removing the Ring 2-4 times a year caused one of the safety screws to fail. It just spins in place and no longer backs out. I had to use a tool and bend the screw over to get the Ring to fit back on its bracket properly.The camera has a serious delay when it detects motion. For example, when I get my Amazon deliveries, by the time the camera starts recording, all I get is the back of the delivery person as they walk away from my door. It’s so slow, a criminal could easily run into your yard and place a piece of tape over the camera before it could record them.I fails to activate 10% of the time. People can literally walk up to the door and walk away without the camera recording anything. I’ve found Amazon deliveries sitting in front of the Ring and it didn’t register or record that event. So, at least 10% of the time, a criminal could walk right up to your front door undetected.Speaking of failing to activate, when pulling up live view, it fails to connect to the Ring at least 20% of the time. All you get is a black screen with the answer/hang up buttons. No image. When someone rings the doorbell or a motion triggers a notification, it only fails to connect maybe 1% of the time. Obviously, the Ring has a lot of bugs.I get a ton of false alarms. As a matter of fact, 90% of all notifications I get from Ring are false notifications. By false, I mean when I check the video, there’s nothing going on in my front yard. Nothing.There’s a $3 monthly fee to see any of your recordings. There’s no option to keep videos directly on your device. If you don’t pay the $3 a month, you’re limited to live view and notifications. However, at least ⅓ the time, the notification are delayed long enough to hit the paywall when trying to see what triggered the camera. This is EXTREMELY ANNOYING. No matter how fast you act upon a notification, you sometimes hit a paywall when trying to see if your home is being violated...makes me kind of hate this company.I will say that their customer service is great. They send out replament parts, no questions asked and they’ll work with to, as long as it takes, to try and resolve an issue: it’s just to bad this version of Ring is just a lemon and the fact you’re forced to pay $3 to see any of your recordings is just plain annoying.Hey Ring, how about this, let the app keep the 3 most recent videos on-device. If someone wants to see older videos or keep all their videos, then they can pay the $3. Also, let videos be downloaded to the camera roll.Lack of HomeKit support and the mandatory $3 thing is what’s keeping me from upgrading to a newer model of Ring. It’s also unclear if the newer models have fixed the camera lag (time between something triggering the sensor and the camera recording). Because, really, until that’s fixed, there’s no point in upgrading.

title: This product is pure crap. It's not out of alpha development
body: This product is pure crap. It's not out of alpha development, but it was released and sold as a finished product. There are so many things wrong with it, it's hard to know where to start.I guess the first thing is that without paying for the cloud account most of the functionality doesn't work, but they don't tell you this up front.Next, the video quality is abysmal at times. Sometimes it's good or OK, but other times you can't even make out anything in the blurred video. Sometimes all you get is a black screen with audio. Sometimes no audio either.Sensitivity: They completely failed at the detection algorithm. I have 3 of these devices. The one facing the street, which is about 35' away, will start recording when someone, or a car, or every bus or truck goes by. At the same time I've never seen a mailman, or delivery person come up to the door. I've been running that one for over 20 days. It misses entirely the most important events, and gets every insignificant one, such as me taking out the trash. I don't know how they managed to fail so badly in the motion detection algorithm.The 2nd one I put up at the back door, doesn't face a street, but a deck with trees in the background. It misses the first several, and most important seconds of every recording, but it's amazing at detecting me at 180 degrees, if I look out the door and my nose is too far forward. The first several seconds after it starts recording, may record a video, then it's a stop-screen image for the entire duration of the recording. Entirely worthless. Typically 40 seconds or more of an image is a still image from 40 seconds ago.Battery life. The 6-12 months the battery is supposed to last is comical. The same back door one above, which had been charged to 100%, prior to installation, ran for 10 days before it was completely discharged. They really should provide a transformer, power plug, with extra wiring so you could snake a power supply to the device if you wanted to. It only accepts an AC output in a power supply and they don't even support that, except if you connect it to your existing wired door bell system. This is pathetic!It misses the first few seconds of what it should be recording! So, if you are lucky, you see that someone entered your place, but not who. You might manage to see the opened door, just prior to it closing. It's bizarre, almost like it a joke and taunting you.The next part isn't the devices fault, but rather something that never occurred to me. We currently have five people living here. Between 30 and 60 times a day, we walk in and/or out of the house, and do something, like take out recycling, or trash, or bring a dog outside, etc. After missing the first few seconds of each of these transactions, I get notified up to 60 times a day, with a video of the back of my head, etc. I might not have even tried this if I realized the massive pain in the butt it would be, which leads me to the interfaces.No time or date stamp on the videos. No preview of the first image. No deletion from within a video playback, except on a smartphone. Do deletion of a video from the list view on the smartphone. The control interfaces crash or fail to update correctly. This requires that you close them and then re-open them. This is buggy, early beta software.If this is the best of these types of things on the market, which it appears to be, you should wait a few years, until they work out the BS, that we shouldn't be exposed to.Don't get this product.

title: Update: Not recommended product. Major improvement needed.
body: Update: After a year or so, I have decided to remove a star due to it not working most of the time. It takes too long to connect and person leaves. Also, they have lackluster customer service. I was signed up for their cloud recording subscription and my card number expired so I had to update it. It was basically impossible to do myself via the website. For a technology company their customer service reminds me more of the government, large and ineffective. They never offered any resolution to my problem via chat, don't bother, and after 3 tries to contact them.Basically, has its benefits and also has drawbacks. It seems like the most thought-out web capable doorbell cam/intercom but there are things which could be improved upon. I did a substantial amount of research prior to buying and I decided on the Ring Doorbell for its unintrusive minimalistic design, the web storage feature, and the fact it can use existing doorbell power not batteries.Install (the good):Installation was pretty straight forward and I did appreciate that the doorbell came with everything I needed to install it (minus drill): bracket, screws/anchors, level, drill bit. The installation video did mention some silicone or puddy to plug the hole which I did not get, but maybe that is just a recent omission on the part of manufacturer.Install (the bad): It would have been good to have some grommets or washers to help angle the doorbell to desired position. Not everyone will have a perfectly flat or ideal location for the right camera angle. I doubt that the bracket will prevent someone stealing the doorbell but at least you have a nice portrait of them if they do. Just seems for the price it should be a little more durable feeling.Operation and features (the good and bad): The doorbell does have the most features in comparison. As mentioned there are things which could use attention. The first is the finish of the doorbell itself. We wish the top matched the finish of the painted part. It would improve the look a lot. It would be nice if the doorbell and bracket was metal or at least wrapped in metal. I think most people would appreciate it. Especially the market the manufacturer has priced their product for. It will also engender interest for their next offering. When one pushes the doorbell button, We get prompt rings on our devices. We like you can have multiple users. Our existing chime does ring like it used to, we like that. I do wish you vould program a custom ring on both ends though. I sm nit a fan of the attempt at branding the ring. The doorbell does have a slight few seconds pause before it can be pushed again so that is a plus for. anyone who has people ring the bell inscessantly. The camera has a wide angle giving a fish bowl effect but it has pretty good visibility angles. The resolution could be improved. Maybe a glare shade would help cut down on glare. The mic is sufficient unless there is wind or loud noises going on. There is no volume really on the doorbell which would have been nice. I dont mind the fee for the recording feature but I am hoping there will be some better offerings in the market in the future. Intgratin into a smart hone scenario interests me and I will update to review its abilities in that effort, if any.For lack of a better option I will probably keep it unless ithere is another better option available. Also now that I have drilled four holes into my entryway I don't want to start over again. I do bet soon there will be an improved model or better offering elsewhere.

title: After 4 years I give up. Ring Doorbell just does not work as a security device.
body: I was very hopeful and it seemed like a good idea, but this doorbell (Gen 1) just has not performed from the day it was installed. I have the Ring hardwired and connected to a brand new interior doorbell that was installed at the same time by an electrician. Unless you press the Ring button firmly the light will spin but the contact does not engage the interior doorbell. My available wireless is also at the low end of the bandwidth for the Ring so by the time my phone or tablet alerts me it can be more than a minute. In many situations it is the motion sensor that is activated and alerts me. This doorbell including motion detection is so slow that I seldom know that a package has been delivered until the couriers or USPS are in their vehicles and driving down the road. Many a day I have found a package has been delivered but the delivery person has been crouched as they place the package and then if they are moving fast even the motion detection may not be activated as they leave and I get no record of the delivery (I use I a wide band of coverage and high sensitivity). The only person who gets regularly captured on the Ring is me as I enter and leave the door, so I know the motion detector does work. Delivery people are moving fast and they seem to be able to evade the motion detector which gives me little hope that a thief would also be recorded. I have had this Ring jam up about every 6 months, particularly when there is an update. This is a real pain as you have to unscrew it from the wall to reset it and if you are on vacation the Ring becomes useless. I have contacted customer service multiple times they tell me that I have to upgrade my internet DSL service or router, move the Ring closer to the access point or buy the newer Ring. Upgrading the DSL will double my monthly internet access so I opted to add an internet access point extender to the front porch (did not help), upgraded my router (helped a bit) and added a second access point (helped a bit more). Rather than spend a couple of hundred dollars to upgrade the Ring, I spent $25 on a Wyze security camera from Amazon. What a difference. Every time someone gets near the front door I get a quick motion notification that someone is at the door. Now I get all sorts of notifications of someone or an animal (usually deer) near the front and this motion is often not reflected by the Ring. The Wyze does not charge a monthly fee to access the recordings and they have software that allows more control that what is on the Ring including live view. The community security reports on Ring are also useless as I live on an Island and the zone of coverage excludes part of the island but includes part of the mainland which has the majority of the criminal activity. I've been gone for 3 weeks and it is clear to me in running the Ring in parallel with the Wyze camera, that the Ring is just not a good security device in my situation. While I was gone, my Wyze camera repeatedly recorded up to 5 times a night that the Ring light was coming on erratically but the Ring was not recording anything - the Wyze camera was clearly much more sensitive at recording any motion. I am going to drop my Ring subscription and rely on the Wyze camera for security recordings. The fees I was formerly paying to Ring will be used to buy additional Wyze cameras. I would not recommend that anyone buy a Ring doorbell unless they are already paying for very fast internet service, and even then, an inexpensive camera like the Wyze may be a better option to monitor your front and other doors.

title: great product - for about 4 months.  Update: POS now.  Update: not happy yet.
body: 6th update - a friend from put of town came. He had an iPhone. He was able to set it up immediately. I gave it to him. Guess the ring doesn't work with Samsung Galaxy J7 phones, maybe others too. Don't waste your time trying to get it to work. It either will or it won't and if it won't, send it back.5th update - bought another one. Not only did it not work at my house but it didn't work at my son's house. When it didn't work here I gave it to him. The Ring people had convinced me that it was my wifi. We now think it may be our phones.We both have Galaxy J4 Star. Before that J4. 4th update - still doesn't work right and it's 2 years later. Best I can do is check the app ever so often to see if there's any video. Still does not ring my phone or alert me to motion at the door although it will video it. We think it burned out the house doorbell (it was hardwired to the ring).Bought a motion detector. Between that and the video I have a "system".3rd update - I give up. It's never going to ring on my phone. I've spent over three hours on the phone and still cannot get notified of someone ranging my doorbell. Great picture when they do but have to check archives frequently. Motion alert too delayed to see who stole the package. New problem - as I type this review the Ring turns on and sends a picture.. No sound and no one pressed the button. Too many problems with this doorbell to deal with. It's not ready for prime time. Great concept though.2nd update: Finally able to talk to Ring. I saw on the news where the Amazon cloud was down the day I had tried to reach them so tried again. This time I connected with Ring very easily, After almost two hours my problem was partially resolved. Had to bump me up but the new guy was able to tweak my router and get the connection to my phone. Only problem now is it won't notify me when the doorbell is rung. We set up my tablet and laptop too and although they all get the picture none get the sound, yet, when the technician added his phone as an additional user his phone would ring when I rang the Ring doorbell. I tried it using my wifi and using the tower. So now I have a camera. If I'm home and in the house, I hear the house doorbell since I am hardwired. If away from home - just luck if I see the call on my phone when it happens otherwise, nice camera. Ring's customer service was awesome. Spent almost three hours with me trying to resolve this issue. Unfortunately it's not totally resolved. Great when it works.Update: I've had the Ring over six months. Worked great at first but I was never able to get a live feed. Then it started to ring when no one at door. Sometimes when someone is at door I would get a black screen. Finally it quit ringing when someone was at door. Now it will not connect at all. Basically it is worthless. Tried to call customer service. First they answered but no one picked up when call was transferred. Now I get a message that there was a "application error". Finding the customer service number was a chore to begin with. Something fishy going on.NOTE: THIS IS WHEN BOUGHT MY FIRST SAMSUNG GALAXY J4. NOW THINK IT JUST DOESNT WORK WITH THAT MODEL PHONE.It deliver as promised. My entry way is obstructed so I don't get a picture until the doorbell is rung. Usually the delivery person has walked away before it starts filmimg. However that's my problem and not Rings fault. Otherwise, I'm very pleased with the device.

title: Great idea, terrible execution!
body: If I buy an advanced doorbell, the first thing I want it to be is a doorbell, unfortunately that is the category the Ring Video doorbell fails in epic fashion.I purchased a two pack of Ring Video Doorbells during an Woot sale (an Amazon owned business) for $99.The Ring Doorbell is easy to install, has decent 720p picture quality, okay (but over exposed) night vision, and is aesthetically pleasing. What's the problem? It doesn't always ring on the smartphone app when the doorbell button is pressed! Since this is the only way to hear the doorbell from inside the home, when the app does not ring I will have delivery packages left on my steps without my knowledge and friends visiting I won't know about. The first time it happened I dismissed it as a fluke. Then less than a week later, it happened with both doorbells only minutes apart.As a part-time delivery driver I always assumed that when I delivered a package to a house with a Ring Doorbell, that if I pressed the Ring button and the doorbell sounded that the doorbell was operating and notifying the resident(s). This is not always the case (at least with my two Ring Doorbells) which is the reason I'm sending them both back and getting a simple wireless doorbell that uses radio frequency transmission to ring an indoor receiver (i.e. the dozens of cheap wireless doorbells sold on Amazon that don't require a smartphone).Here is a copy of the email I sent to Woot tech support to which they did not argue or ask a single question, but simply gave me an RMA and told me to send the Ring Doorbells back for a full refund (so obviously this is not a rare situation):"I am having a problem with both of my Ring doorbell cameras from Order #xxxxxxxx. They are both very unreliable. I have very stable and fast Xfinity/Comcast Internet service with multiple WiFi zones. One Ring doorbell camera is connected to one zone and one connected to another zone. Both cameras are connected to two phones, one iPhone and one Android. When the Ring doorbell button is pressed they only sometimes ring on the phones, about 75% or less of the time. Just today I pressed the front door Ring and it did not ring on either phone, neither did it record anything! I proceeded to try the backdoor door Ring and got the same results. When I check the device health, for the front door Ring I have an RSSI of 47 and a battery level of 78% and for the back door Ring an RSSI of 45 and a battery level of 87%. Firmware says "Up to Date" for both Ring devices. I am highly disappointed. If a doorbell can't do it's basic function of ringing when the button is pressed I want nothing to do with either of these devices. I want to return both devices for a full refund."In addition to this major problem, the Ring doorbell is also does not mount securely. The Ring doorbell mounting plate comes with four standard Phillips screws. But to get to the mounting plate one has to first remove the Ring doorbell from the mount which is secured with two anti-theft type screws. The only problem is the anti-theft screws don't countersink into the surface of the doorbell, but rather protrude out from the bottom making it extremely easy to remove the screws with standard pliers. Another epic fail for a security device!How the world's largest retailer can not only sell, but actually be owner of such a faulty device is beyond me. Jeff Bezos, please fix this product line or do away with it.

title: DON'T WASTE YOUR MONEY!
body: This this is the world's worst. Firstly, it advertises to be "wireless" then when you call to complain, they say, well, you are using it wireless, it would work much better if you hard-wired it. WTF! My friend had it hard-wired with the exact same issues, so that's not the problem. ABSOLUTELY NO MOTION , EXCEPT CARS PASSING DOWN THE STREET. ZERO - NADA - ZIP. Forget it, they should remove the Motion aspect/selling point cause it does not work. Now the doorbell - here's how wonderful that works. You come to my door, you push my doorbell, after about a 5-10 second delay my phone and wall chime begin to ring. About 15-20 seconds from that point the app opens up for the video to display, which then takes about another 15 seconds to focus, because first you see nothing but black, which then becomes just a big white blob on the screen, then as it focuses and tries to come into view, you see the HEAVY pixelation of what will one day become a video if you're patient enough, but you surely can't tell who/what might be going on, and then finally, after total frustration has set in, there it is - the picture of whoever WAS at your door walking away and is already all the way down the walkway which runs up to your house and either entering their car or continuing down the street on the sidewalk. You could try SCREAMING so the speaker might be heard by them, but heck, they'd probably hear you because you're in your backyard by the pool screaming outside and it has nothing to do with the fact there's a speaker on there. I even emailed back and forth with the owner who actually told me my motion problem was due to placing the bell too high on the wall - it couldn't be any higher than 48", so I went out, removed it, drilled another 4 holes in my exterior wall (which is now beginning to look like a dart board because initially I put it on the door, and all I got in my view was a picture of the sides of the insets on both sides of the door - so don't make that mistake if you dare to say, oh, others say this is a good product so I'm getting it anyways - that's what I did.) Anyways, after going thru other B.S. adjustments that totally failed, the owner asked me to send a picture - I did - and he said, "I see your problem - you've mounted it too high. Move it to no higher than 48" and you'll see a BIG difference" - so I did - and IT DID NOTHING - STILL NO MOTION - NO IMPROVEMENT AT ALL. Oh, and it was at this point that the "owner - Jaime" of this company stopped replying to my emails for help. No offer of replacement, no other ideas. Just jacked around with emails for a few days resulting in more damage to my exterior walls, etc. Trust me, for this amount of money, forget it! It is useless as anything more than a doorbell and even that is delayed and who wants it for anything else because video's so slow to load you'll NEVER catch most people at the door, but you'll get a good shot of the back side as they're walking away. And trust me, it couldn't have been any closer to my router which is setup just inside the same wall the thing is mounted on and the router gets top notch reception. For the record, my neighbor who had hers hard-wired finally had enough and returned it. She thought she'd try a Zmodo, which is also on the top of the line lists you see everywhere online. That one isn't doing any better for her. Great idea, IF IT WORKED! My return has been processed and is on the way back.

title: Low functionality, more gimmick than good.
body: Motion detection zones are a joke even after 2 years to improve, no changes at all. They would rather sell you an angled backplate to tilt it down rather than address the software problem. The software is soo lazy they don't even overlay the zone setting on the camera view (likely because it doesn't even work the way they say it does).The device relies on your network to function, yet any video capture or saving is paywalled as a cloud service. On top of this many streams lack video or audio, and a single motion event can set off multiple consecutive alerts and wont allow you to view the stream (which should work fine because these events get saved to the cloud with the same success rate as normal).Overall performance is sub-par, the delay between getting a notification and opening the video stream means you NEVER see someone at your door live unless they stay and wait outside. With about a 20 second delay a mailman or room mate is already off screen leaving you with a live view of nothing... But you know someone was there because the motion alert went off, right? Nope! After trying every possible motion zone setting the best results I've ever gotten still include a vast majority of traffic (camera mounted to view across driveways, not even pointed directly at the street, car traffic occupies 30% of the screen at most).About 9 of every 10 videos saved to the cloud are just cars. Even this would't be soo bad if I just had a lot of spam alerts to go through to find real events, but I have been in my kitchen as mailmen and other foot traffic passes and verified the ring doesn't pick it up. I've had company come and go that doesn't get picked up, and I've had people go through my car during the night in full view of the camera with no video saved overnight.Summary:Pro:-Battery powered wifi doorbell (can replace a broken conventional wired doorbell rather well)-Ability to see in dark rather good once the stream is already open (minus points for almost no ability to self activate in the dark)Cons:-Motion zones do not effectively determine when the video is captured-Motion 'distance' seemed to be tied to audio level, not motion (loud truck in an ignored zone still gets recorded, quiet mailman goes unnoticed on the porch)-ZERO functionality for local storage (Maybe a pro if you love subscriptions your don't need)-Poor connectivity (not talking wifi here. Very often a stream will fail to load on my phone, but a minute or two later I can view the video on the cloud. Also atleast 10% of all streams are just black regardless of whether I attempt to view it live, and if ever I do connect live and see a black feed, that's also what got saved to the cloud.)-If you contact support the most they'll offer you is a tilted backplate or a wifi repeater in a copy paste format devoid of any real attempt to address the actual issues.Conclusion: Gimmicky product designed to make amazon look like a quality choice for automation and bait you into subscribing to the most basic features you can imagine. Poor implementation, lack of meaningful improvements, and latency issues soo bad that calling it a "live" stream is almost false advertisement.A string outside tied to a bell inside also looks like a 'smart' product, after buying this and paying for the cloud service.

title: Save your money, buy a better engineered video dorbell.
body: I purchased a ring doorbell in December of 2015. I was told that if I could use the doorbell as a standalone doorbell, using the battery, or hardwired and I wouldn't have to worry about the battery. Their FAQ currently says "Ring Doorbell can operate wirelessly using its built-in battery. You also have the option of connecting your Ring Doorbell to your home’s existing doorbell wiring". In their FAQ another one of the questions is, do I need to charge my ring before hand to which they say, "No, most Ring Doorbells arrive with a battery charge ranging from 50%-70%". These two statements would lead the average person to believe that if you hardwired your ring to your existing wiring the system will charge and work fro your wiring.Fast forward to may 2016, and my doorbell chime starts buzzing when the ring detects movement and starts recording video. I was told by tech support at ring that my chime must be bad. I purchased a new chime, one that was on their list of "approved" doorbell chimes. No change, I still have a buzzing sound from the doorbell chime when the video comes on. Now I am told my doorbell uses the battery and basically only trickle charges through the hardwired doorbell system in my house and the Ring doorbell system can only handle a 4-5 activations a day or it can't keep up with the charging requirements. I was told by the technical support people today that my battery was low and that is causing the buzzing in my door chime.One of the questions in their FAQ is "WILL I BE NOTIFIED WHEN THE BATTERY IN MY RING DOORBELL IS LOW? to which they answer "Yes - you'll receive email reminders when your battery runs low. The Ring app also features a low-battery warning, as well as a battery level indicator that you can check at any time". I have never received a low battery alarm email or notification and there is currently no way to check my battery status in the current updated Iphone app.Also I was just recently told by tech support the ring doorbell doesn't use the hardwired system for power it only uses the hardwired system to trickle charge it's internal battery. tech support says the doorbell runs off it's internal battery regardless if you have the doorbell hardwired or not. I live in a very rural area, I have dogs, I have kids that go in and out, I go in and out and they say that's too much movement. Can you imagine if I lived in a busy city with people walking buy at all hours of the day or night? My house sits 150 feet off of our road so and we live on a dead end. I was told that I should be able to see the battery status from the ring app. When I questioned this they said "oh sorry that feature was disabled in the new app." So now I am told I am going to have to pull my ring doorbell off the wall outside and bring it inside and charge it every month or so. Sounds like poor engineering. So when I brought the doorbell inside today to charge it, it was constantly saying motion detected and starts recording while it's on a usb charger in the house. One would think that if the system was plugged into the usb port it would disable the sensors and video so the system would only charge. Save your money and buy one of the other systems that you don't have to dismantle and bring inside to charge.

title: and they go bad within a week or 2
body: I agree with the review by Munchiniwa... I am surprised she didn't give them 1 star but I will. DO NOT BUY THIS PRODUCT. I bought one for the front and for the back, and they go bad within a week or 2, some don't work right from the get go... I have exchanged 4 already and now I am getting ready to exchange 2 more and one of the new ones the night vision works when it feels like it FOR THE TOTAL OF 3 MORE!!!CUSTOMER SERVICE IS HORRIBLE: GOOD LUCK GETTING A REPRESENTATIVE ON LINE!! I END UP WAITING FOR AT LEAST HALF AN HOUR AND MOST OF THE TIME I GET "DISCONECTED" OR HUNG UP ON AFTER WAITING FOR HALF AN HOUR.... THEN THE HOLDING MUSIC IS REPEATED EVERY 60 SECONDS... OVER AND OVER AND OVER... I COMPLAINED ABOUT IT AND NOTHING HAS BEEN DONE.MY CLOUD: I BOUGHT 2 CLOUD SERVICES AND NOW BECAUSE OF ALL OF THE EXHANGES THIS COMPANY STOLED 1 FROM ME. I HAVE EMAILED MANY TIMES AND ALL I GET IS AN EMAIL THAT TELLS ME THEY RECEIVED MY EMAIL.... NOTHING ELSE IS DONE EVER AFTER THAT!! THEN THEY SEND ME EMAILS SO I CAN RATE THEIR SERVICE, AND WHEN I DO,,, THEY TELL ME THAT THE TICKET HAS EXPIRED!!I WILL BE BUYING THE NEST.COM SURVAILLANCE WHICH IS 24 HOURS FOR THE SAME PRICE AND ITS HD VIDEO PLUS MANY OTHER FEATURES!!! I ALREADY USE THEIR NEST THERMOSTAT FOR OVER A YEAR AND IT WORKS GREAT!! MY BROTHER USES THEM ALSO AND LOVES THEM!!!DON'T WASTE YOUR MONEY AND YOUR TIME.... ITS A FULL TIME JOB JUST TO HAVE THIS GARBAGE WORK!!SAVING THE ON CLOUD: ITS VERY DIFFICULT TO SAVE THIS ON MY OWN CLOUD WITHOUT HAVING TO TRY 5 TO TO 10 TIMES EVERY TIME.... SOOOOO TIME CONSUMING!!!COPY OF REVIEW FROM Munchiniwa:First off, the Ring is very cool. I really like it and many people that come to my front door (salespeople) know what it is immediately and don't bother me. There are four problems however, one very serious (in my opinion).1 - The motion function engages when people are walking away, not when they walk up. MANY of my videos are of people's backsides. I don't know if this is a software issue or what. Tried increasing the sensitivity to the point where I was grabbing cars driving down the street but still only got people leaving my front door. Weird.2 - This kind of goes along with #2. You cannot access the doorbell with security software like Blue Iris,et.al. Now, I get that they want to sell you their Cloud service and I'm ok with $30 / year to have an off-site backup. But if you could link the doorbell into your security system you would have an actual video of everything in your possession and you could access it from any computer in the world. That would be REALLY handy for police investigations and any legal hassles that may come your way.3 - If you do not answer the alert fast enough, you are locked out-- even though someone is standing at your front door. You cannot "turn on" the camera from your phone or tablet. If someone is there and you do not have an "ACCEPT" button on the screen, there is just no way to see or talk to them. This is obviously a software flaw. You should always be able to "wake" a camera remotely and talk to a person at your front door. Or maybe you just want to see the view outside your front door. You should be able to access the camera / microphone and enjoy your front yard.

title: Fails at its main basic function
body: The idea of the product is great but the execution is terrible. First, I would like to say that the advertising is misleading because on the commercials the video being shown covers the full screen of the mobile device. Mine looks like I'm looking through a peep hole. I'm guessing the lens is recessed too far back. The so called tech support told me that the image can be zoomed in once my Android device get updated sometime in the future but in your commercials the video is not zoom in. The image is simulated or is using a ring device without the protective cover over the lens. I shoot videos and photos for a living so I can give a somewhat educated guess about this issue. My next issue is that there is such a long delay when someone rings the doorbell to when my device notifies me of the guest. It’s so long that the ring device will timeout and I can't answer the call. This is very annoying and pretty much makes the device useless cause that's the main function of the device. Which is probably your server issues. I don't know why if the device is on the same network as the mobile device it needs to go through your sever but I guess that's what allows it to contact multiple devices and upload to your cloud service. Speaking of that cloud server, that’s my next issue. I would like to keep my own clips. I have a very sophisticated security system and cameras were I use blue iris surveillance software on my PC to monitor my property. This device offers no way to connect with my software or doesn’t come with any software to allow me to record or store to my own personal cloud. I get that you need the subscriptions to maintain and expand your service but 199.00 is plenty for this device that doesn’t have an Ethernet option or any IR lights, and it doesn’t allow live remote connections. The buyer should have the option of self-monitoring via software provided with the unit or integration into another program. Please don’t get me started on the motion detection. It is totally useless no matter what setting it’s on it still goes off if a car goes by (road is about 50’ from door) and still doesn’t go off every time someone comes to the door. Right now I’m really considering trying out the doorbird, but I’m holding off to see if some of these issues get fixed, especially the main basic function of ringing my mobile device the moment some rings the doorbell. I could probably live with the other issues. I certainly can’t recommend a product that cost $200.00 and it only works just some (not even most) of the time. Another small but irritating issue is with the mobile app for android. It would be nice if when the notification pops up on a lock screen and says someone at your door, you could have the answer and deny buttons interactive on the notification screen. I have plenty of apps that have interactive pop up notifications. That one is just a me issue since I know that everyone wouldn’t find that to be useful. I know that I probably wasted my time writing all of this because you already have my money and could care less, but the question asking me if I would recommend this product really struck a nerve.

title: Big promise, BIG disappointment.
body: I purchased this direct from Ring, along with the Stick up Camera, solar panel and remote chime.I had high hopes for this system. Today I requested an RMA number to return it all.First, our cell reception here is pretty bad, especially on the north side of the house where the front door is.
