To configure the doorbell, you need to load the Ring app on your phone, and use it to tie together your phone, WiFi, the doorbell and a phone connection. Our phones were 4 years old, so we bought new phones on another network with better signal in our area.So once the doorbell was up and running, I had to mount it. You can wire it to your existing doorbell wiring, or run it off its internal battery with a claimed 1 year battery charge. Because our doorbell is on the side wall of the porch, not facing the street, I screwed it directly to our door.Our porch is a long deep tunnel, facing north. The lack of light on the porch caused everything beyond it to be just a huge blinding white light. People on the porch looked like headless black stick figures with a blinding white light on their shoulders. Had Ring's techs adjust the brightness which was of little help.Nighttime use wasn't much better. Our two porch lights were in the picture, making it useless.Figured putting it further out would help with the light problems, despite now being at a right angle to the porch. So, with a great effort, I did major surgery on the front wall, from the inside, to raise the existing doorbell wiring high enough to keep from cutting heads off in the video. Connected it to our existing doorbell, which now rang non-stop when the Ring doorbell was pressed. Drove the dogs and us crazy. Had to finally unplug it. An old-fashioned solenoid drive mechanical doorbell might have worked better. Our digital doorbell plays different tunes. One at a time. There's even a small diode shipped by Ring to help with digital doorbell issues. No help here.The idea of a message and video when strangers approached sounded good. Til we realized it would also chime everything we went in and out. My phone was rapidly filling up with videos and notifications when we went to the car, the mailbox, etc.The sensitivity adjustments were a pain. Dial it back and nothing was picked up on. Adjust it out enough to catch visitors and we got notifications and videos for every passing vehicle. Today was trash day, so every passing truck tripped the doorbell, but was out of frame before the camera caught anything. Just a constant stream of wind-chime sounding alerts and a phone full of empty videos.Overall, a great premise that didn't deliver. And I never even installed the remote stick up camera. Pretty sure my new phone would have melted under all those pointless videos.So I'm returning mine. Perhaps your experience will be better. Just try mounting it in a temporary fashion til you see how it works. Command Strips, perhaps. My doorbell is back in the box, but I have 8 screw holes in my door and stucco to repair.

title: Do Not Buy.  POS
body: Do not buy.First let me say I have worked in tech for over 20 years and am very handy with construction and electronics.Installation: The holes on the devise are not deep enough to easily slide the device onto the hooks on the mount. It requires a pretty serious "tap" with a rubber mallet to get them to hold, otherwise, after tightening the screws on the bottom, the top slides out.Registration and connecting: Easy enough, but the app instructions say things like "please wait until the lights stop blinking" and the lights on the app are blinking...but they mean the lights on the DEVICE stop blinking. Yes, this seems intuitive, but every other command and direction was specific to the app interface or clearly said "on the device."Connecting and using the device: Completely unreliable. Motion works maybe 1 out of 4 times, and even when you do get an alert, the video only connects 25% of the time. Same with the doorbell. I called tech support and spoke to 3 different people, all very nice, but had no idea what they were talking about. One claimed it was ny internet, asked me to run a speed test, he said I needed 1 Mbps up and one down to make it work. When I did the test with my phone directly next to the ring device, I got 18 and 6, down and up. Then he said, try another test next to the wifi. I asked why? He said maybe I need a repeater. I told him I was using a wired access point and he said that was slower than a repeater. That was when I knew he had no idea what he was talking about. I should have said, all this happened after spending 30 minutes doing a hard reset on the device (which requires it to be removed from the wall) and then reading it on my app.The second guy tells me it's my notifications, has me turn them all off and then all on (at this time I had my phone and my wife's phone (identical models connected to the same wifi network) side by side. I added the tech support guy so he could get the motion or doorbell notification at the same time we did and he said he got it, but neither my wife nor I got it. After he asked me to take the device down and do a hard reset, I told him no thanks, and that I tried that an hour ago and it didn't work.Waited a few hours and heard someone knocking on my door. It was a friend of my son's. He said he tried ringing the Ring bell, but we never got any notification of the ring or of the motion (no ring alert or motion alert) so I called tech support a 3rd time. This guy tells me he is going to change the setting for how long the Ring waits after detecting motion to send the alert???!!! I asked him why is there ANY wait? The idea is that when motion is detected, i want to know, no latency! He made the change and we did a test and IT WORKED! That was the only time it worked. Since then neither the doorbell nor the motion detector work consistently or reliably. I have yet to get a motion alert in time to see the actual motion.Total POS. Maybe I got a bad one, but I see no reason to try a second time.

title: Great concept, poor video quality,expensive home security.. Cheaper options available
body: Update 10.10.20I decided to downgrade my 4 star rating to a 1 star rating. As many other buyers have already expressed, once you stop paying for the monthly subscription the device immediately ceases to work or function properly. Pulling up live feed takes forever and the two chimes I bought simply refuse to reconnect. I ended up purchasing the XTU doorbell which can work which can work with a micro sd card if you do not wish to avail of their cloud storage.This Ring doorbell is going in the bin. Most expensive piece of junk I ever purchased on Amazon.I love the concept of this product. The look of the door bell is very elegant. We did not have a doorbell at all and I was really happy I could have installed this on my own without the need for busting walls to wire this doorbell.The live feed is adequate enough and I Iove the option to speak to the person at the door via the app even if I am in another country. The motion sensor is adequate and gives comfort to know you will be alerted when someone approaches your home while you are away. I bought the chime which I realized was unnecessary as this device can be connected to Alexa which will alert you when the doorbell is rung or motion is detected and I have Alexa devices throughout our home.If you do not have a range of Alexa devices like we do then the chime will be a good buy. The video quality on this device leaves much to be desired and you don't see persons faces clearly unless they walk right up to the device. I suppose if the person who trips off the motion sensor is already known by you then you can figure out its them approaching your house but other then that the video is blurry. The video also does not give you a good clear panoramic view.. The effect is more like peering through a peep hole.I was going to purchase three Ring Flood lights with camera to compliment this doorbell... But after doing research what many other viewers have said is true... This is a ridiculously expensive home security option when you consider the cost for the devices and then the monthly subscription fees. One Ring Flood light with camera is presently $299 so if I have to buy three that's $900...and then I will have to pay US$120 a year for cloud storage basically for as long as I own the products. This is a terrible option when considering that you can purchase the (Hiseeu Wireless Security Camera System 8CH NVR + 4Pcs 1080P Cameras + 1TB Hard Drive) for $229 and get all the features of ring such as motion sensor and email alerts... Best part is you get a 1TB hardrive so you don't have to pay a monthly fee for storage... And you get four wireless cameras.. All of this for $229 when one Ring Floodlight and cam is $299 and you get absolutely no storage unless you pay! Madness!So yea.... I am not buying any more Ring devices when there are far more cheaper solutions available... But I will keep the door bell...its nice.... But it's pretty much a ridiculously expensive doorbell without the monthly subscription.

title: 2 stars is being generous (and I've since changed my mind)
body: I research EVERYTHING before buying, but apparently I didn't research this enough.Most of the time when I try to connect to either answer a ring or just to view, it doesn't connect. "Activating" "Connecting" "Trying to Connect" "Unable to connect" So what else is new?Here are the three scenarios I have experienced:1) Sometimes it actually does connect very quickly, by accident I presume.2) Other times it takes 30-60 seconds, or longer, to connect. Anyone who has pushed the button has left by then.3) The other times it never connects and I either just have to try again (and again) or force-stop the app and try again. I really do have better things to do with my life.I called their 'customer support', and I use that term loosely. All they would tell me is that it's my fault... the internet, the router, the signal, the weather... whatever. The device is hardwired to my existing wiring (not running on battery) and my router is less than 15' away. EVERYTHING else in the house runs flawlessly on the WiFi. All except this thing.But is it working even if I can't connect to it and view it, or answer the door? (Which sort of negates having it if I can't connect to it when I need to.) On the occasions when the app opens, if I look at the history I can see that it has recorded events. However, it usually starts recording AFTER the person is already very close to the door. (Yes, I know how to set the zone sensitivity.) And then I can watch them walking away from the door. By the way, I don't recognize most people from just their backside. And when it does record movement, it records for only 20 seconds, even if movement is still taking place. If there's a slow moving person, I MAY see them come to the door, but not whatever they might have done on my porch or on my property after the first 20 seconds. Again, mostly worthless. For example, recently a delivery vehicle came to my house, and I did see that in the recording, but only AFTER they parked, not driving up. But after 20 seconds it stopped recording, so I never saw the driver get out of the vehicle, never saw him/her walk to the house, and never saw them return to the vehicle and drive away. The next recording was of another car driving by (already halfway past the house) and the delivery vehicle was no longer there. The device never saw or recorded it leavingSo, if you're Ok with being unable to see or connect to the device most of the time, this is definitely the device for you. And if want to buy mine, please let me know. I have no real use for it other than to look at it and realize that I made a mistake buying it.UPDATE: If you enjoy paying for things EVERY MONTH, then this is the product for you. Without their 'subscription plan' (monthly FEE) the only thing you'll see if try to view the camera, is a live view (if it works at all). Who was the person that knocked or rang the bell 2 minutes ago? You'll never know. You have to PAY AND PAY AND PAY to see that. I'm disgusted with this POS.

title: Flawed and useless
body: I was very excited when I received my Ring door bell, but that went away fast when I started having problem after problem. Here is the list:- The unit does not record unless you have a paid cloud account with Ring. Other companies offer free cloud storage (or at least they store your recordings for free the first 24 hours).- My unit is advertised to record in 720P, but it has inferior quality compared to an IP camera that I have with the same resolution.- The device stops recording after 20-30 seconds for battery-operated devices even if physically installed as hardwired (60 seconds option is only for hardwired devices without a battery option).- When you try to answer an event on the app, it takes a while to open and by the time it loads, the person at your door is gone.- When you star an event it saves it, but when I check a few days later is gone, these happened every single time.- Connection to Wi-Fi is not stable, even when my router is less than 10 feet away and I have the Ring device on a designated network with around 64 Mbps download speed and 12 Mbps upload.- After 2 weeks it stopped ringing the chime (which is mechanical and it works fine if I use a regular doorbell push button)- After 3 weeks itworks stopped charging (even though I have 22V coming from the transformer and at the wires connected to the mounting plate/charger) That's when I decided that enough is enough and returned it.- I reached out to customer service a couple of times but they tried to blame my Wi-Fi, my phone or my wiring and couldn't offer any help.I would like to add that I am an IT professional with Network and Low Voltage experience and I did extensive troubleshooting to no avail, including:- Restarted Wi-Fi router and monitored connection speeds and bandwidth, which checked okay.- Restarted phone (Samsung S8) uninstalled and reinstall app.- Removed and re-added Ring device a couple of times- Changed transformer. Originally, I had a 16V transformer and upgrade it to 24V, just in case that 16V was not enough juice.- Ran new wiring from Ring device to transformer AND from transformer to Chime. All voltage readings checked okay at every endpoint, and all continuity tests passed.- Replaced the ChimeBottom Line:My main complaint is about the short length of the recordings, which in addition to the lagging on picking up movement, makes a very poor system to detect and record events at your property. All a burglar needs to do is to cover their face for 30 seconds). The company has advertised for a while a new feature (the user ability to control the length of the recordings) but they have not delivered yet. Maybe I got a faulty device, but after reading so many customer reviews about issues with the motion detector feature and other flaws, I will stay away from these kind of devices until the technology has improved. I read about other brands having the same issues as well.

title: JUNK! Just an overpriced doorbell
body: I have had my Ring Video Doorbell since the beginning of June and to this day have not got it to work as advertised. I contacted support numerous times, each time they had a different excuse as to why it wasn't working correctly, and each time provided a "fix" which did not work. I also had the device exchanged through Amazon, and the problems still persisted. The app which provides the settings for the doorbell has settings to adjust the areas of motion which are detected, frequency of alerts etc. Still I get only two results... it detects EVERYTHING (even motion which is not there) regardless of settings resulting in false alerts from sunrise to sunset OR it detects only motion within five feet or less of the doorbell. Either way completely defeats the purpose of having purchased it to begin with. There is also the issue of the first few seconds of video being completely whited out, which half the time results in the videos being completely useless. Mind you the video starts recording with a delay as well. I should also mention that even with it being minimally functional, the battery life is nowhere near as long as advertised and it takes almost an entire day to fully charge it.Overall I would not recommend this device to anyone as it is nothing more than an overpriced doorbell that needs to be charged every two months. I am sure they could come out with a firmware update to address many of the issues, however, they instead came out with a new doorbell with these fixes and priced it at twice the cost. If I could have rated it with zero stars I would have because that is what the device deserves.AUGUST 2019 UPDATE - Never did get the device working as advertised and that's with buying every accessory under the sun! I initially contacted Ring support at least 15 times and they ran me in circles until I couldn't return it. The best I've gotten it to work is now, where it only detects motion within six feet and defeats the purpose of having it. I can't extend the range ANY because if I do I get nonstop motion alerts. Ring support said it's due to traffic 90+ feet away when it's only supposed to have a range of 30 feet. I recently got a new router and needed to take the doorbell down to hit the Setup button they stupidly place on the back. But the $0.99 screwdriver made for the device snapped off. Finally got them to send a replacement screwdriver, after trying to get me to buy a whole new doorbell, and I got the screwdriver today. First time I tried to unscrew a screw it twisted the screwdriver into a corkscrew and the tip broke off inside the screw. Ring support said I'll have to "remove it with force" but because it's no longer under warranty, they will give me a code to buy another useless doorbell at a discount to replace it.DO NOT BUY IT! If I could give it negative stars I would. It doesn't work as advertised, it's made with cheap materials and Ring customer support is one of the worst I've dealt with.

title: Stopped working after two months, not reliable
body: [[VIDEOID:dbb69c38b8cce2de0b9ba1c88468a4e1]] Update 10/05/17: Customer service's promise was never honored, the labeled box for returning this item never arrived. These products have no warranty, as seen in the other reviews, once you buy this unit you're on your own, no return policy, they would only offer to replace your new device with a used one... This device only worked for a few weeks, just enough to clear the short amazon.com money back deadline and then it stops working. This product has been a scam in our experience, buyers be cautious. Update 9/19/17: Customer service has been outstanding, we had to cancel our subscription and returned the device because we were exhausted from continuously reconnecting it, restarting, and buying more components to make it work. I would point out that customer service was very polite, local english speaking, and very supportive.We give this company 2 stars on the product but 5 stars for customer support, Gabriel from customer service was very supportive and we plan on buying these products in the future when it becomes more reliable. Update 9/18/2017: it's been about two months from the date of purchase and this device has already died... it worked on and off when it was hardwired to the doorbell's 16V line, it only staied connected continuously for about two weeks and we had to keep reconnecting to the router that is only 12 feet away... We're done with it. We're cancelling our subscription and returning everything we bought for this thing. We had purchased the Ring Chime decice hoping that it would work better with that speaker, but no luck.the other day it got stuck emitting a hight pitch noise and had enought time to record it on video, will post it as soon as we figure out how to upload videos to the reviews. We are just too tired of hoping for this unit to work reliably.The presentation is great, it also comes with its own tools for installation, but the functionality is disappointing; it has potential but doesn't work very well quite yet.The volume on the phone app is so low no one can hear it... there are no settings for tones, you are stuck with the factory sound. The notifications only work half of the time and all it does is send a visual note that looks like a text, no sound, sometimes it barely vibrates for a microsecond, but that's it. It doesn't provide enough sound or vibration to notice that someone has pushed the bell button.The video quality is great, the video recording service is very good, the field of view of the camera is perfect, the liveview option is hard to find in the app (as there are two fields with the same title) but it does work great once you find out how to access it.Long story short, it is a good idea but we never know when someone is ringing the doorbell... we learn about it after the fact when we happen to look at our phones.

title: Camera works great - customer service and "video storage fees" SUCK
body: My husband got this for me for christmas because the way our house is set up I can't see who is at the door unless I open it (which you don't always want to do). This works great showing me who's at the door so I can feel comfortable opening it, or ignoring it.I liked the doorbell so much that after 2 months I signed up for their explorer/beta program. I had been pleased with the 30 day view of any video, but after 4 months paid the stupid extra fee of $30 to be able to SAVE the video recordings from the doorbell. I needed to save the videos for some contractor work I had going on at my house. Otherwise I wouldn't have upgraded. Being able to see the videos for 30 days was fine for me.I then purchased a stickup camera and installed it. We set up the motion detection, but unless we watched it live, couldn't see any of the videos of what JUST happened 1 minute earlier. Apparently ring changed their policy in the few months since we had signed up for the doorbell. Now the only way to see any videos was if we paid ANOTHER $30 to view the videos! Ridiculous. Storage is cheap. Amazon, Apple, and Google sell it super cheap - why should I pay more money to ring to see a video from 1 minute ago? There should be either a tiered option for storage with them, or allow people to choose their own storage options for the videos (to another cloud service). It's not like the cameras are so cheap either - I paid $200+ for a camera or doorbell and now can't even see the videos recording on it for some period of time unless I give them more money. I should be able to see some amount of videos - whether it be 30 days, 15 days, 7 days, or even 48 hours - SOMETHING - included in the purchase price. Holding my videos hostage until I pay an additional $30 for each camera is ridiculous.I then contacted Ring's customer support about this - questioning when this changed occurred (no longer showing videos for 30 days in the free account) and why the cost was so high for each camera. The rep then wrote back and said he put both cameras on a 30 day trial. UM that's nice except that I PAID already for a one year video recording subscription for my doorbell 4 months ago!!!! My credit card was charged - I have email receipts etc. The rep emailed: "Huh? Do you ever show being charged, at any time? If you did, we failed on our side. We never show you being charged." So that's convenient. Not only do they want me to pay an additional $30 for the second camera, they are trying to make me pay another $30 for the camera I already paid for because they now claim they have no record of the charge - even though I can prove my amazon visa was charged... This issue is still on going...So to sum up, cameras work great - they are really helpful. Holding all video hostage until users pay is BS. Trying to charge customers multiple times for something they already paid for is totally sketchy.

title: So disappointing...
body: I waited...I waited to give them a chance to work through any kinks, and then (fortunately it turns out), I decided to try out the lower end model before shelling out a couple of hundred for a product with mixed reviews. And then, when Amazon bought them, I thought - yay! I trust it now, and it is on my list. Months later, I finally buy it and anxiously await the arrival of my new gadget that will allow me to save packages from the rain and feel a bit more secure overall. (It wasn't that high on my list, admittedly.)So, it arrives. Well-packaged, on time. Simple install and set-up instructions, which is good since I had to do it 3 times due to conflicting wi-fi signals (phone app - not their fault). Then, with my son's help, we mount the device neatly on the door and proceed to push the button - and I get these lovely notifications on my phone. That when followed show me a black screen with the words "Your phone is having trouble connecting to Ring."So...I restart my phone. I google it. I remove the device, re-add the device, re-set-up the device (2x this time)...no love. So, I check my router and wi-fi speed test and then I buy the Chime Pro wi-fi extender, wait anxiously for it to arrive, and go through all of the steps again (since first time obviously didn't take, but that's understandable)..and...."Your phone is having trouble connecting to Ring." Every...effin'...time...."Your phone is having trouble connecting to Ring."...every effin' TIME....... I meditate a bit, trying to regrow that lost hair...have a couple of drinks, try it again, and "Your phone is having trouble connecting to Ring."....sigh....it's time to write my own review.I feel like my name is now Dave and Front Door is HAL. I don't know if I should take it out back and shoot it or just leave it up as a constant reminder to be kind to our AI brothers and sisters....and maybe, just maybe, one day it will show me a picture...maybe...and it's not like it can actually do anything anyway other than sporadically notice my comings and goings and possibly send blank pictures to the cloud....of my sporadically coming and going - and maybe the lizards on the wall...but, I digress...I still haven't taken it down - don't want those ugly holes in the wall showing my shame to my neighbors with their "traditional" doorbells...oh...the humanity....But, seriously, save your money - or at least don't waste it on the Ring (one, I presume) version of this product. Maybe after Amazon has a chance to really dig into it, we'll get a new release that fits the same wall plate (or at least same ugly holes in my front wall) and actually works. Like Echo. (And no, I don't trust the Ring Pro now either - if this stinking turd is still on the market, I can only surmise that Pro is more of the same with a coat of lipstick.)

title: Super dud!!!!!
body: After two days with support at Ring it is clear that this product is a dud. I was really excited to receive this, and bought the Chime to go with it.Well, if the sole purpose of this product is to let you know who is at your door or gate and to let you communicate with that person it is a total failure. And if I have to answer one more question from tech support about where my router is positioned (in my tiny house) in respect to the doorbell as if that was the obvious answer to all the problems with this product and not a problem with the product itself...my head might just explode. First, the quality of the video is horrendous (see picture). It reminds me of old video tape after 10 generations of duplication. Second, night vision...what night vision? I have a camera in my yard that switches to night vision when it gets dark (way way cheaper than this paperweight) so I know what it's suppose to look like. The video switches to black and white and very clear. I'd left the still uninstalled doorbell in the living room last night and it got activated by something...maybe my dog. So I looked at my phone and my iPad to see how the night vision was working. Uh...total blackness, except for a speck of reflective light so I knew it was on and working. So I thought maybe it needed a little light to work so I went down and turned on a couple of lights. Nope..no night vision...just the low light affect of a room in a red ambient glow. I stepped up to the camera to see if my face would b discernible in this non night vision low light but my face was but a blob. Would show you a screengrab of that from the saved video but tech support had me uninstall the app from my device and then couldn't get connected again so can't access account. So aside from horrible quality picture, and no night vision as it claims to have, the doorbell camera end of it would not receive audio when I answered the ring. I tried talking to the "visitor" using both my android phone and my iPad and he could not hear me, although I could hear him. And to cap it all off, while testing the device as my friend was about to install it outside, it would freeze up and we had to wait a few minutes before being able to ring doorbell again. Don't think my visitors would appreciate that much...or package delivery. Tech support didn't really have any answers. We checked the upload speed when the devices froze presumably trying to upload or access videos, which he said was fine. Everything else internet wise and speed wa fine too. This is not cutting edge technology. It's pretty basic nowadays so no excuse for it to be so low quality and so ineffective. So yeah...I will be returning this which has taken up way to much time that I can never get back. An expensive paperweight for sure.

title: BIGGEST PIECE OF TRASH I EVER SPENT MONEY ON
body: UPDATE:Before returning my Ring Doorbell, I emailed the company about what a terrible experience this thing was. The company replied and sent me a FREE unit and said they'd also send a WiFi extender along with it which should help. The new doorbell came today, but no wifi extender was with it. Regardless, I have my own wifi extender literally right next to the doorbell anyway, and the signal to my router is also strong from the spot.I found it does not matter whether I connect to the extender or the original router, the thing DOES NOT WORK. This is the SECOND unit I've tested and it flat out just does not work the way they say it does.How the HELL anyone is giving this complete TURD of a product 4 and 5 stars is beyond my comprehension. This thing flat out refuses to work. I bought Netgear Arlo wireless cameras to replace this thing after I took it back, and I will be mounting and installing those soon enough now that this second doorbell was a DUD.STAY AWAY FROM THIS THING UNLESS YOU WANT A HEADACHE (and if you don't want to ever know if someone came to your door).Anyone who may have been looking at these... Hands down THE biggest piece of garbage I've ever spent money on. Going back to Lowe's after owning it for only 2 days.I was excited to have found the answer to 2 problems in one box, a security camera and a doorbell.First of all, you cannot access the camera at all unless someone is at the door. It pretty much negates the possibility of using it as a security camera to check in on your place. "Supposedly" they are adding this feature "soon." I would not count on it.Second, and MORE IMPORTANTLY this product is COMPLETELY USELESS as a DOORBELL. This doorbell can take up to half an hour to a FULL HOUR OR MORE to notify you when someone is at your door.After a test where my phone never got the notification I receive a "someone is at your door" notice almost an HOUR later.The video quality is not even remotely close to what they show in their sales videos. It is LITERALLY the difference in quality between Austin Powers first video car phone and the second one he got at the end of the movie. Even on a Wi-Fi lan with 150mbps up and down the video is choppy, lags, and drops audio.If I could give this $200 piece of garbage zero stars on Amazon I would. I now have 4 holes in a prominent place in the siding of my house thanks to this piece of trash and now I have to spend more money for some sort of decorative plate to try and hide them.BE WARNED — THIS THING IS A COMPLETE PIECE OF TRASH. If you want to find out for yourself, make CERTAIN you buy it from a store with a good return policy!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

title: Completely unreliable garbage.
body: I set mine up on battery power, with Ring and Motion alerts enabled. I've gone through two devices in less than five months. It's always something with this thing.The first doorbell always showed 100 percent battery right up until the battery died. And, it just died. No soft shutdown where it saved settings before turning off. Just died. After charging, I had to set it up all over again. Just about the time I had realized how long the battery lasts, after using it for three months, it lost all audio functions.I finally got the replacement about a week later. Unlike like the first one, the ring button didn't work right from the start. At least the button on the first one lasted about a week. It occasionally rings, but usually not. Fortunately, when somebody walks up to the door, the motion detection picks them up at the sidewalk, then, when I finally get an alert twenty or thirty seconds later, they're standing at the door, after having already pushed the button that does nothing, so I can just start talking.That's IF I actually get a video feed with the option to answer the call. That video feed with the option to answer the call quit happening this week. All I can do is watch the recorded cloud video after it finally finishes saving to the cloud four or five minutes later. After five complaints to support in four days about this, Ring finally admitted it was an issue on their end and that they have no idea when it'll be fixed. So, if you buy a Ring doorbell right now, you won't be able to use it to answer your door. Hint: Don't buy one.I could even work around this issue if they would actually implement Live View like they've been promising. But, they won't. So, there's still no video on demand either. Just like my old crazy aunt that used to deny your desert if you wore plaid on Tuesdays, Ring won't implement Live View for battery powered devices just cuz.In five months, I've replaced the device once, gone without audio for a week, and gone without being able to answer calls for at least a week. So, for two hundred bucks, you get a doorbell that only rings for about a week. Two way audio that you can't use, and the ability to watch cloud videos that were recorded after the fact, in forty one second intervals. Good luck with that.Since Ring claims Live View does work with hard wired devices, I have to go buy a doorbell transfer and some wire and hard wire the stupid thing in order to get a feature they claim to have, but won't give me, to work around another feature that they claim they have, but doesn't work. I'm pretty sure once it's hard wired, it'll still be crap, so I'll update my review once I've done that.

title: Horrible Customer Service on a Device They're No Longer Interested in Quality Testing
body: I spent two weeks trying to troubleshoot the device Amazon sent me. It would complete the setup process, but never connect for a "Connectivity Test". The doorbell would always show "Offline" in the app, despite sending notifications of motion. If I answered a call or connected when there was motion, audio would not work. Because it showed "Offline", Live View was never an option.After trying all troubleshooting available in their help documentation, I connected with Ring support via chat. As I was verifying my account, the Ring Doorbell suddenly connected to the WiFi for the first time! That was short lived - 2 hours later, the WiFi dropped again and I went through several reset attempts, but could never get it to show online again.I thought my issue might be low battery related despite the app showing 47%, so I charged it for several hours. After fully charging, performing another factory reset, and walking through setup again - the battery level never changed. It got stuck in a firmware update loop for several hours.At this point, I reached out to Ring support again and they spent 3+ hours having me troubleshoot and test various combinations of resets and setups including turning off Bluetooth on my phone, deleting the app, restarting my phone, and waiting on updates that took over an hour. One representative led me on for well over 30 minutes providing "percentage updates" of 33% and 57%. He also stated that these issues were not as common in the newer models when asked (pretty much admitting that they know this product doesn't live up to the same quality checks as their other models). Then as I'm waiting for the next progress update, he randomly says "Have a good day!" and disconnects.I immediately reconnected and was met with a representative that said there were no ways for them to see the progress of an OTA update and that they should never take more than 10 minutes. What!?!All in, I've spent 3 straight weekends trying to set up a device that after 4 collective hours of Ring support has finally been deemed defective. They've offered to send a replacement, but also stated "There is nothing wrong with the model but this device you had was 1 out a tons that had this issue". I'm not certain I'm willing to relive their nightmarish support again though if this replacement doesn't work and will probably just return it at that point.If you're looking at buying one of these Ring Doorbells, skip the entry level for the Video Doorbell 2, the Video Doorbell Pro, or the Video Doorbell Elite. It's clear from their support team that the standard version isn't worth their time to support or test anymore. Or just go with a competitor...

title: DON'T BUY THIS PRODUCT!
body: UPDATE 8-20-2019: This Ring doorbell sucks! The device has an RSSI of 38 which is supposed to be great. I had a package delivered today and the delivery driver walked all the way up to the front door which is about 20 ft in plain view of the Ring doorbell. He set the package on the ground, took a picture of the package, rang the Ring doorbell and walked back to his van. HE NEVER SHOWED UP ON CAMERA, THE RING DOORBELL DID NOT START RECORDING UNTIL HE WAS GONE! This thing is useless.I've had the ring doorbell since Jan 2017 and it's now July 2018, so about a year and a half. I have it hard wire to the doorbell power so it doesn't need batteries. I've had two packages delivered today at different times of the day and I also had someone out to my house to take pictures of a problem area. The problem area is clearly visible to the Ring Doorbell camera but the camera didn't even activate to record the movement so i didn't get a notification at all. When my first package was delivered on my doorstep, directly in front of the Ring Doorbell, I didn't get a notification nor did it activate the camera to record. When the second package was delivered, I got a notification but by the time Ring was finally able to connect (I got a message that it was having trouble connecting, I tried again and it did finally connect.) I could see the additional packages on the doorstep but by the time the camera activated to record, the delivery person had already dropped off the package and left. It will record if someone rings the bell and stands there long enough for the camera to start recording but that really defeats the purpose of why I purchased this product. I want to make sure I get a recording if someone is stealing packages not just dropping them off. The video is also very choppy and grainy. It's a wifi doorbell and I was concerned with the range to my router might be a concern, so I called the manufacturer and explained the issue. They said if could be a problem and told me I might need a wifi extender. Well, it turns out that they happen to sell those also. So I bought the Ring Chime Pro and wifi Extender on May 31 2017 hoping that would solve the problem. So, I've had the Ring Doorbell operating through the Ring wifi Extender for over a year now as well. It's still awful quality and doesn't capture anyone coming to my door about 90% of the time. It's a lot of money for something that doesn't function as described. The description says you get instant alerts when visitors press the bell or trigger the sensor. The alerts have not once been instant for me. It says Crystal clear HD video. I get grainy and choppy on all of my devices at best. 1 star is much more than this product deserves.

title: ONE OF THE WORST PURCHASES I'VE EVER MADE
body: I implore, beg, urge and encourage you not to buy this. I'll try to make this as swift and coherent as possible, so please bear with me! It's important to note that the same issues happened on two different units, meaning the problems are integral to the design.I have excellent WIFI coverage, FYI. The lag-time between the button being pressed and the app being notified ranged from 15s to 50s if it notified me at all. Almost ALWAYS, it was too late to catch the person at the door. On many occasions, pressing the button wouldn't notify the app OR the CHIME unit (which I also purchased). It would do nothing.I tried using it HARDWIRED - this caused repeated issues whereby it would Ding my wired doorbell intermittently. During the night I was awoken by my wired doorbell going DING DING DING DING for no apparent reason until I removed the unit. Using it UNWIRED, the battery lasts only three weeks, WELL short of the suggested 9 months. This means you have to remove the unit and charge it overnight multiple times a year. Utterly impractical and shockingly inconvenient in this day and age. I know this is all boring, but I have to get it off my chest.Contacted customer service on over ten occasions, trying to fix all the above issues. Must say, they were extremely competent and helpful, but but ultimately unable to make the product work as advertised.The Live View (when it worked) is only okay, but when you compare the image and sound quality to the Nest (I have both the older and newer models) it’s embarrassingly inadequate. On many occasions, dare I say most, it simply wouldn't connect to Live View at all.A massive bummer is that I installed this into stucco, so now I have 4 massive holes by my door. I can't return it as I bought it over 6 weeks ago, so now have a $200 piece of junk sitting in a drawer, alongside the CHIME which is equally redundant.How can they release this product to market when it clearly is nowhere near ready? The advertising is simply LIES. If Apple were to release a product at such an early stage of development there’d be riots, and yet RING is charging Apple prices for a cheaply-made, ultimately useless piece of wall-junk. I feel I’ve been ripped off: I believed their enticing sales pitch, i WANTED it to work, gave it the benefit of the doubt for six weeks, and have now returned, tail between legs, to my $2 wired doorbell. Please don’t buy this product. If I could look you in the eye, I’d be doing it now, imploring with wide eyes, nodding slightly. Perhaps even patting your shoulder. DO NOT BUY IT. But if you do, don’t worry, it’s only a doorbell.

title: Just does not work as advertised
body: Just does NOT work as advertised, not even close. I would never buy this again (actually purchased this at Target a few months ago - on sale plus a Target gift card for purchasing; but just installed it before Christmas 2016). Problems / Negatives: 1) The motion settings / zone adjustment doesn't really seem to do anything insofar as eliminating the motion of vehicles passing on the street in front of the house. On the motion zones settings, I have narrowed the motion range settings down to approximately 7 feet. In other words, the Ring is supposed to "ring" or notify me whenever a person gets to within about 7 feet of my front door. No, it doesn't. Instead, a person has to get to within about 3 feet (I've tested it many times) of my front door before I get notified. Plus, on cold mornings, when the temperature is in the 40s or below, the Ring will not "read" a human that is standing right in front of it. Sporadically and erratically, it will not even give me a "ring" notification when someone actually pushes the ring button on the doorbell. More often than not, I get nothing. 2) There seems to be no way to eliminate the Ring from reading vehicles that pass my house on the street, even though these vehicles are a minimum of 80 feet from my front door. Again, the field of motion is set to no more than 7 feet, so why is it STILL giving me notifications when cars, trucks, vans, etc. pass my house, sometimes over 100' away? I get the "There is motion at your front door" notices all day long on my IPhone, I check it, and it is some SUV passing by. Ridiculous. 3) When it does record the motion of the vehicle or person that triggered it to record, the recording last only 3 to 4 seconds, not 30 seconds like the indicator on the app shows it should. So unless someone is standing motionless right in front of the Ring for several seconds, and the Ring records them for its precious 3 seconds, you cannot tell who they are or what they look like. 4) The quality of the recording is about the same as from a 1960s color t.v.. 1080p, 720p - no, more like 1p. 5) When I get the notice on my phone that there is motion at my front door or the doorbell has been rung, click on the notice, the Ring NEVER can connect live to the person, NEVER. The app just gets frozen, spinning and spinning and will not connect. The supposed ability to talk with a person that is standing at your front door WILL NOT WORK. This is supposedly THE feature of the Ring that makes it so attractive --- but in my case, it JUST WILL NOT WORK. I frankly cannot imaging anyone giving this product any sort of positive review.

title: Astoundingly disappointing on multiple levels
body: I am really at a loss to express how disappointing this product was.To start off, I am extremely tech-savvy and my home is wired with a good 40+ z-wave, Insteon, Hue, and Zigbee devices. I also have extensive experience in electrical engineering.Right off the bat, the first Ring doorbell I received would not charge via hardwire. This was a brand-new installation with a new transformer, new resistor, and all voltages confirmed with a volt meter. I spent a good 45 minutes waiting on the phone with Ring tech support to get the inevitable conclusion that this was a defective unit. But instead of just swapping it out right there on the phone, they first wanted me to send back the mounting plate, wait for a new one to be shipped, try the new mounting plate, call back if there was a problem, and THEN they would ship out a new unit. That was very disappointing customer service from the beginning.Luckily, Amazon came to the rescue and shipped out a brand new unit overnight to replace the defective Ring unit.One would think this would solve the problem but alas, no. TWO DEFECTIVE UNITS IN A ROW that would not charge via hard wire. Thinking that there was some freak problem with the transformer, I borrowed a friend's Ring doorbell. Boom--charged right away. So yes, I ended up with two bum units in a row. That should never happen.Setting aside the double hardware failure, a few other major gripes:1) The Android software for this is absolutely awful. It is incredibly slow, buggy, and crashes frequently. And good luck trying to use the "Live View" feature. It takes at least 30 seconds to connect to the doorbell and again, crashes the app the majority of the time. The frustration level is just off the charts and I cannot believe the app ever made it out of beta.2) The Ring does not play well with two or more devices. I have an iPad permanently mounted in my living room for all types of home automation, including the Ring app. The Ring app is always open in the background obviously. Apparently if you have one Ring app opened somewhere and you then try to open it on another device (such as my phone when I'm out of the house)--crash. How is this possible?3) This is just personal, but the actual doorbell sound on this thing is really jarring and there's no way to change it to anything else. That should be a basic feature.4) The interface is slow, slow, slow.This looked like such a wonderful product on the surface but was an utter disappointment. I'll be returning the second defective unit.

title: Excuses and incompetent company. Stay away!
body: Update 7/13/17: Ring sent out this email this afternoon:Ring and Amazon Echo ShowDear Neighbor,We noticed that you recently set up your Ring Alexa Skill. Unfortunately, full support for this integration is currently unavailable for Ring Video Doorbell and Stick Up Cam.Ring Video Doorbell 2, Video Doorbell Pro, Video Doorbell Elite and Floodlight Cam are supported, although there are limitations. For these devices, you will be able to access Live View and direct your Ring device(s) with voice commands, as well as watch live videos from them on your Echo Show.Please note that you cannot talk to your visitors using Alexa on the Echo Show, however you are able to listen to audio from your Ring devices.Click here to learn more about the Ring integration with Amazon Echo Show.We are excited about this new integration and can’t wait to introduce features that let you get the most out of Ring. As soon as further updates are available, you’ll be the first to know.Thanks for your support,The Ring TeamThen I did a chat to verify this information. I had TWO Ring customer service people tell me on their chat that it is compatible. One kept saying it was even after sending her the text of the email. I am so sick of this company. Problem is though - what is something worth buying? Ring can't be the only game in town.As of today, all my Ring videos is the person leaving. They will have you troubleshoot and troubleshoot by pressing the reset button or re-installing the app. Already done, Ring. MULTIPLE TIMES AND THE PROBLEM (WHICH YOU DENY) STILL EXISTS. Just like other issues in the past that you denied then magically were fixed with an update.DO NOT BUY THIS PRODUCTExcuses, excuses. The firmware is screwed up and Ring admits it! They have no ETA to get it fixed. It is "something they are working on." On the phone with Ring right now. Even told me if they replace the unit, the problem will still exist! Asking for a full refund from them and all Ring users should do the same. It is sad that a good idea has been screwed up by incompetent owners and developers. And to help others, my problems were all old video motions back (with no ETA TO FIX) and horrible glare on my motions. Just hung up the phone. Ring refuses refund because I bought it from THEM FROM THEIR AMAZON LISTING. INCOMPETENT PERSON AND SUPERVISOR ACTUALLY CLAIMED THAT AMAZON GOT THE MONEY AND NOT THEM! Stay away from this product. It ends up becoming an expensive doorbell.

title: Your doorbell WAS ringing!  It's barely more than a 200-dollar button.
body: Hmmm, I'm trying to figure out why I don't share the enthusiasm of other reviewers here. I had the Ring app up and running pretty quickly on my Android (Lollipop) phone (a newer quad-core, very fast phone with rock-solid 4G LTE service). The doorbell pairing with my WiFi was equally fast once I realized that I had to temporarily make my hidden network visible to do the setup.The picture quality is very good, even with backlighting. The flashing button light and doorbell chime are great confirmations for visitors that the bell has been rung.My problem is that it sometimes takes a couple seconds to ring my phone and other times, actually MOST of the time, it takes 20 or more seconds. When it takes that long to ring my phone, the bell returns to idle and shuts off the camera / intercom feature rendering it totally useless. I read that there is a "live-view" function to turn it back on being rolled out but I don't have that option.My WiFi signal is not an issue. My phone is not an issue (I've tried it with WiFi and cellular 4G LTE). It seems that the Ring app is rather clunky and processor intensive, resulting in slow response on the phone. The app constantly displays, "wait". Reminds me of the days of dial-up! No thank you.***UPDATE*** After several months using this doorbell, I like it even less. After talking to tech support, I purchased a WiFi extender and placed it inside within 5ft of the door and have a "very good" signal according to the Ring APP. That said, this camera is still totally unreliable. Most of the time it senses motion and activates just as the person is walking away so all you see is the back of the person as they walk out of the frame. When someone rings the bell I receive notification almost immediately on the phone but, by the time the phone connects to the video on the bell, the person is usually gone - totally useless!Ring finally added a "live view" to the app but it is also pretty slow and unpredictable. It usually takes 30-seconds or more to "log in" to the camera, often resulting in a connection failure.Finally, once every couple of months the Ring disconnects from the network for no apparent reason and will not reconnect. That means disconnecting the entire unit and bringing it inside to run the installation program again.Again, the Ring concept is fantastic! If this device worked, it would be amazing. Unfortunately, it's too glitchy and not ready for primetime, especially at this pricepoint.

title: Deeply flawed Inconsistent Product and Service
body: For the most part I can view activity from outside the home with this unit however it is a deeply flawed device and service. I am a tech savvy person so install only had minor snags. When I first installed it was indicated that I would need a permit in my state and county if using qs a security system. The dropdown selections for the state and county had multiple companies with Ring listed so I wasn't sure which one was correct. The call with ring support was crap, their first line support bumped me to tier two immediately and I was given an attitude and basically told there is nothing they could do but to contact my county. They wouldn't even let me read them the options my county had, they talked over me and there was no desire to help me aside from saying a scripted response of "this request falls outside of the scope of our service". They didn't even hear the options I had! I had to randomly pick one because my county does not have phone support for this. I have had this unit for 6 months and here's the deal. Mine has no night vision so I have to leave a light on out front to see anything in the video at night. It frequently fails to display live view stating to try again. I have to completely close the ring app several times and try again and eventually I get lucky. About 1 to 2 times a week it won't display live view or record video for like 8 hours. It randomly picks back up again. No video and no alerts while it is down. I called support to troubleshoot this issue and it took over a half an hour to try multiple things to get it reconnected. We had to take the device down from the mount, hold a reset button on the back, disable it and reinstall it. For something that loses connection several times a week, I don't have time to do this every time it occurs. I have to wait the 8 or so hours for it to decide to work again. So when the device is down it's down. If it goes out at a late night hour when you are asleep then how are you to know? Or do you want to be on the phone for 30 minutes or more trying to troubleshoot this in the middle of the night? When charging no alerts to let you know when it is charged. The lights just stop blinking on it hours later. Shame no app alert to let you know when it is ready to put back up. I purchased an indoor rated stick up cam that died after first time plugging it in and setting up. Amazon replaced it. If you are concerned about security I recommend something else. These are quick and cheap for several scenarios but the product is inconsistent.

title: Does Not Reliably Perform as Advertised
body: I would not purchase it again. I have done a lot of testing and been on three calls with their people. In summary, the availability of live video upon a doorbell ring always works. Whether or not you get an audible warning on your phone is random. The wording of the documentation led me to believe the video image would automatically pop up. It does not, sorta. You will get a banner on your phone that you have to touch to open the video. This is the functionality that Ring says exists. Yet about 50% of the time a video window does open on its own. Ring says this is not possible.The’ motion detection is unreliable, from both an environment and software standpoint. Their solution is to send out a wedge kit to angle the doorbell. Even though mine is an optimum installation. The motion zones do not seem to make any difference, there are many false positives and false negatives, and it will rarely pick up motion at night.After a 30 day trial, there is a $3.00 per month charge for recording and storing video. At first I thought it was pretty cool and was going to order it. Now that things are so random it is not worth it. So if there is an alert you are going to be quick to get to the image or you will miss it.I found the support people to be kind and professional. They answer the phone quickly. But they do not seem to have enough knowledge to handle a problem. It seems there go to solution for every problem is to send you a wedge.I will probably keep it though. I bought the cheaper one and that may be my problem. While it does not do many of the things advertised consistently, it does the one thing I need. When I am at home and hear MY doorbell ring I can manually go to live view and see who is at the door. I will probably not be scaring away burglars from a coffee shop.Finally, I know technology pretty well, and should have realized the folks at Ring could look at the video coming from my doorbell at anytime. When I was talking to a support person she mentioned there was a garbage truck in front of my house. A little bit later she asked my permission to do something with my equipment, and sent me a code to read back to her. Later on I mentioned that it was an awfully powerful capability, but she said they always had to have permission to activate my camera before doing so. I think she forgot that she was looking at my garbage truck prior to asking my permission.Very cool and very creepy, all at the same time.

title: Updated: I have never hated a product so much in my life.
body: [[VIDEOID:56817d8325e5943318e65a3ae7a13aa7]] I bought this from Ring's website, but like most others I buy most everything from Amazon so I thought I would post it here. I can honestly say that I have never written a bad Amazon review until now. This product is absolutely terrible. I have it sitting at the end of a 100 ft driveway mounted to a garage. The camera has been absolutely terrible and the motion settings are inconsistent if not downright terrible. My house has been broken into twice since the camera has been installed (it is empty) with people driving down the driveway right in front of the camera and it not picking them up. The night vision: good luck seeing anything it is pitch black in the videos at night. Everyday when I pull up to the garage, I drive right up to the camera and park in front of it, get out of my car and walk past it without it ever picking me up. But it will catch cars driving down the street all the time which sets off the chime and my phone all day, but misses when people drive right up to it. Also, the zoom is useless. I contacted ring regarding the inconsistent motion and if there was anything that I can do to improve its results and their response was "well we see that it is catching motion". I was very excited for this product which may be why I am so disappointed, but for the amount of money you will spend on this, buy a decent security camera system that has motion alerts, actual decent cameras, and can be linked to your phone(they are out there). I have never been more disappointed with a product.Update:I cannot express the anger that I have with this and customer support. Someone broke into my house for a third time this morning. Ring didn't catch him again as he walked right past the camera. The ring is within 5 feet of our router that gets 150mbps speeds. They do not have an answer for me. Do not expect this to do its job. It is simply good to see who rang the bell and that is it. The motion detecting is a joke. I will be throwing this in the trash and buying an actual camera system that you can actually see clearly.I have attached a video taken today to give you an example. I walked from that open garage, around the truck and in between the truck and the Audi and into the garage next to where the camera was mounted. You don't see me, because the motion detection didnt activate until I was already past. I have several more videos just like it, but this is one to give you an example.

title: Worst, most frustrating piece of junk EVER! DO NOT BUY!
body: Incredibly poor product; customer service is worse. I bought a Ring Pro. It didn't work, but the Customer Service rep was so incompetent that it took 3 hours to prove that it was nonfunctional, even though that was evident from the outset. They sent me another one. NOT overnight. NOT 2-day shipping. Slow boat from China, "you should get it in 5-7 business days" shipping, TO REPLACE A NON-FUNCTIONAL PRODUCT I paid over $200 for.So the new one arrives. NO video. Doesn't work except as a doorbell, and badly at that. I've now spent about 4 hours to replace my perfectly good old doorbell with a $200 POS that won't display video. Customer service tells me my WiFi signal is too weak. Mind you, I can sit on my front porch 2 feet from the Ring Pro and stream Blu-Ray movies on my laptop, but somehow that's not a good enough signal for this piece of junk. So they send me (no charge, but again, S-L-O-W) a "Chime Pro" WiFi extender. I plug it in, everything connects, but the "Live View" video is so highly pixelated that I cannot see anything clearly except one patch of grass in my front yard. VERY helpful!Customer service tells me the Chime Pro has to be CLOSER to the Ring Pro, which is NOT convenient given the absence of electrical outlets near my front door, but I grab an extension cord and relocate it, and at this point, it SORT OF works. I can see a still video, but any motion at all, like a car driving by out front, gives me one or two pixelated frames to "capture" the action. DRAMATICALLY WORSE than a cheap webcam. And just to be clear, even without the range extender, I have a WiFi signal that is perfectly fine everywhere in my house and yard, but somehow this complete piece of TRASH cannot function properly even with THEIR OWN range extender. Whatever you do, avoid this AWFUL, AWFUL product.(Update several frustrating weeks later: Went through a total of three separate Ring Pro doorbells, a Chime Pro WiFi Range extender, and an angle bracket to get the thing actually aimed at my porch. Three installs, three removals. Never worked for crap. I did finally get turned over to a competent, helpful Customer Service Rep (Victoria), but it made no difference. The PRODUCT is terrible. I am convinced that by the time I was done with the 3rd unit, it was "working" as designed. It is just a BAD PRODUCT. Finally returned the whole box of junk for a refund, and now I am searching for an alternative.)

title: Pass this item up.
body: Update: Support told me that 20 feet was optimal and that I needed an extender as mine is 24 feet from the Access Point. This despite the fact that my measured throughput at the bell is 34Mbps. That is just about 7 times what Netflix requires to stream HD quality!It certainly seems like their device is operating outside of what is expected. In the Internet of Things age we live in, how many technologies do you own that only work "optimally" within a 20 feet arc?This wouldn't be so bad if support wasn't so difficult to deal with. I'm going on a week and despite providing them mountains of evidence, they are not at all receptive to providing me my money back.Check the reviews; while it works for many, there is a significantly high failure rate...and ton of unhappy campers. Ring's reply is to force you to purchase more technology to compensate for their product's weakness. This is not a consumer friendly company.Caveat emptor.My experience with Ring parallels that of some others who complain about the problems around video capture..The motion capture feature only captures video after the fact. That is, when the UPS person drops a package on my porch, the capture simply shows the box on my porch. It does not capture the UPS person walking up the walk, up the stairs, down the stairs and back down the walk. There seems to be a delay from when the motion occurs to when the video starts recording.In a different test, when a USPS delivery was attempted, the video camera did not turn on until the USPS person’s finger was an inch or two from the button. (Yes, the sensitivity is turned to max).Tech support seems to have no answers. Frankly, their attention on the matter was seriously lacking. It seemed to be the type of support where if things moved off their script, they became lost and ceased to respond to emails.One other annoying aspect of Ring is should you miss the Ring, you can not press a button and have it reconnect you with the door. You literally need to answer the bell while it is ringing to establish connection with the visitor. This is not always practical with a cell phone that may be in a purse or pocket.Interesting technology with promise but the implementation seems to have some bugs and their support seems woefully inadequate to do more than throw canned responses back to the customer when there is a legitimate problem.

title: PIxelated videos bcs ambient light,no  local storage to view videos offline, SLOOOW.  Keep disconnecting frm WiFi,Not worth it.
body: 1. The images and videos from the camera pointing outside are pix-elated. Camera was replaced one, then customer support assisted in the problem with the next camera, initially blaming the internet connection, but that was not the reason when I moved the router next to the camera inches away. The conclusion was pretty much the camera outside by the front door on a covered porch CAN NOT produce a decent quality image of the person ringing the bell because of the ambient light.-So, if the ambient light is a problem with the video quality, then what? Use the camera inside? It does not make sense.-2. The fact you have to pay a monthly subscription to have access to the videos, it is outrageous. Why the camera does not have an internal SD card where the owner can check "offline" the videos? How much can this add to the price of the camera? The vendor try to drag you on a monthly subscription service to check YOUR videos? Does not make sense either.-----UPDATE: I wish the rate will have a negative amount of stars. I will give it a minus two (2).After a while I noticed the time between somebody ring the bell and get the notification on the phone LOCALLY is noticeable long. Very. Again, last technology updated router: checked. 3 feet away from the router: check. 65Mbps ISP: checked. And to make it worse I am on the phone waiting for someone to give some information about how to proceed because now my door bell ring does not even ring. So I have to put the old analog switch again.-----UPDATE again, my sons bike was stolen in front of the camera and nothing happened. I just filed a police report regarding my son stolen bike from the porch in front of this $%#$% camera. Since now the ring camera is keeping disconnecting from my Wi-Fi all the time, pretty much no notifications are being made over the phone. This is just an expensive bell switch. The only reason I have not remove this and gently fixed with a hammer is because now I need to pay someone to put the original bell switch the way it was.-I truly regret the day I had bough this. Big, BIG waste of time and money now. My honest advice? Get some type of camera that actually works, high resolution, local storage so your videos in your loafers wont hit the internet when another hacking event happen for having your private information in the cloud. - (minus) Five stars now. DO not waste your time and money on this brand.

title: BELIEVE THE REVIEWS THAT SAYS IT WON'T SEE PEOPLE APPROACH YOUR DOOR.
body: UPDATED YET AGAIN: It has continued to intermittently send our regular doorbell chime into seizure. I have given up using it for it's intended function. Typically our kids have gotten to the door, asked who it was, and gotten an answer before the app has even aroused itself on my phone. It is fun to go back through videos it has captured and see my flag waving in the middle of the night, or car lights driving by in the distance. Still haven't captured any useful information on it though.UPDATED AGAIN: The unit refuses to see actual people who walk up to my front door, no matter what I do with the sensitivity settings. It doesn't miss a ripple when my flag waves though.UPDATE: The diode problem solved itself, go figure. The motion sensor has proven to be a real problem however. I have used various sensitivity settings, all with poor results. No matter how I set it, it will pickup our flag waving out front. It also frequently picks up trees swaying across the street, the sun popping out from behind a cloud, etc. While it is picking all this up, it somehow failed to pick up someone driving into our driveway (directly in the line of sight), walking up the front steps, and taking items off of our front porch. Five minutes before and after I have nice video of our flag waving though.This is a great idea for a product. However, the execution leaves alot to be desired. When mounting the mounting plate, I drilled pilot holes, and started to screw in the screws provided. One by one, they all snapped. So I wasted a half hour or so going to find my 3/4" stainless screws I should have used in the first place. Not a huge deal, but definitely has me wondering about the quality of the rest of components.THEN, 24 hours after installation, my existing doorbell (which is actived by the Ring.com unit) went into seizure. This necessitated me removing the device from the mounting plate, unwiring it, and troubleshooting to determine that the 4 cent diode they sent me was bad. So now the unit does not ring my doorbell, and I get to take it apart and rewire it again if I ever get the diode. It's not like there's lots of Radio Shacks around any more. Ring said they would send me the diode, so we'll see how long that takes.So far I find the product really irritating, particularly so since if they had spent another 25 cents per unit I would not be having any of these problems.

title: Only charges with VAC and not DC voltage: Inability to reconnect to WiFi remotely renders it absolutely useless.
body: was a bit disappointed as I thought my old door bell (COMMAX VideoDoorPhone) can be removed and Ring Video DoorBell can be installed due to the WiFi access to my phone. But it turned out unlike other Video DoorBells, this connects only to 8-24VAC (Alternate Current) and not to low voltage DC current which is the supply to my door outside. Fixing a AC-DC transformer is not possible too. So I am left with the option of charging the bell every 10-15 days as the battery drains. I gather most of the countries have low voltage DC supply in the old door bells.The best thing I appreciate is the excellent customer support. They are quite sincere and take pains to solve the problems. I had an issue with the Internet connection and the software did not respond. I was sent another Ring Door Bell within a week or so. And my cloud subscription was extended for 6 months for the inconvenience caused!It is a cool device and mostly perfect except the voltage issue which they said they will look to improve in the next version. I will buy another one once they do for my 2nd home.I will recommend anyone to buy this product esp. if they have VAC low voltage supply in the existing door bell.Review in March 2018: I had 7 Ring Door Bells and 7 Ring Chimes/Chime Pros; 6 of them in my building in India and one here in middle east. I paid for annual plan and I was promised by the Ring Customer Care guy that the plan will cover all my 7 devices and all the door bells' cloud recording was OK too. But suddenly today, my local Ring Door Bell stopped showing cloud recording; after 5 months! On contacting the "supposedly good" customer support, I was told that I must pay for another plan for this device.I complained that this should be informed to me in November itself and not to discontinue the service all of a sudden. They don;t seem to care. I guess the customer care gone bad now.Another thing is: Two Ring Door Bells out of the six in India lost the WiFi connection all of a sudden. I am told that I cannot reset this or choose the WiFi unless I am physically there. Now I have two expensive Ring Door Bells giving NO alerts for a month UNTIL I fly to India to physically reset. Customers should be very careful about this drawback as the whole purpose of alerts is lost due to this inability to reset and connect to WiFi if connection is lost.

title: Software and compatibility issues
body: After an hour on the phone with their (very helpful) tech support I still couldn't get this device working properly — no live view (which is what I specifically bought it for).The Android app is supposedly compatible with my version of Android but even after the tech updated the doorbell's firmware we couldn't get live view working. Even using the web browser interface on my desktop computer wouldn't get live view. Then the tech discovered their web interface isn't compatible with Windows 7. First of all, developers need to take backward compatibility into account — there are still millions of computers running Windows 7 — but more importantly, the PURPOSE of using a web interface instead of a standalone application is to eliminate operating systems compatibility issues!(EDIT: Upon reflection, I'm guessing the software designers relied on a Windows 10 API that's not in Windows 7 and 8 rather than writing all their own code in the web interface. This would save them money, of course, but it also negates the raison d'etre of a web interface, elimination of OS compatibility issues. The really sad part is that whoever on the design team thought of this probably got a raise rather than a slap on the wrist for doing so.)Another quirk is that the video signal is always sent through the internet, even when you're viewing (or trying to view, in my case) from within your own LAN. Obviously, you need to send video over the net to view when you're away from home and using your phone to connect, but there's no reason to do so when the camera and the viewing device are on the same local network. As a result you can't see the view from the doorbell camera when your internet connection is down, even if you have power and your doorbell and viewer are both connected to the same router! Dumb, dumb, dumb. But it's probably another example of doing what's easiest for the people who write the code rather than doing what's right for the people who buy the product.Also, I would note that their Android app doesn't recognize device orientation (yes, we're in the year 2018!), so it was very awkward to use on my Android tablet on its desktop stand, which is of course horizontal orientation: Everything was sideways.In short, a promising piece of hardware let down by cutting corners on software.Returned for refund.

title: Motion Detection = Fail, Doorbell = Fail,Security Cam = Fail
body: Wait for future versions... not ready for release yet.I really wanted to love this product. I hoped it would capture visitors, show me when the mailman came, display when boxes were left, operate as a doorbell and serve as a security camera.Wrong on every count...There is such a delay - after 30 days it has never captured anyone other than myself on video. The motion sensor has such a HUGE delay it never even captures people leaving. Just nothing. The doorbell itself has a delay the first time it is pressed and people jut leave because nothing happens. Pressing a second or third time sometimes works.It does a good job of detecting large trucks that drive by and that is about all. But when they come to the door the mailman, UPS guy, Fed Ex Guy are all invisible to the Ring.Security camera? Nope. You cannot activate this camera to just watch - so if you miss the alert notification in the required time it is over. There is no way for you to initiate the video or conversation.Customer service was rough, they insisted it was my slow internet. I had WiFi at the Ring location of 22mbps (15 up). Their website reads it only requires 1 mbps needed.Then customer service said I was too far from the WiFi, but the Ring was only 5 feet away? It was the closest device on our WiFi. They mailed me an extender, but that is for gaining coverage in dead zones and using one did nothing at all to improve performance.So they finally relented and sent me a replacement. It has been 2 weeks with the new unit and it is exactly the same as the first - useless!Customer service will tell you it is your fault for not trying enough settings. Then they will tell you it is your Internets fault for being too slowThen they will tell you your Ring is too far from your WiFi.Requires 1 mbps = my Ring location getting 22mbpsRequire withing 30 feet of WiFi= my Ring about 5 feet from WiFiTried all motion settings - areas, distances, frequenciesTried adding a internet extender on advice of RingAs I said - I wanted to love it, but it just does not work.Pros:When someone does ring the bell twice and I happen to get the notification in time, the camera and the sound are surprisingly nice.

title: Half-baked first-gen product that needs a lot of work UPDATED 10/30/2019
body: The Ring doorbell simply doesn't meet expectations.Edit 10/30/2019: I am updating this again as I've had extensive experience with the product both with and without their subscription. I will be leaving the rest of the review mostly as it is but re-editing it for the sake of brevity.The good: Live view works fine. No audio feedback issues anymore.The bad:1. Motion detection does not, and apparently never will, work properly. You can set the zones as low as possible, but as long as motion detection is on, it's still going to catch cars 20+ feet away as they drive by. Oddly enough, it doesn't catch ALL of them, but enough to make the feature useless. (Note: my initial review referred to it as proximity detection, since this is actually what it does, it doesn't detect motion, but the app refers to it as "motion settings.")2. Somehow, the oversensitive motion detection completely misses people walking up to the door. I've had many videos of delivery drivers walking away from the house. I've also had people approach the house, knock, and leave without the Ring ever detecting anything.3. Battery life if you're not connected to house wiring is abysmal, partly because of the motion nonsense. If you don't subscribe to the service, you might as well turn motion sensing off, because you're going to disable to motion alerts on your phone anyway (there's way too many cars driving by for me to be alerted every time it happens when I'm not home). With motion detection on, expect about 3 months of battery life before you have to pull the thing down and recharge it again.4. It takes WAY too long for the app to connect to the Ring and vice versa. It's far worse going into Live view, where I try to turn the doorbell camera on and wait over 5 seconds just hoping that it'll eventually connect. Sometimes it won't even do that even though both devices are on my strong, fast Wifi network.
