The wifi extender is just as far away from the doorbell as the regular router, so I don't think it was a connection issue originally. Updating to four stars because I like the doorbell, but I wouldn't rate it five stars because we had so much trouble setting it up.https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0195Y0A42/ref=ppx_yo_dt_b_asin_title_o01_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1This was my original review.I have gone to Ring support (its a bot that takes forever to respond and isn't helpful), searched the FAQ on the Ring website, and posted to the Ring subreddit and no one has helped me. I think their refusal to answer my question on the subreddit (since they respond to other questions) is an implicit admission this device won't work on Spectrum in Southern California.Our ring doorbell is set up and the thumbnail image periodically updates so I know its working (for example, in the morning thumbnail I see my car. After I leave for work I can open the app and the thumbnail image has changed to something like "2m ago" and my car is no longer in the driveway in the thumbnail image).However, whenever I try to access an event video, the video starts but the screen is completely black. So the seconds will count forward in the video player, but we never seen an actual image.When I click "Tap to go live" it says "activating device..." for a long time and then it times out and says "live view ended" without ever showing us any video.We checked our router settings and its set up for the WPA2 that Ring says it supports (not the one it doesn't). On the Ring website there are suggestions for setting up a DMZ but neither my husband nor I are particularly tech-savvy and we could not figure out how to do this at all. We also thought about setting up a guest internet that doesn't have a firewall but we also couldn't figure out how to do that either.This is very frustrating. I also bought a whole Ring Alarm security system but I think I am returning it for SimpliSafe. If the Ring support team can't even attempt to help me with the doorbell, I have doubts about their alarm systems too. This is such a shame, I just want my house to be protected.

title: Don't have to answer the door to creepers and people selling stuff anymore!
body: A dude followed me while I was walking home the other day which creeped me out enough to run right out and buy one of these. Because I cant see who's at the door unless I go answer it. This device is exactly what I need to give me the option to look and see who's out there without them seeing me back. Also i can talk to them without answering the door, and I can get notifications even when I'm not home.I wired it myself and since I'm not all that DIY i'm pretty proud of that. Everything you need including instructions is in the box.I had to turn off the motion sensor because my house is small and my neighbors are close so even at the lowest setting it was giving me notifications when the garbage truck went by and when my neighbor pulled out of her driveway, but for a more spread-out neighborhood I bet that feature is really handy.I like knowing when a package has been left on my porch when I'm not home. My only other problems are the extra fee for saving the videos it captures. its only $30 a year but extra fees suck imho. It's not a dealbreaker, but I always feel like when I shell out so much $ for a product that it should be fully functional for the price.There does seem to be some lag time between a person pressing the bell and me being able to get the phone, pull up the alert and respond. It all just depends how long the person stands there. The package guys are long gone by the time I'm able to get on the intercom and say thanks. They just ring the bell and leave. In fact, that happens when I answer the door too.The dumbest criticism you will read about this product is that the doorbell, being doorbell height, generates images from a terribly unflattering angle so don't expect your visitors to look cute when you see them on your phone waiting at your door.The company was helpful when I used the site's live customer service because my wired doorbell chime didnt work. I eventually decided buying a chime for $30 was worth not having to troubleshoot my very old doorbell wiring. I literally plugged the chime in and it worked just like that.I believe they intend on continuing to improve this doorbell in the future and I'm sure it'll turn out to be really great one day. I wish you could just turn it on when you wanted to and look outside. I feel like I'd prefer that to the motion sensor because I get too many alerts. If they fine tune the motion sensor and let you turn it on when you feel like it, i'd probably give this 5 stars.

title: I knew it wouldn't be perfect but given the number of positive reviews
body: I was looking into a motion activated outdoor camera and a doorbell that I can connect to my wifi network. I did my research and based on many write-ups and forums, I chose Ring. From many reviews, I knew it wouldn't be perfect but given the number of positive reviews, I wanted to give it a try.Out of the box, it was a very straight forward set-up. I installed my Ring and connected it to 2 Chimes in my home. In initial tests, it was hit or miss and it didn't work reliably enough for me, but the signal strength where Ring was installed wasn't very strong with my front door closed, so I wound up upgrading my router to one that covered more distance. That made a significant difference and given my experience with the device was far better and more consistent, I no longer have reservations about the product.I still do have problems connecting to answer calls over public wifi or poorly connected areas. And anyone at the door doesn't bother to continue ringing the bell or stick around long enough for me to find a better connection. So when I'm able to view the recording, I see the full activity of the individual approaching the door and eventually leaving. At least I know my Ring is working fine with the motion sensor capturing the entire activity rather than kicking in late and getting the back of the head of the person leaving, which some have experienced.I know that Ring will soon get an on-demand feature which will allow us to connect to the device any time. I think when that becomes available, it should address the shortfall of most of my missed calls - that's if that person at the door would linger around long enough for me to get a better connection.Overall, I'm very satisfied with the product and plan to add to my Ring and Chimes with their Stick Up Cam when it becomes available. Since I was considering a wifi security camera to cover the sides and back of my home anyway, if their new camera works fine, it should help keep my set-up simple without compromising the security that I'd need.On a final note, and one very important, I should mention their customer support has been excellent throughout my trial period. I've certainly have had to reach out to them several times over Live Chat and someone has always been quickly available to provide assistance. I think the ability to reach out and connect with customer support easily for help (or vent) during setup and troubleshooting can help become a deciding factor whether or not to keep a product!

title: Great with apps, Camera could be better + Motion Challenges
body: Overall we LOVE the Ring Doorbell. We did have a Pro installer come out to hook this up to our internal door bell (we have 3 hard wired chimes) so that certainly made things easier. The app also works great on our PC + Smartphones (iPhone 7 + Samsung S8+) as well our our tablets (iPad + Asus Zen Pad). Overall we are pleased with those aspects. On to the challenges:1) The 720P video camera could be better. We don't have an issue with low light b/c we have a dusk-to-dawn light AND a security camera on our front porch. However, the 720P wide angle circle lens could be better and higher resolution. Even with that limitation we can still tell WHO is at the front door so not a huge issue but quality is definitely NOT what we expected based on the online videos we have viewed (and yes our high-end Asus RT-AC3200 outer is <15 feet from Ring so we have excellent signal strength).2) Motion detection is a bigger issue for us. We live on a cul-de-sac next to a park so we get a ton of foot traffic. The dog walkers are NOT a problem but the car traffic is. Despite scaling the main zones 1-5 back to about 10 feet (our house is about 30ish feet from the sidewalk) we still get triggers when big trucks go by and sometimes even SUVs and hybrid SUVs. I don't want to turn off those zones but we are getting about 10 false motions daily and it gets worse when the sun is out and we get a reflection off the side of a truck. We are tempted to turn off zones 1-5 and only keep the close-in zone 6 on but that feels like it would cripple the motion detection. We definitely want the motion to trigger with someone in our front yard (and yesterday with zones 1-5 scaled back some kid ran pretty close to our front door and motion did not trigger). So, overall I am disappointed with my phone ringing at least 10 times daily with false motion triggers which causes me to look at the video only to see another car rolling by.We hear the the Ring Pro fixes the motion and video problem (now 1080p) but we don't want to return this one to Costco (even though we could) and then spend more money direct on Ring.com (Costco doesn't carry the Pro as far as I can tell) to get the Pro version PLUS have the installer come back out (we read that the Pro version requires you to put an an adapter directly on your wired door bell). Overall, we are happy with Ring but until there is a way to make motion better on the non Pro version I can only give this 4 out of 5 stars.

title: Improvement in software on the way to allow remote initiated contact with device.
body: I had this installed by my alarm company which uses products that communicate with cellular signal, not wifi. That requires 2 separate apps. I wish I could monitor both from a single app. I really like the fact that I know when someone is approaching the house. I have it set to a 5 ft. radius, but it detects all the way out to the street. I get alerts not just from cars or people in my driveway or front door, but the garbage pickup as well. It doesn't detect regular cars beyond the 5 ft. radius, but it does react to large trucks passing my house at the street, 30 feet away. There have been several reviews which have pointed out some shortcomings/desired improvements. It would be nice to be able to initiate a contact with the device, instead of depending on the device to initiate contact (like I can with my Samsung Smartcam which allows viewing and two-way communication anytime. And recording on an SD card, if I choose.) This would relieve the criticism that you are "locked out" if you don't respond in time to a notification. And you are dependent on the cloud. Also, it would be nice if you could differentiate in settings between the door bell ringing and merely detecting motion in front of the doorbell. In other words, if I wanted to be notified if someone rings my doorbell in the middle of the night, I could allow that notification to come through on my iPhone at night, like I allow a phone call to come through, but block email and text notification.I like this device and it is certainly a good alternative to having just a doorbell, particularly if you go out of town or are away from home a great deal. I am sure they will improve the product based on customer feedback.Adding to my original review: I was recently out of town and was able to speak to a repair person at my front door, could allow them access remotely through my security system (not integrated with Ring, unfortunately.) Sound quality and picture quality in both directions were excellent. I just heard from the company that they will be adding the ability to initiate connection with the device, instead of depending on an alert to allow access. This should address the problem that you can be "locked out" if you don't respond to an alert or doorbell ring quickly enough. It will allow you to survey the view remotely at any time. Now if they would speak to Alarm.com and allow integration with that security software, I'd be the most satisfied customer.

title: Cool product that is ALMOST a killer solution!
body: This is a killer concept, but needs a few things refined. I purchased this locally (at a large warehouse club "C") because they included a chime module as well. I removed my existing doorbell button, and hardwired the Ring into place. When someone pushes the Ring button, my normal doorbell sounds, and I get an alert on my iPhone (2 alerts, actually, but I will get to that). I can swipe the alert, and the app opens, and within a few seconds I can see a live view from the Ring. The same happens for motion alerts. Cool! A couple 'quirks'.1. There is often a delay with the sound starting after the video. I am 100% sure it isn't my wifi (I am an IT engineer - my network-fu is good in that regard) - it could be some lag on the app or the phone hardware, but I suspect it is some delay of writing to the cloud and then from their servers to my phone.2. There is no live view: I can only view the camera's video when a motion or ring has occurred. I understand they are working on rolling out live-view to more people with hardwired, but there is no timeline (and I am concerned that they are stretching resources as they are already taking orders for the next version, the 'Pro'). Fixing these 2 (admittedly) small issues would make this a killer product.On another note, I have played around with a few home-automation hardware solutions. I was able to add the Ring to the Wink Hub (which I use with 2 thermostats, multiple LED lights, cameras and some other toys). The integration is good: I get a 'Wink' alert the same time as the 'Ring' alert on my phone. The Wink alert just opens the Wink app with a link to open the Ring app: Not really a useful integration at this point, but wanted to note that as well.The last thing I was bothered by was I posted a brief review pointing out these small issues on Ring's Facebook page. Instead of a reply or a discussion, Ring just deleted the post. I found that very odd, and more than anything else made me question dealing with the company: Most startups would welcome feedback and a discussion - but they don't seem to be one of those companies, which is disappointing.I plan to test this for another week or so (I have had it up for a couple weeks already - including while we were out of town for a week). If they can't get the live view and the audio delay sorted I may return this and try another solution (or wait until they get the next version out?).

title: Doorbell rant
body: [[VIDEOID:90c9ebf18fb14fb824b1b570fbaaec9b]] This was one of the most disappointing ring products ever purchased. No it's not the wifi signal that customer service tries to tell you being the problem cause it ring doorbell is literally in a room next to the doorbell with Xfinity's gigabit service and yet unbelievably all ring products don't even support 5 ghz signal they can only accept 2.4 ghz (which can mean a slower streaming experience) however processing power must be taken into account.I have all 3 Ring products, the Ring Floodlight, the Ring stick up cam (solar power) and of course the disappointing Ring Doorbell. Of all three, the Floodlight is the best, it captures motion with almost no delay and excellent night vision and sound.The Ring stick up cam comes in second with less options for motion zone controls and some delay in motion detection of about 5-10 seconds which still is hardly acceptable but tolerable.Last the Doorbell, the bottleneck. There is so much delay that items can be stolen easily within seconds. At times I could walk up to the door and it doesn't notify me until 15 seconds later and this is on a super high speed network with 50 mbps download and 40 mbps upload and it could be faster if it were capable of being compatible with 5ghz at 500 mbps to 1ghz download BUT then I guess they should have thought about that limit and stress a micro processor instead.I'm just down cause I like the chime and the inconspicuous look that anyone would be better off buying simply only the floodlights in front instead of this doorbell. I should have just done that.***update***Ring tech support reached out right away and provided an ear full of my experience then provided a Chime Pro just to see if it improved. I received the Ring Chime Pro in a few days and the results were that it's NOT the weak internet signal but that is was a weak RSSI signal. Due to the product's inability to transmit a strong static signal through a wall/walls it results in a "weak signal" however even though the Ring Doorbell still lacks that part, the improved RSSI from 70 down to 60 makes a good difference by not losing signal.I feel as though many of the products still need to be refined in hardware, firmware, and software, it still gives me peace of mind knowing what's happening outside my door so thank you tech support for your persistence in effort to accommodate.

title: Here's your piece of mind!
body: The concept of the Ring is everything I've been looking for in a home camera device! Using it for nearly a month, my household can't seem to live without it since it gives us that piece of mind when we're away, knowing that we get alerted every time someone is at the front.I have mine installed on the actual front door which is pretty cool in that it allows me to see my entire entry way when the door is open (which stops against the wall), giving a full 180 degree view of both inside and outside. Unfortunately mine isn't connected to my existing doorbell on the side, which would be the better option as I would've preferred hearing my actual doorbell ring and have it powered by that. Even with the camera being wide still, I wanted it to face my entry straight on instead of off to the side.A few issues to state though (1 star off):-The motion sensor still isn't the best. I've had mine set at different ranges but it will mostly only go off when you're already up close to the unit instead of approaching it from a distance. When my phone alerts me, the person is either already walking away or has been standing there for a good time.-There is lag time from when you press the button to getting an alert. Kind of like the issue above, if someone were impatient and couldn't wait for an answer via the app, they would walk and it would be too late to give them the impression you were home.-No live viewing.....yet (it would definitely kill the battery so I need to find a way to hardwire it)I've contacted customer service with all these known issues, in which they stated that based on user feedback, they are continuing to work on these and will be improving the device. Their responses have been pretty quick either on the phone with a live person or via email which is definitely a plus for this new company and product.The app is nice and simple. I love how it gives you the option to download any of the videos onto your phone/tablet.I will continue using this unless a better product comes out to be more superior/advanced, but overall I am satisfied with what we have so far. I do CCTV work for a living, but surprisingly I don't have a full camera system installed yet. When that time comes, this will still be a much needed addition to a full system down the road since it has more to offer than regular cameras alone.

title: Incredibly Innovative, Fantastic Functionality, Amazing Security!
body: I have owned the Ring Video Doorbell for a year and a half, and let me tell you, it has been amazing in so many ways. I did NOT purchase the Ring Video Doorbell on Amazon, but rather at Home Depot because at the time, I wanted the ease of returning it in case it didn't deliver what I needed (it was just too good to be true!), but clearly this has not been the case. I took the time to write this review after owning it during these 1.5 years and purposefully waited until I could honestly attest to its functionality. Apparently, there are similar products from what I have seen, but the common complaints seem to be around the lack of continued innovation and on-going customer support for those "competing" products. Don't fall into the trap of purchasing a similar (and I might add, inferior) product - trust what you read by reviewers here who take the time to write about their experiences.Why I purchased the camera: We currently live in a building with 3 apartment homes located in an area with a lot of foot traffic. When we moved here, we certainly didn't realize this, and definitely didn't realize that 1 block east and beyond was, how shall we say, questionable, as is 2 blocks north and beyond. You never notice this if you always drive west or south!! At any rate, I have attached a couple of pictures to demonstrate where I have placed my Ring video doorbell, which is in the foyer, between the external door at the street and the internal door that is the entry to 3 apartment homes. We clearly took extra steps to avoid theft of our new doorbell, but clearly this is not necessary with the way they have designed it (but we just wanted to be sure!).That said, I purchased the Ring doorbell for several reasons. One, this old historic building doesn't have a doorbell nor an intercom that works, and two, for a bit of security since the landlord refused to install any sort of camera. To her point, who would manage the camera, and where would the hard drive be stored? No matter - the Ring doorbell is a superior solution for what I needed it for - and it's mine to take with me anywhere I want to. What prompted the immediate purchase was one day I went downstairs to take the dogs out, and noticed there were packages downstairs that were opened and the contents were gone!! I felt nauseated, and although they weren't my packages, I was trying to imagine how this occurred. Another reason was that often times items being delivered by subcontractors for Amazon and were often "attempted", but I had no proof they were being attempted - and I wanted proof!I absolutely love the look of the camera - it is very sleek and simply looks like a chime. We originally put up a sign that there was a camera long before we placed the Ring doorbell there, but quickly figured out that this was simply not going to be a deterrent after the previous neighbor's packages were stolen. Once we installed the camera, we left the sign there, and still, people never believe it is a camera, including the upstairs neighbor's brother, who kept swearing he wasn't leaving the downstairs door open when I could clearly see that he was! I just wanted to scream, "Do you see the sign?! There really IS a camera there in the foyer!" It's not like I was trying to hide the fact that there is a camera (given it is clearly "advertised"!), but perhaps that's not always a bad thing either. However, I don't use the camera to "spy" on my neighbors' comings and goings, but I DO use it when I am expecting a package, or I am curious how pizza slices were abandoned on the foyer floor and by whom.What this video doorbell has helped me achieve:- This video doorbell has helped me instruct the delivery guy for Amazon where he can safely leave my package when I am not at home to receive it.- It has enabled me to see who comes in the foyer and tries to get into the second door that provides access to all three apartment homes.- It gives me a heads up when the landlord is coming into the building, as she frequently likes to come knocking on doors unannounced, and frequently just walks into one's apartment when she feels like it. I also know that she lied when she said she brought the HVAC guy into the review the shoddy HVAC system because the camera shows she entered the building alone. Of course, I never told her I knew this.- It enabled me to see who threw pizza on the floor under the mailboxes and just left it there. I literally thought it was a homeless person since they often leave trash outside the door on the steps. However, upon further review, the camera revealed it was the upstairs neighbor who stood in front of the camera and chowed down on the pizza for 10 minutes, and threw the rest of the floor of the foyer and just left it there atop a brown paper bag. (And no, I never told the upstairs neighbor that I knew it was her boyfriend.)- It has also helped me provide valuable feedback to Amazon for the delivery of my packages, such as when an item was said to be delivered, yet no one came within 30 feet of my camera, which happened twice. This has also helped with those "attempted" package deliveries. I am sure my file now has a "BEWARE" notation on it that says "please don't lie to this customer - somehow she always knows!"- It also gives me the ability to know when someone decides to use the steps to drink his beer or smoke his cigarette, which wafts up to the windows and into the house. I walk downstairs and kindly inform them that they are "tripping" the motion detector on my camera, and they apologize and move along after promising they will actually put their trash in the trash receptacle rather than leave it on the steps.- It also captured the theft of the neighbor's package 2 days prior to Christmas this last year - it was very surreal to see it on video. Even better was to be able to download it and provide it to the neighbors in case they wanted to press charges.I can unequivocally say that initially I truly had to understand how the video doorbell worked, and had I written a review in the beginning (September 2015), I might have given it 3 stars. However, it isn't exactly fair to provide an average rating when the understanding of how it actually works is average, or below average. I also had to adjust my expectations for its capabilities and not expect it to do things it was not yet designed to do. I can honestly say that any time I have had a question, the Ring team has been responsive and has taken the time to help, such as when the camera continuously had a poor Wi-Fi connection, and rendered poor images. Turns out they were actually right!What is so amazing about this company is that they continuously update their product and their app (both iOS and Android). Having purchased this in September 2015 from Home Depot, I have noticed the small but consistent changes to the app and the firmware. For example, one year ago, if someone provoked the motion sensor and the camera began recording, it would take maybe 20-30 seconds to finish the recording, save it and send it to my phone, meaning that any activity occurring during that lapse of time would not be recorded - at least not on the settings I was using (I was not using continuous motion at the time for fear it would deplete the battery too quickly). I mentioned this to their team and was informed they were working on this - and indeed they did. Currently, the time lapse between being concluded and sent to my phone is a matter of maybe less than 10 seconds (I don't know exactly because I have not timed it), and this is significantly better than before. This is very important because it apparently only takes a few seconds for someone to walk into that foyer behind the UPS guy and steal the package he just delivered, which can happen before the sensor is re-activated. To ensure the video sort of continuously records constant motion, I put the Smart Alert on "frequent". Perhaps it uses the battery a little bit more, but that's what I needed. I have even noticed that prior to changing those settings, I generally charged the battery about once a month because we had so many events (i.e., neighbors, foot traffic, cars, etc.), but the Ring team seems to have somehow managed to extend the battery life as well (or the life between charges). The settings can easily be adjusted to fit individual need, so you have to play around with the settings to figure out what is best for you.The settings I use that have worked well for me:- Placing the motion settings at 30 feet for all zones is what has worked for me, and I don't care that I get cars, busses and passersby because I don't have to neurotically check the camera every time it goes off.- I have motion alerts set to 24/7.- I have Smart Alerts at "frequent", although I used to use "standard" for about the first year. I have found that with the traffic at the foyer, it enables me to see an action that was started and has continued without the recording being automatically cut off and rendered at approximately 45 seconds. Perhaps this causes me to charge the doorbell 5 days earlier than I did with the "standard" setting, but no matter. I am sure this will be improved soon, as well, if it hasn't been already!- I have both ring alerts and motion alerts set to "on".- Apparently there is a new setting that is a "live view" that I just saw 5 minutes ago, so I will have to check that out. It also appears that you can log into your account and see recordings from your desktop, but I am not clear when this became a feature. I see that you can download videos to your laptop, which is fantastic, email a video, share it with the Ring team, and play and delete videos. I think folks have been asking for this for a while, and the Ring team is clearly listening!!In terms of their app, it has been fabulous. In the past, if I felt like something was not going right, the first thing I learned to check was whether or not a new update was available, and this was usually the fix. Now I don't have to check because I am notified right in the app that a new update is available! I have also noticed that I would previously get an email when it was time to charge the battery once it was at 10% threshold, and now I get notified right on my phone, which is 1000% better because an email might just get buried! Also, I just noticed that you can set a schedule for motion detection if that interests you. I always want to know who is nearing that external door at any given time, so I don't use this feature, but I can see how this can be useful.I hope that by sharing all the ways this video doorbell has helped me will prove to be useful to many folks because it took me a very long time to write this, but the product was worth my time!! Cheers!

title: DO WHAT I DID... and you will have a fantastic Ring video doorbell.. love it :))
body: [[VIDEOID:4d48d4aea85b3fb4061e9f0e456d5ac]] Had my Ring doorbell for 2 months now bought it from ebay.com for the same price as on Amazon but because I had to use my e-bucks before it expire, as usual i only write reviews for the product I buy at least a month or two after i used them especially electronic items so that I can have all the time to tweet & test it out, the failed product I would returned it within the time allowed and that usually within 2 weeks max, for this Ring doorbell i'm glad that I gave it a try although I had test another off brand video doorbell 1/2 the cost bought from ebay, i end up returning it realizing i get what i paid for :(For awhile i was mislead by reading too many mixed reviews, more good than bad but the bad reviews make people wonder and so was i! but when a product get thousands of reviews there always will have few bad as well, that happened in any products on the market, I use to spent hours sipping through one review after another before i even order it, more often it get me confused with mixed thinking, not worth my time, now with thousands of reviews with a four stars? I'll give it a try, many bad & neutral reviews I find with this Ring doorbell are neither from other product competitor that trying to destroy this great invention product, or from a frustrated buyer/user that didn't give enough time to figure out why the product is not working, I also find many bad reviews posted just days after they received the product, most complaints I find are video is choppy, blocky, does not record, did not alert, etc. amazingly it happened to me the same way when I first it setup and installed the unit, I spent a few days diagnosed the unit, tested, test and test again to the end i find it's not the product itself but it's all about HAVING A GOOD WIFI NEAR THE FRONT DOOR.. This will eliminate all the complaints you see in 90% of those reviews.My Ring doorbell now works smoothly 99% of the time, it alert my iPhone, my wife and my two kids phones every time, no choppy video, no blocky cookie or what ever people called and I love it so far :))Remember now and day all the new products invented or are in the pipeline such as video cameras, alarm system heck even light bulbs will be a smart product same as this Ring Doorbell, they all required Wifi, for the Ring doorbell, it need strong wifi to record and send video to the cloud and to your smartphone, without wifi all smart product will be dumb even that product cost you thousands $$.HERE'S HOW I CONFIGURED & SETUP MY Ring Doobell..My main Comcast modem/wireless router sits in my home office far back corner of the house, the wifi is very strong with a blast service from Comcast tested at 50+ Mbps but still provide week signal to the front porch with the front door closed, I bought a little Netgear wifi extender and plug it into the outlet in my front living room about 10 feet away from the front door (see photo), I configured the Ring doorbell to run the wifi off from the Netgear unit and Whola! works great ever since. it's been 2 month now and I love how it alert me every time.2 days ago I had an appointment with the appraisal somehow he decided to show up 20 mins early while I was sweeping leaves in my backyard in prepare for the Kodak moment, he rang the doorbell and i answered the door from my backyard, it took awhile for me to head to the door as you can see in the video I posted, i had to hang up the call before i open the door to avoid confrontation haha.. I have so many videos that i like to post so that you can see how smooth the recording and the sound is but Amazon only allowed me to post just one video per review, video quality is about 720P in HD compared that to my little 10P VGA door peek hole :), and the two way intercom is amazingly clear coming out of this small unit. i took out my old doorbell and hardwired my Ring doorbell to the existing 2 doorbell wires, mounted the unit to the side stucco wall facing the other wall, I find the motion senor is very sensitive, I set mine to the lowest setting of 5 feet and still once a week the motion alert do catch a recycling truck passing by my house and sent an alert to my phone, I really mind but actually like the way it is because I don't get too many visitor therefore the recycling truck that pass by once a week provide me an auto system test that let me know the doorbell is working, on another day there was a gentlemen walk up to my front door look at the Ring doorbell and all the sudden he backs away down the steps and eventually took off without ringing the bell, I get the motion alert to my iphone but than he walks away, I don't know what his intention was but I realized people will not mess with you if they see the video camera looking straight at them, i mean the Ring doorbell is right in their face haha!Here's what I know about this Ring doorbell..MOTION & RING ALERTIf motion is detected or the doorbell rings and you deny the call the recording will last 40 second and stop recording, during the 40 seconds you may deny or accept the call which i find way enough time to watch live video and still have time to think about deny or answer the call, while watching the live feed the Ring app let me zoom in for a full screen view, has speaker & microphone on/off function, if the person still in range of the motion after the first alert recording is done, it will trigger another motion alert to the phone, if you answer to the motion or ring alert, the recording will continue until you hang up the call, the motion sensor is very sensitive which is good but I recommend to set it at the lowest setting if you going to mount your doorbell facing the street, you can always turn off the motion alert after all and it still do the recording without alerting you, or turn off the motion range completely and it won’t detect any motion.3-5 SECONDS DELAYEDI find the video recording has a 3 seconds delayed and the sound feed is about 5 seconds , i don't think it effect the normal use of the doorbell, most UPS guys drop the package, ring the door and immediately walk away to get into their truck for the next stop, so even without the delay they not going to wait for you to answer the door, we all know how our delivery guy look like, not a big deal, the thing i'm worry about is the stranger or worse someone try to kick my door down, but again those people usually stay and wait to see if someone answer the door, sometime they tend to stay longer and ring the bell many times over just to make sure no one is home, so for me I have no worry about the seconds delay, it will record!HARDWIRE INSTALLATIONI hardwired my unit, never have issue with the battery running out, you still need to fully charge the battery first before installation because the low voltage wire from the old doorbell only trickle charge the battery, without the battery that low voltage wire will not provide enough power to do video recording and uploading etc.. as long you hardwired it you will never have to worry the battery will run out unless you have 50 visitors in a day for couple days in a row, The unit seems very well built and sturdy, I mounted mine and also try to yank it from the wall, it’s not easy, with the screws provided it is locked in.. Just becareful when installing the 2 little screws on the bottom of the unit, I stripped my screws the first time by using the provided screw bit with my electric drill, big mistake, I call customer service at Ring and they immediately sent me 4 new screws and the bits, Ring customer service is excellent and fast understanding of the product and the needs of the customerWHAT IF SOME ONE GRAB THE DOORBELL AND TAKES OFF WITH IT..The company have a life time replacement if your unit get stolen, i'm sure they won't have to replace much due to stolen because in my opinion and experience from the unknown visitor the other day, people tent to face away from the camera, or even immediately walk away, why risk having your face recorded, heck even my cousin and visitors that i knew still ring the bell and kinda look away, everyone is shy of the camera, but a free lifetime replacement is a plus..HOW TO ACCESS ALL THE RECORDED VIDEOAll the recorded video are stored in the Ring cloud server instead of local usb which is what I preferred, this way no matter what happen even if the unit get stolen at least I know I still have the videos, all my cameras inside the home are using cloud storage even though they have the onboard usb slot, with Ring doorbell you can access the recorded videos straight from your phone or on your desktop by logging into your ring.com account, Ring offered $3/month for cloud storage or pay $30/year it come to be $2.50/month after the initial 30 day free trail, I think it's very low cost compared to what I have to pay for cloud storage with my dropcam.My conclusion..I think Ring doorbell is a great invention and well thought out, although I bought my unit from ebay not direct from Ring but they still provide lifetime replacement if it get stolen, most of our friends & relatives call before they come by the house, therefore every time the door bell ring, we already know it is a stranger, always one of us have to sneak up to the front door leaned out face against the door and look into that little peek hole to find the stranger, a lot of time viewing through that little hole we can't even make out the face only to recognized by the cloth they wear, isn't that scary? Worse.. what if my face was leaning against the door and someone decide to kick down the door, i don't think i even have enough time to say hi to the hardwood floor haha.. but now I'm glad i got the unit installed, i don't have to worry about who's on the other side of my door, there's time all 4 of us from different locations during working hours answered the door all at once with our iphones, I just feel more secure knowing i can protect my family and answer the door anywhere not just from my backyard. I think every family should have this important security device even if you are a renter as well as owner, now every time my doorbell ring or motion alert we all excited to answer the door hahha!! Love this thing, thank you Ring and its team!!I would recommend anyone to try it out, you will probably never go without it again, trust me..

title: Ring DoorBell problems quickly addressed by manufacture reps. (World Class IMO) / full product review.
body: DO NOT LET MY NOTES BELOW GIVE YOU THE WRONG IMPRESSION. INSTALLATION WILL BE SIMPLE FOR MANY OF YOU.IF you do not need the molly mounts, installation is very simple and you should be able to do so in a very few minutes. Still -- I recommend you watch the videos on Ring.comIF you need the mollies to mount it, it becomes how comfortable you are using a drill to your home exterior wall. A drill bit of the correct size is included in the box - no need to sort thru your drill bits and guess which one to use. Just be sure not to drill thru the entire wall from outside to inside. You will only drill a small way in.Find Your In-Home WiFi Network SSID and Password - You will want enable the Wi-Fi connection before mounting .FIRST THING you should do is use the included USB cord and charge the Ring Doorbell like a cell phone. When it arrives, the internal battery will be about half chargedWhile that is charging:Load the Ring App on your cell phone(s) and tablets.Ring doorbell apps are available for both Apple and Android versions.Do so for all the cell phones and tablets you want to have Ring Doorbell apps / use on. This may include family members phones if you so choose.Register on the cell phone app at least once. You will want to register / activate the cell phone app BEFORE mounting the Ring Doorbell on you outside wall.You can give your family members the login information OR after you register and log in, use the app to email your family members the link to have their own log in app link different than yoursIF you use the same log in for all family members, only one registration is needed. IF you use separate registrations, each will need to be set up individually (before mounting is my recommendation).I have the housekeeper on her separate registration. If I take a weekend trip or wind up in the hospital, I can, of course, deal with it myself ...or have her do so.Read the instructions - watch the videos on Ring.comReview everything in the box - Keep everything in the box until you use it - keep the provided tools and screws in the box. You may need them again at a future date.Watch the videos on Ring.com to avoid the mistakes I made. - I repeat this recommendation due to the assumptions I made causing me problems I made a few wrong assumptions .IF you use a power screwdriver, be slow and gentle with it.My biggest mistake was using my power screwdriver on the locking screws.Whatever you do -- be very gentle and only use a manual screwdriver on the locking screws.IF you use the battery and not hard wire it, you will have to remove the lock screws and recharge the Ring Doorbell at some future date. It can only be recharged by connecting the USB connection on the back of the Ring Doorbell. After you do so, simply replace your fully charged Ring Doorbell (same unit) on the wall mount and gently return the lock screws in place..==An additional item is available for additional cost - While not required, you may want a in Ring chime (additional optional item) in places that you cannot hear the current doorbell or if you decide not to hardwire it. Multiple Ring Chimes may be set up for the same Ring Doorbell.My original review followsUpdate: I switched on the motion detection. I had 6 false alarms (nothing there) in less than two hours. The motion detection at a very modest setting ate 5 per cent of the internal battery in that time.While I had the motion detection turned on, Fedex made a delivery. Motion detection failed to pick up the driver. I only got a view of the driver leaving on the video from when he rang the doorbell.[[ASIN:B00N2ZDXW2 Ring Wi-Fi Enabled Video Doorbell]][[ASIN:B011MYEMKQ Ring Wi-Fi Enabled Chime]]I purchased my Ring device via a Amazon linked merchant for a slightly reduced price. Other than a slightly delayed shipping time and a modest shopping fee, it is the same device shipped new in the box. This choice was less net cost compared to ordering directly from the Ring company or Amazon Prime.Instructions included have room for improvement. I called the customer service number provided for additional verbal help. The phone rep was polite, calm and quite knowledgeable. He did not hesitate to admit he was unsure of a couple of things. After a very short hold, he returned with a answer.Strong Recommendation - If you fell the need to call Ring customer Service for any reason, I found it very helpful to have my PC opened to the Ring website. The Customer Service rep directed me to a couple of links that were much better than the included instructions.( I have personal work experience doing the same job for a different company I consider this company to have a top notch customer service and rep support system in place.) For some reason, the device was missing the locking screws (more later). Ring Customer Service was fairly prompt to answer the telephone and order out the missing locking screws.My only negative Customer Service concern was an Email notice indicating (assuming) the matter was resolved prior to it actually being resolved and automated closure of the Customer Service ticket. As requested in the email notice i replied and the Customer Service ticket was kept open until I was satisfied.I had an installation problem in addition to that. For some reason, the device mounting wall plate (included) was not working with the ring device shipped. Customer Service was easy to work with to order a full replacement Ring device kit. They paid the shipping to me and provide a paid mailer to return the kit I had a problem with.One other Customer Service problem came up. For some reason, there was a problem with the shipping ticket for my replacement unit. They caught the problem and telephoned me. I am unsure what the ticket problem was, but they caught the problem, acted on it and shipped my replacement device promptly - two day US postal service. The return envelope was likewise a two day return envelope. Both shipments were paid by Ring and trackable. Ring Customer Service sent me an email when the replacement was shipped. I made note of the return tracking number before sending the problem kit back.The replacement was a new full kit including fresh mounting screw with mollies and a separate set of wood screws. The locking screws were also in the replacement device.IF you use screws with mollies, you may need to drill holes in your brick or other mounting location. The kit includes the correct size drill bit.It also includes a screw driver with a removable (reverse-able) screw driver head.CAUTION: If you use a power screwdriver, be slow and gentle doing so.The Ring device may be powered by conventional doorbell transformer or the built-in rechargeable battery included. There is a USB cable and plug on the inside wall of the Ring device. The other interior wall pressure sensor is needed to set up your Ring device BEFORE mounting on the exterior wall where you want your Ring Doorbell device to be of use .Instructions should indicate it is a good idea to fully charge the Ring doorbell before installation, BUT did not Both of the Ring devices I received were at 50% charge upon arrival. My read of the current setup does not say anything about charging the battery before installation.I chose to use the internal battery to power it. I have had it installed for a couple of weeks. The Ring App on the phone says the internal battery is still 100 percent after light use.Actual installation is easy.> Charge it> Use the phone app to set it up and register it with Ring.IMO - In house Wi-Fi is best. You will need your inhouse Wi-Fi password to sync The Ring device to your cell phone(s) and OPTIONAL in home ring bell which acts like a regular door bell chime. If the phone is out of power, away from home, left in the car etc.,the optional device will ring like a normal door bell chime lo let anyone home know someone rang the doorbell. Multiple chime units may be purchased if the home is large or multilevel. The internal home doorbell chime is an added OPTIONAL purchase. It is Wi-Fi dependent. Use the phone app to set it up and coordinate it with you new Ring doorbell device.. KEEP the included screwdriver bit handy in case you need to reset the doorbell chime.Positives: doorbell and security camera in one .Easy to set up and, install if thought out before doing the install. Except for the mounting bracket problem, all other install problems were of my own making.The locking screws on the bottom secure the Ring Device in place and avoid someone from running off with it are a MAJOR concern and need redesign. I used the wrong style screw driver on those screws and stripped the screwhead. When the internal battery needs to be recharged, I will have to find a way to remove the damaged locking screws so that I may charge the internal battery. Removal will be required to recharge that battery unless you hard wire the Ring device to power it.I have the replacement screws sent and will be far more careful replacing and re-securing the Ring Doorbell to the mounting bracket after recharging it..This bears repeatingI am ok for now but due to the damaged anti-theft security screws, I can foresee a problem in the future. Anti-theft security screws at the bottom need a better screw head.. I damaged the screw head of the security screws trying to use the screwdriver provided as instructed. It will be a challenge when I need to pull it to charge the battery.Negative: it would be nice if the RING doorbell battery could be recharged either wirelessly or wire connection under the device in such a way the removal of the Ring doorbell would NOT have to be removed. There are portable USB chargers. That connection should have a setting to only charge the battery without enabling the other USB functions.===========Additionally the Ring company offers a Cloud service to store your Ring Doorbell events for a fee.Some customers are offered a chance to join their feedback group and are given the cloud service without fee.

title: Ended my doorbell search (and solved other issues I didn't even know I had...)
body: So here is another one of my fairly lengthy reviews....The Ring is actually one of my favorite new devices, and the only "doorbell/camera/intercom" that does EXACTLY what I needed it to do. I had done research for a while before I saw it advertized. Granted, some people are not in the unique situation that I am in (which I'm about to explain), but I think the details will be helpful regardless.So here's my story. Bear with me....I am a writer, so I'm naturally verbose... I live on a decently sized 7000 sq ft lot, with the main property set back far from the street, probably 200 ft or so. There is another smaller property (that I rent out) just in front of the main house. In front of that is a small office. Each of these buildings get closer to the street. Because everything is so far back from the street, and I essentially have no backyard, we put up a 6ft privacy fence and gate along the street, to enclose the property. We jokingly call it "The Compound". So you get the idea. There are also two large dogs, who not only love to explore and escape when possible, but intimidate strangers and package delivery people who show up to the gate.   So, my problems were multiple. Because of the dogs, no one could open my gate and walk up the pathway to either the main house or the studio apartment to ring a doorbell or deliver a package. Because there are two living spaces with two separate people as well as an office building, it gets confusing as to who lives  where and who is in which building at any given time, so multiple doorbells seemed pointless. Because of the privacy fence, leaving packages without signatures on the street outside the gate (obviously there's no porch or private area) is impracticle, not to mention most carriers won't do it anyway. Then, there's the addional issue of security. If we had doorbells, then we wanted video. We knew that if we were going to have a doorbell, then it needed to be on the front gate, but also transmit wirelessly, ideally to a phone/tablet, but a computer at the very least. There is no electricity that far down the yard by the street, so hard wiring was not an option without a lot of work and money. Not to mention hardwired doorbells (unless you have a pre-existing unit) require step down transformers. So it seemed like we were pretty screwed. We had researched some units, I think Skybell was the name of one, and a couple of others, and while they offered WiFi transmission, they required hard wired electricity.  Then we found Ring. Almost seemed too good to be true, because there were features that we hadn't even thought about that solved additional concerns. Ring HAS A BUILT IN RECHARGEABLE BATTERY. There's a no brainer. Amazing that someone hadn't done this before, but there you go... So naturally we bought it just based on that to give it a try. Obviously it can also be hardwired to an existing or new doorbell plate and all the tools are in the package.  So here's why we love it, and why the extra features are so useful, and I've heard there are more to come. They just released Chime, and I'll discuss that in a second. These points are in no particular order....  A: Ring has a good, high resolution camera (bear in mind, that the further away from your WiFi router you are, the resolution will degrade. That's just science...), the night vision is impressive, and the wide angle of the lens is more than enough to see everything. The microphone is very good, as is the outdoor speaker. Honestly much clearer than expected.  B: The WiFi power is solid. You set up the connection to your network through the Ring app on your phone (more on the app later). It's a very easy process, and the app walks you through it. Because of the massive distance of the yard I described, we did end up having to add a WiFi extender to the office (which was the closest building to the street), to make the camera connection more efficient. Bear this in mind if you're installing far away from your router. Once Ring is connected it shows up as a registered device, and you can add more later if necessary. Now here's the super cool part, and how it affects home security. I now know Ring advertises this as one of it's key features, but to see it in action really sells it. Much like the Belkin WeMo system, once the device is connected to your network, it is then routed through your phone, literally enabling you to answer your doorbell (and see who is there) from anywhere. I have answered the gate from 2000 miles away, asked for a package to be left there, and then had a friend (or roommate) go and pick it up. Nobody knew I wasn't home. Visit Ring's website for videos on this feature.C: Almost a continuation of B, Ring also features customizeable motion sensing. I don't really need to go into detail on this, because everyone will use it differently, but for us it's handy for package delivery when someone doesn't ring the bell (yes, I get a lot of packages....). It would also be useful of course if you suspected that someone was trying to break in or tamper with your stuff, or you have a crazy stalker, which leads us to....D: Cloud recording!! Ring has a cloud recording service. It's fantastic. If you activate it (which I believe is about $3 a month), every time the bell rings or the motion sensor is triggered, it uploads the video to the cloud, and you can review it at any time through the app. So far we haven't had the misfortune of security issues, but it is really handy (and worth it in my opinion) to see who was at your gate if you miss a "call" from the Ring. E: Multiple users. A huge selling point (well we were already sold, but we discovered this later) is that you can add multiple users to the Ring device. My roommate/tenant downloaded the app, set up an account and then I added her to the list. When the gate rings, she is alerted at the same time I am, no matter where we are, and she also has access to all of the cloud recordings and features. This is a great feature if you live in a compound....;) Joke aside, we both travel a lot independantly for business, so having multiple users be able to access the features I've previously outlined, increases security and also allows each of us to take care of things at the house more efficiently.F: The app is very well designed. Simple, easy to use and intuitive. We're all Apple users here, so I've only seen it on iOS, but I have it installed on my phones and iPad and it works great. I actually use old, beat up iPhones and iPads as wall mounted "light switches" for home automation stuff, so my whole house rings when someone is at the gate. I can use any of the devices to see the camera. Also, when you login to your account on any iPhone/iPad, your registered Ring device(s) pop up. Aside from that, the app allows you to customize settings (mainly motion), add devices and look at cloud recordings. You can also monitor battery life if you are using the wireless rechargeable battery. Again, all done very intuitively. I was going to mention this separately, but this seems like a good place instead. While I am happy to have my whole house "ring", my tenant does not want hers to. She works from two phones and an iPad, has the Ring app installed on all of them, but mostly keeps her phone ringers off. As a result she misses a lot of doorbell rings. She also doesn't necessarily, as many people wouldn't, always want/need to get Ring notifications when she is out and knows I'm home. Amazingly, the Ring folks just launched Chime, which is pretty much for people like her. We will be installing one in her place. From what I understand it is essentially a stand alone, almost "old school" doorbell ringer (chime, i guess....), that you wall mount and rings in your house separately from your phone. It is connected through the Ring app and is customizeable (volume etc). I haven't played with it yet, but will soon and review it.G: One last note about the unit itself. It's very sturdy and weather resistant. We live in Central Texas and just went through a bashing from the edge of the hurricane weather. Ring is fine, just as they advertise. Once you mount it, there are two small security screws on the bottom that are designed to make stealing it a pain in the ass. Ring apparently has a replacement program for theft (see H) by the way. All installation tools needed to mount and set up are included in the package. Screws, screwdriver and bits, and any other accessories for hardwiring to electricity (mine had a diode included in the box). There was even a micro USB cable for re-charging the battery, which is very easy to do. Undo security screws with special bit, take Ring off the mount and then plug it in as you would a phone etc... Battery life is apparently 6 months or more before recharge with normal usage. For those of you that care, the packaging it arrives in is very elegant and frustration free.H: Customer service is impeccable. While in an ideal world you should never have to call them, I initially received a unit with a slightly defective battery (it wasn't holding a charge very long). They replaced it immediately (arrived in a couple of days) with virtually no questions asked. Also, the website is informative and answers most questions. If you have to call and the hold time is too long, they'll call you back without you losing your place in line.My only gripe about Ring so far is that there is no Mac OS desktop support/app. I work from my computer a lot. I am a musician, a photographer and a writer, so when knee deep in a project that requires concentration or silence (i.e. phone turned off), it would be nice for a doorbell alert to pop up on the computer screen and a little video window. HOWEVER, I have heard rumors that desktop support is on the horizon.So there you have it. My lengthy review. I hope it helps. Excuse typos and grammar. If Hemingway had written on an iPhone, it would be pure s*** too.....;) That's assuming of course that you like Hemingway in the first place.....

title: My best smart home purchases yet!
body: This doorbell is exactly what I wanted...and more! I bought this doorbell, because my front door has slightly tinted windows, which make it extremely difficult to tell who is at the door prior to opening it. That is what initially started my Amazon search for a "video doorbell with a speaker" and ultimately resulted in purchasing the Ring doorbell.Basically, you install it where your doorbell sits (hardwired to those existing wires or using battery power) and link to it through a free app you download to your smartphone or tablet. It “talks” to your phone wherever you are (even overseas!), because it communicates over the internet via your home wifi. Make sure you have solid wifi if you want the best results!Once I got this installed, I quickly realized there are other uses...some of which were in the other reviews here, which were extremely helpful, but some of the other uses I just wouldn’t have thought of prior to buying it. I love this thing and they keep pushing out excellent updates to their app, which makes me look forward to what else this company will come up with. Awesome product!Basic Uses (what I thought I’d use it for)• Answer the door without actually opening the door…intercom/video.• Speak to delivery person to leave the package on the porch away from the irrigation while I’m at work.• Record suspicious activity or visitors while I’m gone...or even worse, when I’m home and in bed for the night! I definitely think the camera is a deterrent, by the way, as people definitely notice they’re being recorded and sometimes are pretty funny when they react.Surprise! (what I actually use it for)• My all-time favorite use of this doorbell…candid videos of conversations by the door with my little guy! Seriously, I have captured some of the sweetest, funniest, most adorable conversations between him and me on our way to school, him with his grandmother after school, him with his dad playing basketball in the driveway. I love this doorbell for this reason alone!• Intercom! I talk to my kids and husband more often than they’d probably like using this thing’s intercom…both when I’m outside and they’re inside and vice versa. Love that so much! Super convenient…and the neighbors probably appreciate it more than yelling.• Useful videos of an electrician and a pool guy explaining to me what needed to be done for maintenance after two recent repairs. I saved these to a folder on my laptop so I can reference them in the future.• Monitor teenage daughter coming to and from home…as well as any potential guests she may be inviting over…but more so it makes me feel better to see her coming in the house after school rather than waiting for her text me.• Monitor the kids while they play out front…you can listen to them and watch them…awesome! (Older kids, of course…little ones need full in-person supervision).• Hilarious videos from my family at various times making faces and saying crazy stuff into the doorbell!• Hilarious videos of visitors once they realize it’s a Ring doorbell and they’re on camera. Smile!• Listen to the birds in the morning while still inside the house getting ready in the morning! (Live View – I’m all about it! Best feature, hands down.)Install• I’ll be honest…totally didn’t install this myself. My hubby did and it took maybe 20mins. He’s fairly handy, but I watched the install videos and honestly felt like I could’ve done it myself (don’t tell him that!).• We hardwired ours to the existing doorbell wires, so I can’t speak on the battery usage.• After you download the free app, under settings there is an AWESOME “Physical Installation” option that asks a few questions about where you’re installing and then links you to a how-to video. Genius!• Ours is installed on wood, so it was simple, and came with screws to prevent theft. I read theft was a concern in some of these reviews…the person would be on video, which is a pretty darn good deterrent all on its own…but it would be pretty difficult to remove with the screws provided. Plus, according to their FAQ’s, they’ll replace it if stolen.Alerts• You can get ring alerts (when someone actually rings the bell) or motion alerts (when someone sets off the motion detection).• You can turn one or both of these on/off as you wish in the app.• You can have these on multiple devices and turn them on/off for each device. (Users can have their own “Shared Users” profile…or you can just share your password, which is what my husband and I do.)• Smart alerts!!! Yaaaas! I love this feature. Imagine your kiddo riding their bike back in forth in the driveway and your phone repeatedly alerting you to this motion...each time they pass...over and over...that's how it went until I found the smart alerts setting. I love that this feature is smart enough to recognize that and shut down those alerts! You can set it to frequent when you want to know any possible movement, perhaps when your kids are inside the house! There’s also a “standard” sensitivity, but honestly, the “light” setting is absolutely awesome if you have kids that play in the front yard! (Wish my motion light was that smart!)• You can open the video right from the alert on your phone. Pops up like a normal notification that you swipe to open.• I read a few reviews before buying this that indicated a hesitation or long delay between the alert and opening the video…I’ve not had that problem at all. It literally takes as much time as it takes me to swipe my phone which opens the app. That quick!Live View• I LIVE FOR THIS FEATURE!!! As mentioned above, I have ended up using this doorbell for much more than an intercom and visual on the person ringing my bell. Awesome!!• It sometimes circles for a good bit, maybe 30-45 seconds, before actually opening the video. This is a con, given my wild imagination when my husband travels and my need to see it now! However, it isn’t all the time and for probably 90% of the time it takes only 5-10 seconds to load.Motion sensor• If someone pulls in your driveway or walks on your front porch, the video starts recording and you receive an alert on your phone…anywhere you are located!• Proso You don’t have to wait for someone to ring the bell…they just walk through the motion-sensor range/zone.o You can adjust the range or how far out the motion reaches.o You can adjust the zones, which is a half circle broken into 6 sections, which allows you to limit where the motion is set off.• Conso There are times you get a “false notification” when it’s actually the garbage truck driving by. (Whatevs…not a deal breaker for me in any way.)o There are times when there is a delay in the start of the recording. (This isn’t my favorite thing, but I am guessing my wifi speed plays into it and still doesn’t make me want to remove a star!)Video/Voice/Cloudo You can see the person, hear the person and talk to the person…you can also choose which of those three things you want to do!o You can listen to and watch the person at your door while having your voice muted, which is the default setting.o If you want to talk to them, press the green “talk” button.o If you want to hear them, press the speak icon…or press it again to not hear them.Zoomo You can zoom in while watching the original video by using your hand to manipulate the zoom like you would with any other product on your phone. Love that!o Once the video is saved, say you’re watching it after the person was at your door, you can only zoom in once and directly in the middle of the screen. Not my favorite, but it is better than no zoom, so I’m not complaining!Voice/Video Qualityo Both are excellent. Seriously.o Even the night vision is solid.o The replay is the same good quality. You can definitely see the person very well, even from a little ways away.Cloudo I chose to pay the $30/year for the cloud recording feature. I think it’s a small price to pay…see my surprising uses above and you’ll get that the $30/year fee is more than just for security!o I also save any video that I want to keep as soon as I watch it.o It takes a little minute for the videos to sync, so if you miss the alert by a short minute, then you’ll be stuck waiting for it to sync over the next few minutes or will need to activate Live View.Aestheticso The original version (this one here) is the one I bought, in the bronze color. It’s good quality and the bronze is a rich/shimmery color, but it’s pretty wide…double the size of my original doorbell. I’ve seen they now have a slimmer one with advanced features…super sad I didn’t wait for that one.Overall, I seriously am very happy with this purchase. Even my husband, who wasn’t super psyched to spend a couple hundred bucks on a doorbell, ended up really liking it. I highly recommend it and I definitely can't say that for some of my other "smart home" products!

title: Ring vs Skybell comparison = Ring wins
body: Update 6/6/2017Someone pried the button off my doorbell and I had to interact with Ring customer service for the first time. I'm upgrading them back to 5 stars because I was so impressed with my interaction (unlike Skybell). I went on chat to see about buying a replacement button. Zach told me they didn't offer replacement buttons, but would be happy to replace the whole unit for me. A few minutes later, I had an order confirmation and a new unit on the way. I'm impressed by how easy it was to contact them, and how responsive they were. On top of that, I'm pretty sure a break-in to my neighbour's house was averted when a suspicious person came to look through her windows last night and she confront him through her Ring.Update 6/1/2016Finally got live view on my own Ring now. Other members of my household got it a few weeks earlier than I did so they don't seem to roll it out by household but rather by individual account.Update 3/31/206Although my Ring has the latest firmware update (1.6.76), I still don't seem to have live view on my app. According to the Ring support site this feature is being steadily rolled out and users will receive an email when their device has been enabled. My neighbour's Ring has already been activated so I added a screenshot of what your Ring should look like with live view as an option. Note : According to the support page on Live View, you will only have this feature on the regular Ring if you are connected to doorbell wiring for power.Update 3/23/2016You might want to wait to get the new Ring Pro depending on your needs. They just announced that they are accepting preorders for availability within the next couple of months (not available to preorder on Amazon yet.) The new Pro version requires wired doorbell power while this current version allows battery. The Pro version is 1080P instead of 720P and supposedly has better options around motion detection and night vision. I'm curious to see if the Pro version will resolve some of the issues I've seen (and some of the criticisms other reviewers have) of the delay between a motion alert or doorbell ring and getting the alert on your phone.In my previous update 3/19/2016 I erroneously stated that only Ring Pro will have live view. As Joe pointed out in the comments, Live View should be available as a firmware update for existing Ring devices. My device did not yet update so I cannot test this feature, but once mine does I will check it out and update this review again!-------I bought the Skybell first because I hadn’t heard of Ring. Due to a manufacturing issue where I started seeing the video feed of SOMEONE ELSE’S front door instead of mine, I got a Ring instead. I'm actually glad it happened because I prefer the Ring so much more to Skybell, despite some of the limitations.Having had a chance to install both on my door, here are my impressions:***Installation***Skybell comes with the bell, a mounting plate, and some instructions. Screws were standard and installation was relatively straightforward. Getting the doorbell to connect to our network was a PAIN. It wasn’t able to connect via a 5G network plus it didn’t have strong enough signal strength so we had to get a second router for and set up a whole separate network for Skybell.Ring’s package impressed the heck out of me. You get a little tool pouch with all the tools you need to set up the Ring, including a screw driver, drill bit, self level and more. If you are screwing into stucco (as I was), I don’t recommend you use the included drill bit. The hole it drilled was a little large and I had to silicone caulk the hole afterwards to give the screws enough grip. Ring connected to our network pretty seamlessly and didn’t need to have a separate network nor require the 2nd router.***Size***Ring is MUCH larger than Skybell. I wish it was thinner but I understand there’s a lot to cram in. The Ring does stick out quite a bit visually on my wall, but worth it for what I get. See attached photo to see the size difference.Skybell: 2.15cm / 0.8in thick, 7.1cm / 2.8in diameterRing : 0.87in thick, 4.98 tall by x 2.43 inches wide***Battery / Power to the device***Skybell is fairly low powered, so there is no battery attached to it. It runs off the power from your doorbell so it cannot be mounted anywhere you do not have direct power.Ring is battery powered. I charged it to about 60% via a USB cord first before I plugged it into the wall. You can get some trickle power from the doorbell, but depending on the weather and use, you may find you drain the battery faster than it charges. An hour after the bell has been plugged and with one test video call in I was at 57% battery. Three days later it charged to 77%. This might get annoying, but it does mean Ring can be mounted anywhere, not just where you have direct power.***Mobile App***Skybell only lets you have one user at a time. If you want more than one person to have access to the video stream you have to share accounts. I do like that you can see your doorbell on demand. I like being able to check in on my door. I understand that Ring is working on this but it’s not yet available.Ring lets you have multiple users. The first installed user just has to send email invites to the other users. Ring shows you recent activity, but you need to pay a $3 a month subscription to see your videos on cloud. I wish that wasn’t the case but $3 isn’t that bad. I imagine I will upgrade for the cloud storage long term.In terms of use of app, the Ring interface was relatively straightforward to use and understand. I like being able to set the active zones for tracking motion so that you don't get too many false alarms.***Video on demand**Skybell : yes, but I saw another person's front door too.Ring : not yet, coming soon***Motion detection:***Skybell : YesRing: Yes***Video quality***I couldn’t find the specs on the Skybell. Ring seemed to have slightly higher video quality in the day and slightly higher audio quality but this may be subjective.Please note : If you are expecting the quality that you get on your web cam or Facetime on an iPhone the quality will NOT be comparable. This is still early technology so the voice quality and video quality will be a bit crude. I suspect the design team had to make some trade-offs between video quality and ability to power the unit via battery / trickle charge from the doorbell electrical wire. That said, I am able to identify the voices and recognize people in the camera so that for me is sufficient though of course I hope that future generations will continue to improve in this respect. Ring's cloud recording page gives you a pretty accurate idea of what your videos would look like.***Lag / Delay between door bell ring and phone notification***Seems to be about the same on both. There is a bit of a delay, but for me that’s acceptable compared to the alternative of just missing the door altogether.***Customer Service***Haven't had to interact with Ring's customer service, but Skybell's was so horrid I'm still upset about the interaction. Skybell wanted me to ship my defective Skybell to them at my expense when it was broadcasting someone else's video stream instead of mine right from the start. I get that it's expensive for them, but it's NOT a way to win a customer.UPDATE : I actually did have to interact with Ring customer service and they impressed me greatly. My doorbell button was probably tampered with and they issued an exchange, no questions asked within minutes of contact.Overall, between Skybell and Ring, Ring wins for me hands down.

title: Perfect for the disabled and security-conscience
body: First, some background. I have a spinal cord injury and spend about 23 hours a day in bed. This means getting to the door is a huge burden, as I have to transfer to my wheelchair and get through 2 doors to get there. This takes so much time, the visitor or delivery person leaves before I can even answer the door. I also cannot hear my doorbell in our master bedroom.I ordered the Ring Doorbell through HSN, as they offered 4 flex payments so someone like me can afford the product. I never have $200 to spare for gadgets so this was my lucky day.We had been researching wireless security systems for the last two years, but nothing met our needs. We wanted cameras so I could see the door and our property while my husband was at work. We have frequent visits from Jahovah's Witnesses (but I think my husband finally got us off their list when the last two left in tears. For some reason, two young girls were going around alone and we educated them as to why this was a horrible idea, but I digress.We've also had plenty of crooks scouting out homes for burglary, and while I can meet fire with fire, I'd prefer not to. The final straw was a 4am Ding Dong Ditch, so we knew we had to make a decision on a system.Every system we found required a base system, plus expensive add-on cameras adding up to over $1000.00. Most also required wiring, lacked cloud storage, had low-res cameras, weren't weatherproof, lacked IR night vision, needed batteries too frequently based on the high mounting points we've decided on, and I can go on forever.I finally found the Ring Doorbell and it met our requirements for the first need we had- frequent missed deliveries requiring a signature, answering the door to solicitors despite extensive signage advising not to ring, criminals, you name it.You can imaging my frustration and physical pain when I would answer the door for something completely useless where I wasn't needed. I'd had ENOUGH.I shopped around for the best price. Ring.com offers a payment plan of 2 payments but that was still not in the budget. Luckily, I found the HSN deal and placed my order.The Product:The packaging is extremely nice. Simply cut the two adhesive circle on the top and bottom.
