I did not realize the gaps were preventing the Ring from connecting to the doorbell wires (being hardwired). Instead, it was running off battery. I eventually discovered my error my looking at the phone app. It said "battery" in stead of "hardwired". I fixed it and all ran fantastic.Now the best part. One day I notice my Ring Live Picture had a slight, small area that seemed foggy. I thought it must be moisture had gotten into the unit. I email Ring and sent them a screenshot of the picture. I sent the email around 8pm.The next morning I had a response from Ring. They had reviewed my email and screenshot, determined the lens was scratched. I emailed back, that I thought it was moisture. No matter, the next email said - sending you a new Ring - no charge.Who else does that????I am happy with the Ring. I would buy it again. I am now looking into the Ring "Stick Up" cameras.Ring is a great choice.

title: RING Doorbell is a GREAT Deal for Enhanced Home Security
body: I am a security addict. There, I said it. I have worked at a major international airport in the security field (NOT TSA). What I know from my experience is that you really need to stop people from coming into your secured zone. The last thing that you want to do is have to confront them after they have already accessed it. You need to convince evil doers that the last thing that they want to do is to break into your house or trespass on your property. So, what works best? An armed security guard is best. However, few can afford that. A dog? Maybe, but are you willing to provide the constant training that a protection dog needs? Not many do. However, just having a loud barking dog will dissuade most bad guys from even thinking about coming into your home. So, if you can't afford a security guard, you are allergic or afraid of dogs, what do you do? Get alarms, cameras and big signs announcing their presence. "Most" burglars do not want to deal with any security because "most" burglars have neither the desire or intelligence to do so. They are opportunists. They break into your home because it is there and available. I have numerous methods employed around my home that are effective.Recently, I decided to try the RING doorbell. I am very glad that I did. I got it for a great price during Amazon's thanksgiving sale. It has worked flawlessly since being installed. Installation, by the way, is extremely easy. If you know where your old doorbell is and know how to twist a screwdriver, then you can install this doorbell. It IS that easy. Then you connect it to the APP and you are done. I have had lots of opportunity to test it since installation. With Christmas coming I have had a lot of packages delivered. It instantly identified every delivery person coming to my door and then alerted me within a second or two. Then I am able to review clear video of whatever is, or was, at the door. It has never failed to alert me of someone's presence. (Having a large German Shepherd dog barking in the background doesn't hurt either.) There are a multitude of uses for this camera. You don't need to have only one. I can see myself installing them at every door.Now the problems with RING. -They are rather expensive. The going price is about $179-$199 for the standard model. Costco has the doorbell packaged with the optional chime for $179. The pro model is $20 more. Discounts are few and far between. I got mine for $129 during Black Friday. - RING wants you to subscribe to their "cloud" service in order to store the videos. This is an extra expense that is not necessary. Most people have access to a free cloud storage service already. Paying RING is just a company trying to produce additional revenue. - An article in CNET said that RING was vulnerable to hackers trying to access your WIFI, etc. The hacker would have to physically take the RING doorbell off the wall that it was on and use the orange button on the back to hack into it. RING has supposedly fixed this issue. The hacker would have one big problem though; while he was taking the device off the wall, it would be taking video of him doing so and alerting you and sending video to storage.I have a rather extensive home automation system with alarms. I have a very loud german shepherd dog. I have signs on my lawn and windows. I have other cameras. But my favorite of all of them is the RING doorbell. And NO, I don't work for the company and NO, I didn't receive this product for free. I just like to share my professional opinion when I find a good product.

title: Genius gadget for your family's safety!!!
body: BEST PRODUCT EVERY INVENTED! This is a VIDEO doorbell that set off by MOTION and people RINGING your doorbell with intergraded cameras and 2 way audio that is sent immediately to your smartphone (Android or Apple). So even if you are shopping or at work you can talk to the people over your smart phone and they have no idea if you are in the house or away!I have to tell you about this product because it has been a tremendous help in identifying the person who has been steeling our packages, and has been a blessing for proof that our UPS guy has left my stolen packages on the front patio of our condo, so he doesn't get blammed. Since I order everything possible from Amazon Smiles, I get packages every day. I was searching for a doorbell that would ring on all 3 floors of my condo through wifi when I found this product! I have Nest Drop Cams in my house to watch my dogs and record if anyone breaks in, hurts my animals or steels anything. The RING DOORBELL is my #1 defense against people wandering onto my patio and taking my plants and packages, and doing any harm if they were to break in. Thanks to the RING DOORBELL, I have been able to show the police the videos, and catch the people who are the perpetrators!This is how simple it works...I do not have a wired doorbell, (which you can wire it to), or you can use the battery that comes with it. I have my RING set to MOTION as well as when someone rings the bell. I had to drill into concrete for the base. It was easy because RING gives you the correct size drill bit, along with everything needed for installation. All you need is a drill. It literally took me all of 15 minutes to instal the amazing gadget! The best part is that anyone that would want to steel it, they really have no choice to be caught on video, and it does have a special screw that you would need to remove it from the base. I do believe that RING offers you a free replacement if it is stolen!I easily set the RING doorbell to notify my husband and I when there is motion or someone rings the doorbell. There are no problems with adding more than one user in case one of us is busy. (You can also add-on other cameras around your house.) We get a distinct chime ring on our iPhones instantly and tap the RING App and we can see and have a two-way conversation with whoever is at the door! Sometimes I answer the door from my third floor office, without having to run down to the front door. With packages that need signatures, this is a huge help since the US postal service never gives me enough time to get down stairs to sign for packages; they are always in a hurry and leave before I can get to the door!It is amazing when someone is on your property, wait until you get their face on camera, and then say, "Can I help you?" We have had people taping flyers on our door, solicitors, and as soon as I say, Can I help you they either try to sell something and I can say, "No thank you", or they RUN! if you pay the $30/ year, which is a lot less than the Drop Cams $9/month, you can go back and review all the videos. That is how the police were able to catch the guys steeling packages , not just from our patio but our neighbors.There are videos on YouTube on how to instal the RING Doorbell, trouble shooting and more for easy to follow help and you get 30 days free recording so you can decide to pay for the service if you want to keep recordings!.I am going to give these out to my each of my family members for the holidays. The gift of safety and confidence is priceless!

title: This product is absolutely amazing! I live in a seedy area (not a ...
body: This product is absolutely amazing! I live in a seedy area (not a terribly bad place, but still a bit seedy) and I've had packages/mail stolen in the past. With this, I'm able to view who is at my front door, or who even passes within 30 feet (a function you have to select.) You're also able to view seedy happenings in the neighborhood (there is an area of the app that allows neighbors to post anything they see to alert neighbors.) I love that I can potentially talk to someone at my front door even if I'm not home, and I love that you can view and/or share footage of happenings. I also opted for their $3/month plan that allows you to store footage. I also love that you can personalize your alert tone....I have mine set so my phone tells me "motion detected at your front door" it's better to me for an alert message because there's no doubt that that's what I'm being alerted about.The range of view you're able to get on the Ring is amazing, it's about 180 degree range, so there is no hiding from the camera, and no one can sneak up on our porch without us knowing.Also, I live in a very old home, and I was skeptical that I could even hook the Ring up to my doorbell after viewing the ancient wires, but it connected like a charm, and it keeps my unit charged. I'm able to view the battery power of the unit from my phone, and I've noticed it go down as far as 99% charged, and then pop back up to 100% after several hours of non-use. It does take a long time to charge when you first get it (I don't think I saw anything saying you have to charge it up when you get it, but I had time on my hands waiting for my husband to wake up, so I went ahead and charged it up to 100% before we installed it.) Also, if you do ever need to recharge it on a charger, all you have to do is unscrew the 2 screws at the bottom, pull the main unit off the wall and charge it....there is a bracket that is screwed into the wall that will stay on the wall while you charge it.Another great thing is that you can see very well at night, with or without a porch light on. It gets pretty dark on my porch, so being able to see at night is a huge relief especially as I'm home alone at night a lot. I can take my phone to bed with me and view my front porch if I hear any noises.DOWNSIDES: For anyone who thinks you'll be able to get the Ring and automatically see who is at the door, there is a 20 second delay. You have to opt to view the happenings, they will not just pop up immediately (you get a notification when there's motion if you opt for motion detection, and you'll receive notifications if someone rings your bell, but viewing the camera is not automatic, you have to click the button and wait for it to connect.)The picture can be a bit fuzzy at times, but it clears up, and when you talk through the doorbell, the person talking to you may be a bit garbled, but you can hear perfectly from the Ring.It feels like I often can't get the video to open as quickly as I'd like, I have to close the app and wait and reopen it, or restart my phone....it will either give me a black screen, or the button won't start loading the video, or it will start loading the video and it'll take a long time.....this doesn't happen every time, but it is frustrating at times.The biggest downside for me is that you can not talk to the Ring from the Echo Show, but you can view the camera from the Echo Show, so that's a plus.All in all, I think the upsides far outweigh the downsides, and I truly love this product.

title: Ding Dong! The Best Is Here!
body: Bought this unit to solve several problems (security, communication, etc.) associated with setting up a new office in my 3-story house in the woods. Very secluded setting. The Ring Doorbell addressed all of my immediate security and communication needs and put it in a very manageable device that did not require extensive wiring or a whole lot of work to install. It works like a charm, is very sensitive to motion detection, and provides excellent video and audio results. It saves me from running all the way downstairs in a hurry to answer my first-floor office door, plus it alerts me before the doorbell is even rung via motion detector alerts so I can see who is approaching, even at night courtesy of its IR abilities. It even detects cars that approach my office area.I noticed an imperfection dead-center in the camera lens of the first Ring Doorbell I purchased, which blurred the central image, that could not be removed. Looked like some sort of residue but felt like a scratch, so either a manufacturing or packaging/shipping related defect. But hey, that's just my luck: if it's going to happen to 1 out of 10,000 people, I'll be the one! Still, no worries: I just sent it back to Amazon as defective, ordered a new unit, and am waiting for it to arrive so I can replace it.I would've preferred purchasing the Ring Doorbell Pro, for the added features it offers especially the live video on demand, but that unit requires hardwiring for the doorbell, which I don't happen to have where I installed the Ring unit on my home (I'm using it on battery power). However, my Father, whom I was business partners with, passed away recently, and although I operate our family business now out of my home office, many people still drop by his house a mile or so away from me to conduct business there...or at least they claim to. I'm ordering the Ring Doorbell Pro today for his home so I can see, for myself, if people ARE coming by there as they say; to be able to instantly communicate with them when they do if I'm not there or am busy; and to monitor the area in front of his home and office to provide much greater security there quickly and easily.These units are Wifi driven, so if your Wifi signal is weak where you'll be installing a Ring Doorbell, I suggest investing the money in getting the Netgear AC750 Wifi range extender to solve your problem, also available from Amazon. It's pricey, but it does the job, able to vastly improve your signal strength and range. My router is on the third floor of my home; my home office is on the first floor. The Wifi signal is already weak on the first floor and was non-existent outside the metal double doors where I placed the Ring Doorbell unit. Due to the distance from the router and the particular building materials used in my home construction - including interior metal load-bearing beams, metal doors and windows, cinder block and concrete first-floor construction with a thick coating of Dryvit exterior siding, which literally wraps the first floor entirely in signal-blocking wire mesh netting - getting a Wifi signal outside to the Ring Doorbell unit seemed improbable. But the Netgear Wifi extender (installed on the second floor) made it through all of that and provided me with a very strong signal to work with on my Ring Doorbell. As an aside, I now run my office PC off the Netgear extender, too, for faster Internet connections. I'd highly recommend one of these Ring Doorbell units for anyone looking for a sleek, easy, fair-priced solution to the kind of problems it solved for me.

title: We were delighted to receive our Ring unit as a surprise xmas ...
body: We were delighted to receive our Ring unit as a surprise xmas gift from friends with no existing doorbell or surveillance system.Physical installation :took around 15 minutes to screw the base into the doorframe using the provided screws and do up the 2x Torx security screws with the handy included screwdriver. We are using the in-built battery with no existing doorbell wiring for power. The included easy to find video tutorials really helped a lot for installation planning.Motion detection sensitivity: We have an adjoining 6ft wooden fence <3ft from our front door so lowest detection area was selected and is the perfect setting with only human movement captured (not the squirrels that run along the fence all day). We don't have any pets or visible plants so can't comment on any sensitivity issues there.Lowlight performance: We have a very bright motion activated LED light that shines directly at the Ring unit <3ft away and it does not cause any visible reduction in nighttime video clarity. Lowlight performance at dusk/night is great and very clear. Current heavy rains haven't appeared to affect the footage or other performance in any way.Ringer: The ringer unit took <1min to install by just plugging it in to power ready for syncing through the app. We have it set to play a chime when activity detection occurs and is loud enough to hear in the adjoining rooms and works very well.App: Installation took around 15 mins to setup on my HTC 10 Android 7.0 mobile and configure the main Ring and the ringer units. The app has a really easy to use logical format, is yet to crash and is very snappy switching between screens in the UI. The app also installed quickly and runs great on my wife's Iphone 6s.Wifi: I work in WiFi so available signal strength is not an issue with well-behaved 802.11g/n/ac 2.4 & 5ghz signal saturating our small 2br duplex home. Both the main ring unit and ringer unit found the WLAN on first try and connected with minimal effort in the app. What is a little odd is the main unit is reporting RSSI of 32 - 'good' and ringer unit RSSI is 34 - 'good'. For signal strength, when measured in dB the closer to 0 and dB of 65 or lower is better. Conversely the Ring support KB article regarding 'poor signal strength' shows screenshots with 'very good' for an RSSI of 51. Regardless how RSSI is being reported in the app the signal for both units has not dropped out or decreased to date which is what really matters.Battery: Took around 3 hours to charge via a PC USB port to the units micro USB port at first use and now after 10 days of use the battery is still showing 95% power.Lens: The wideangle lens of the camera means we can capture the entire walkway that runs the length of our house.When visitors ring the bell the performance is also excellent. The great proximity detection means we are prepared even before the doorbell rings.Overall : performance is excellent with mail deliveries and other activity recorded in very clear HD video and audio is also very clear.Improvements:- The provided mounting screws could be of a higher grade as one sheared off in the hardwood door frame.- It would be handy to have the ability to recharge the unit without removing it via the micro USB port.- An included solar power accessory would be very useful.- Use of dB for RSSI reporting in app-USB Type C instead of Micro USB for the charge port would make recharging much faster

title: I'm very happy with the product so far
body: I'm very happy with the product so far. Let's get right to the facts of my experience:Pros:1) Easy to install. I mounted mine into brick, took me about 10 minutes. Ring supplies you with the drill bit for brickwork so I didn't have to scramble for bits. It's very secure and I appreciate their 'free replacement' guarantee if someone was to steal it.2) Easy to setup in the app. I must say first that I work in I.T. so my version of easy may differ from yours but I really found the app design pretty intuitive.3) Great idea to have a battery as an option. This battery is rated to last 1yr under normal use which is acceptable for me. I wish I only had to charge half of my devices only once a year. I did not use the existing wiring from my previous door bell as I didn't like that location so the battery allowed me to pick wherever I wanted to place the unit.4) Cloud recording. I paid for the annual cloud storage for a few reasons. One being that this device can be used for basic security, besides to see who is ringing your door bell. Let me explain. When you have the cloud storage, you can set your device to detect motion based on a 6 zone grid format of the front of your house and you can choose the sensitivity (up to 30ft) of where you'd like to be alerted for motion. What's key here is that it also records sound when an alert is triggered so if someone came up to your porch or on your lawn and the motion alert is detected, you also get to hear sound. This was key for me once when I had two people at my door step, the motion alert sounded and I was able to hear what they were talking about. Turns out in that example, it was two sales reps from a solar company going door to door and before they rang my doorbell, they realized they forgot some material in their car so they went back to get it, but I was able to know that they were on my porch and could hear what they were discussing even though they didn't ring the bell. Could come in handy if two people were scouting out my house for a robbery and I had the video/audio from the cloud recording to present to police.5) The design. I really think it's a stylish design and as of this writing, they had 5 different styles to choose from.Cons:1) Customer Service - my first unit had a problem with the microphone. Don't get me wrong, they resolved my issue by sending out a replacement without hassle, my gripe is that it took a few days of emailing (did not see a phone option) to get to the point where a replacement was determined as needed. In my opinion, that could've been solved in a 15 minute phone call and the few days it took to come to this resolution left me with a half working (no sound) doorbell. Not the end of the world.2) Motion recording. It's handy to have and I'll take it in the form that it's in, but it's far from perfect. I set my motion sensitivity setting on the lowest setting which is a 6 ft radius from my doorbell, basically, just motion on my front porch should be picked up but I still get motion alert texts and 90% of them are cars passing by in the street which I thought would get ignored. I'm glad things are being recorded in the event of an emergency but it definitely gives me the "crying wolf" feeling whereas I ignore most of the motion alerts because I'm accustomed to them being cars driving by. Hopefully an app/firmware upgrade will fix this.The pro's far outweigh the cons though and I'd recommend this product to anyone.

title: IDEAL product for monitoring who is entering your yard/ringing your doorbell when you're not home
body: We've had our Ring for 2 weeks now and can't stop singing the praises!We first heard about Ring from our neighbors on Nextdoor.com who had caught some strange incidents with strangers in our neighborhood - incidents they never would have known about had it not been for Ring. As they said, you never realize how many people ring your doorbell or approach your house when you're not home.What makes Ring different than other cameras: The Ring camera is part of your doorbell and it's also connected to your cell phone/tablet/computer/any device with an internet connection. When someone rings your doorbell or is within the range of the motion detector (you set the sensitivity) you get an alert on your phone. You can choose to answer the doorbell via your cell phone/tablet and talk to whoever is at the door (or you can ignore them.) No need to open the door and you have a live streaming video of the person you are talking to! You can be 3000 miles away and talk with someone at your door, even convince them you are home etc. With regular cameras, they film all the time and are not 2-way. You would have to filter through hours of footage to find the situations you are looking for. With Ring, it only records when there is motion or the doorbell rings. On the app, you have a list of "incidents" and can watch them moments after they happen.Added bonuses that we didn't realize until we installed Ring: Whenever there is motion in front of the camera, we get an alert on our phone. So, as a fun bonus, when I leave the house in the morning with our kids and my husband is already at work, we set off the motion detector when we walk out our front door. Our kids always leave a little message for their dad in the video that Ring takes, knowing he's getting an alert at work at that moment. He can then watch a little video each morning of the kids. Kinda fun :)I love the motion detector on the camera (you can set this to your preferences and also chose whether to get motion alerts at all) as I feel much more secure knowing when someone is in our yard. I was at the gym this morning and saw an alert pop up. I checked it from the treadmill and saw a package get delivered to our door.We have already had one door-to-door salesman encounter with Ring and I talked to him through my phone the whole time, while sitting on our couch watching TV! I watched the salesman on the video as I talked and told him we were not interested in his product. It was getting dark outside and I could watch him leave the yard, confident he was actually gone (which I couldn't do if I had just closed the door on him.) Wonderful peace of mind. The "chat" through the doorbell is great quality - sounds just like an intercom and is loud and clear. The doorbell has a light around the button and that provides plenty of light to be able to see who is at your door at night. No additional lights are needed.All in all, this is a WONDERFUL product and totally suits our needs. We live in a safe neighborhood but wanted some extra peace of mind about who was entering our yard. Also with package deliveries picking up for the holidays, we wanted to be able to make sure no one was stealing anything left on our porch. This product is perfect for both these needs. HIGHLY recommend!!!Also, EASY to install, EASY app to use, and really nice looking! The chime is also loud and clear (both inside the house and on the Ring itself.) 5+ stars!!!

title: Ring Security doorbell will watch your front door for you!
body: The Ring door bell was my first foray into security cameras. After doing research into the Nest and various other security Cameras i found the Ring to meet my personal needs for security. I have broken down the review into a couple of categories, Setup, usability, product quality and support.-SetupThis was fairly easy as i choose to use the "hardwired" option for a pre existing doorbell i had. The wiring was very simple and Ring provides video installation videos on their setup app and online via youtube. The unit updated its firmware immediately and i was up and running fairly quickly. The setup was very user friendly and did not require any prior electrical or security camera installation experience.-UsabilityThe Ring app is fairly easy to use and navigate. The Doorbell allows for a live view mode including access to recorded videos saved via the cloud. The service is $3 a month per each camera you have with a free 30 day trial. This fee is not to bad and is actually cheaper than some competitors cloud recording subscriptions. The camera is still usable without the paid subscription you just wont be able to access saved events as there is no cloud storage on a non subscription account.-Product QualityThe unit itself has a premium sturdy feel. The unit is a good size and does somewhat resemble a video conferencing unit. Additionally the unit is made to withstand the outdoor elements for extreme heat and cold. I live in AZ so i can only confirm the heat part but not so much on extreme cold "no snow here". When live view is utilized the app takes a couple of seconds for the view to come up. The video quality is good to great depending on the time of day. I found during certain times of the day the video quality on my phone was either really good or just ok. Either way i was always able to make out faces for individuals who walked in front of the camera. The camera has a motion sensor and at first kept picking up cars passing through the street. I had to adjust the settings a couple of times to ensure this wasn't the case. Everyone's experience with this may very due to geographical setup of everyone home including how busy your street is, kids playing in front yard..etc. After about 2 week I was able to figure out the best settings for my area.-SupportSupport from the Ring team has been decent to really good. I generally used the email function and would hear back within 24 hours. I can't comment on their over the phone support as I generally work during these times thus was not able to use. The email responses were thorough and answered any questions i had (mostly motion setting questions) Additionally the app and Ring website provide additional avenues for support as well.-ConclusionOverall I am satisfied with my purchase. My neighborhood has had some individuals stealing packages left at homes and thus this gives me piece of mind in knowing it will either deter a thief or capture a face if a package goes missing. The quality is great and other than having to play around with the motion settings I had no issues setting up and maintaining the system. Everyone else I know who have the Ring doorbell also have had great experiences and thus I haven't come across anyone with a negative experience so far. I do recommend being patient with the motion settings when you first setup your camera as it will take some trial and error to find the perfect settings for your setup/area.

title: Simple security solution. Exactly what I was looking for.
body: I purchased the Ring to solve a simple problem I was having. I knew I wanted a security camera covering the front of our home but I didn't want to deal with the complexities of a more "common place" security camera system. Upon receiving the Ring I got to setting it up right away. The app was helpful in that it asked me what type of surface I was installing it on and then proceeded to show me a video made specially for that surface. After about 30 minutes I was on connected to my network and ready to use it.Overall I am quite thrilled with this doorbell from a security standpoint which was my main reason for buying it. A great example of how handy it is is I was running late and at a red light when I heard my phone do the doorbell chime. I was able to see who it was and let her know I was 5 minutes away. This was the perfect time to have the Ring because she didn't have my phone number.Here's some pro vs. cons:Pros:-Cloud recording is must have and worth the $30 per year fee. I regularly review any motion detection activated videos weekly and know that without the cloud storage, I'd have to immediately look at my phone for every motion detection notification to try and figure out what/who is moving around. Also, downloading the videos is a breeze whether from the app or via the web portal.-The ability to turn off motion detection notifications. At first it was pretty cool getting a notification every time it sensed motion but after a few days, it was pretty annoying being told that "hey! You're leaving your house".-Clear video: In a lot of the negative reviews I was seeing complaints about video quality. This, usually, comes down to your wireless router's placement. Mine happens to be about 20 feet from the front door so the quality of my network connection from the Ring to the router is very strong. Please take this into consideration before assuming you have a defective unit or that the product is generally lacking.-Two way audio: The quality is excellent and have had no issues understanding, or being understood, when using this feature. It's really nice that when viewing who is at your door the microphone is muted by default so they won't hear you whispering to your spouse that you don't really want to talk to them right now.-Angle of view: It's just about 180 degrees in all directions which is amazing to me since I have such a small front porch and was concerned about how much coverage I would get. You'll see by my attached photo that there is no problem with seeing my entire front yard.Cons:-By default the unit makes a chiming noise when the doorbell is rung. It puzzled me at first because all it does it provide confirmation to the individual pressing the doorbell that they rang it. There's no place in the app to change this setting but after emailing support they were able to disable this feature.--Motion Detection: while it isn't always the fastest at recording motion it does capture enough to make me satisfied with identification if necessary. If the mail person is super fast then yes, you'll probably only get the back of them. I've found that having the sensitivity set further out helps with this as it detects motion sooner. I'm not trying to defend the lag in recording but I would guess that your average thief isn't going to be sprinting up to your front door and busting in...unless you only have a screen door which isn't anybody's fault but your own.

title: Even an easy to wire diode is included for those of you ...
body: Unboxing the Ring video doorbell, I was quick to notice every tool you need to install it is included in the box. A level and screwdriver are included in the box along with all of the screws and hardware you would need for install. Even an easy to wire diode is included for those of you that are replacing an existing doorbell. For those of you that are challenged by anything that involves a screwdriver, there is also help. Ring has a network of professionals that will install your doorbell at your home for a $50 flat fee.The first step of installing the Ring video doorbell is downloading the mobile app. To my delight Ring as an iOS app for you Apple fanboys and an Android app for Google devotees like myself. The app is slick and very intelligent. There are tutorials throughout the app to assist you in the setup of the video doorbell and phone app. The tutorial assisting in the activation of the motion detection was informative.Ring uses IR (infrared) technology to trigger the motion detection. If an old newspaper blows through your yard it will not be triggered. The Ring video doorbell is looking for a heat signature. The app also gives you the ability to map the range, sensitivity, and areas you would like monitored by the motion detection.What I like best about Ring is there is no equipment rental. Once you purchase the hardware, you are free to move it from home to home or business to business. Ring users can opt to pay no additional fees after purchasing the hardware. With the equipment purchase, you will receive instant alerts, stream video & audio and live view.Ring also has a small three dollar per month plan for video recording and storage per device per month. Users with multiple devices can opt into the Protect program which gives the user unlimited cameras for $100 per year and a lifetime hardware warranty. If your Ring malfunctions or gets damaged, they’ll replace it for free. The Protect program is significant savings when compared to the fees other home security companies charge for outdated technology. Using cutting edge technology most criminals will not even attempt circumventing your security system.Despite TV shows and web features that profile “America’s Dumbest Criminals” all criminals are not dumb. However, by nature they are lazy. If a criminal knows there is high-definition video recording with IR motion detection and night vision, they are moving on to the next house. Your average criminal is not looking to put on an Ocean’s Eleven type caper to pull off stealing your flat-screen and watch. Criminals look for easy marks that give them the least chance of being caught as you can see in this video.If said criminal is stupid enough to try and breach your security, you have safeguards with the Ring system. You will be alerted in real-time on your mobile app if your system is tripped. Real-time alerts allow you to call the proper authorities and protect your belongings. If you miss an alert, your recording is saved to access online or in the mobile app. If indeed your house has been compromised you can share a video of the party of interest with law enforcement.Ring is also perfect for you condo owners and apartment dwellers. The Ring video doorbell can operate on its internal rechargeable battery. This battery will give you 6 months to a year of video security on a single charge. There is no existing wiring or doorbell needed in its install.

title: The security features the Ring doorbell provides well worth the money!
body: I absolutely LOVE the Ring doorbell. If you notice a lot of the poorer reviews on this device, you will see that most of them occurred in 2015 or if it is too far away from the internet router and there's not a good wifi signal to where the doorbell has been mounted. It seems that the Ring company must have addressed many of these issues, because we haven't encountered any of them.Pros of the Ring doorbell: the ability to answer the doorbell from anywhere that your cell phone works (even in another country!); a good picture of the person who's at your door and the ability to talk to them as well; if you have a physical disability that prevents you from getting to your door quickly when someone rings the doorbell, you can answer from your phone; added security because it appears someone is always home (many burglars ring the doorbell first to see if someone is there before trying to break in); if you pay for the additional cloud storage, you have a record of who has been at your front door for an extended time period (and what was said) in case you would need it for police/insurance purposes; any time there is motion activity at your front (or back door if you put an additional optional camera there), you can be notified on your phone or tablet; if you have an existing doorbell, you can hardwire the Ring doorbell directly into it so it still chimes at home when someone presses the button; there's a lot of flexibility when it comes to setting up the motion alert zones in front of your home and how the doorbell notifies you of that activity. Actually, one of the biggest pro's with this device was just activated yesterday: the ability to obtain a LIVE VIEW anytime you like of what's going on at your door. The Ring company just added this feature yesterday, apparently after many people requested it. It's going to be a really nice feature for our family--we will use it as an intercom service to talk to each other from the front porch where our porch swing is located and where we spend a lot of time in nice weather. I've also started making a habit every morning, of checking for motion activity on our front porch that may have occurred the night before. We have issues sometimes during the summer when school's out and teens have nothing better to do than roam around the neighborhood looking for unlocked cars. If I set up my notification settings to notify me at night when someone is in our front yard, I will be able to apprehend them faster, in addition to having a visual record of their activity.Cons: there's a slight delay (at least in our house) of maybe one second between when someone presses the doorbell and when our phones are notified. However, this is a very slight delay and that one second is not much of a detriment, compared to the security this device provides. The only other aspect I would like to see improved is a better picture other than the "fish eye" view you see when you're looking at the picture that the doorbell provides; again, though, it's still a pretty good quality. I was able to make out the UPS truck's letters when it pulled up to our home. And even though you can hardwire the Ring doorbell directly into your existing doorbell, you need to know what you're doing to accomplish that. Luckily my husband is a handyman and that was not a challenge for him.All in all, I'd say the Ring doorbell has worked out very well for our household. My husband and I are very glad we purchased it!

title: Updated review.  Enthusiastic.
body: THIS IS THE ORIGINAL REVIEW. SEE UPDATE, BELOW, WITH VERY POSITIVE COMMENTS.After about a year of experience, I say that Ring gets a B+ as a high school project. It's an excellent idea, and would be great if it worked in real life. Regrettably, it does not.Soon after installation it became clear that, in most cases, I cannot activate the 'front door live view.' Instead of a live view, I receive select error messages. When the zodiac is in a particularly lucky configuration, I do get a live view. This happens about 20% of the time.This is the case when I try to activate live view on my own, from my phone. When someone actually rings the doorbell, chances are better that I can establish a live view, but it is not guaranteed.I installed an extra network extender in a strategic location. No luck.I was on the phone with the support line for over an hour, trying all sorts of reinstallations and configurations. The technician sounded really eager to help, but he could not overcome the shortfalls of the system.Recently, the system started to issue doorbell alerts on its own initiative, as if someone had rung the doorbell. Nobody had. I deactivated the button which controls the ring alerts, but my phone keeps issuing the alerts anyway. Annoyingly, and expectedly, I cannot activate live view to see if someone is actually waiting in the front.It appears as if my only course of action is to uninstall the doorbell.I would not wish this system on my friends. 'Cannot recommend it' is an understatement.UPDATED REVIEW==============I am glad to update my review, and change the rating from one star to five.A dedicated and determined support person, Danielle J., contacted me regarding my one-star review. I was quite busy and did not respond immediately, but she emailed me and called my phone number, and we set up a support call. Thanks for your perseverance, Danielle. She had me perform various operations on the doorbell. Unfortunately, as in the case of the original support call I’ve had several months earlier, the doorbell kept displaying the behavior I described in my original review. As she promised during our call, she had a new unit mailed to me.I set it up. It has performed perfectly for the last week or so, time enough for the old unit to start misbehaving, so I hope I have now completed a valid testing period. All functions operate as promised, and it is a pleasure to be able to inspect the front porch of my house, and talk to people who ring the bell, from any location where my cell phone can connect to the internet.Advice to new users: My unit required an initial charge (with a phone charger) of some five hours. After that, the unit froze, and appeared dead. I found, on line, that to reset the unit one must hold down the red button on the back for 20 seconds. That exercise restored the unit to life, and I was able to install it.Next, even though I connected the unit to live doorbell wires, and even though the old doorbell rang along with the ring doorbell, the test for ‘device health’ kept showing the unit as battery powered. I was resigned to charging the battery (every six months, they say), but, after a day or two, weirdly and delightfully, ‘device health’ started showing ‘hardwired,’ and has remained so since then.Thanks again to Danielle J.

title: RING does the job, and a bit more.
body: After doing very extensive research and considering Skybell (must be hardwired, so that was a show stopper), I decided to give Ring a try.I will not describe every Ring feature, since you might already know them all by going to their web site or reading the reviews here. But I will give my sincere opinion about the product and their customer service.I did read a large number of reviews. I always read the 'one star' reviews first. I have to say that I respect their opinion, but I strongly disagree with many of them. It makes me wonder if they are written by the competitors.(Full disclosure--I am not, in any way, affiliated w/ the RING Company or their staff and I paid full price for my device.)Since the reviews were mixed, I decided to risk it. After all, 199 dollars is just a nice dinner out with the family in NYC. If I did not like it, I could always return it.And as my alias says, I am a Gadget Guy. I try them all.Here are my findings, and let's start with the CONS.Cons:-My family did not approve the purchase. One of the reasons my family did not like it is because they don't want to have their phones/ipads with them all the time in order to hear the ring. It does ring on the unit as well, but it's hard to hear it inside the house.NOTE 5/14: I learned yesterday, by email, that this problem will be FIXED in July. Ring is launching a new device that you can plug in anywhere in the house and RING will send the ring to the device as well. It’s called Ring Chime. That is definitely a fix for a major issue since you can even have more than one connected to the Ring Bell (no wires).-You are buying new technology, so you have to be patient. You might find bugs or wish for features that are not implemented yet. So again, you have to be patient.-If your device is not hard wired, you will have to take it off from the wall plate using the Ring's special screw driver to charge it. I have a lot of activity in my house, and my unit is always recording movements and rings, so I have to charge it about every 3 to 4 months. It's not hard to do it, but I would prefer to just attach my external battery charger to the unit, while it's still mountedNow the ProsPros:-Because you are buying new technology from a young company, the new features just keep coming. Many features and bug fixes were released since I purchased my unit, including the cloud recording. It looks like they might be an Agile project management methodology company, for software release, but that is just my guess.-Customer service is very good. They answered many of my emails, texted me via Facebook and even called me at home three times. That is pretty strong customer service for a 199 dollar device. I don't get this service for my 2000 dollar TV or even my car.-The Ring just works. It does the job. It sends me videos when someone approaches my house, and connects me to people with video and two way talk when they ring the bell.-You can control the sensitivity of the motion sensors, and they do work.-It makes you feel safer. I have young kids and I always know when they arrive home after school.-I like the design and the lights. (I t could be a bit thinner, but since I don't carry that with me, that would be just for aesthetic reasons.I recommend this product.

title: Disabled Approved & by this ADA Member
body: I am newly disabled and I have had more then some extra time surfing the internet(bad thing). I was confined to my bed for 23 & 1/2 hours a day, for most days. Then to make matters worst, my family worked on average of 12 hours per day. I now am in a power wheelchair for a good part of my days, so I have some new found freedom between the bed, kitchen, and bathroom. All joking aside, I think that you should contact the ADA and let them know about this great piece of automation you have designed. I am only making assumptions here, but I think I may be correct on my assumptions. What you have here is a very "POWERFUL TOOL" for the DISABLED COMMUNITY and you may not even know it. I can see that your product in this industry of life automation that is growing fast, that your modest front door bell is much more than that. Again I joke about the modest part, as I have seen the propaganda on the internet where ever I could find it to make sure I was getting the Door Bell that was going to add to my new found freedom in a wheelchair opposed to being bed riding. I still have use of my left arm and hand as well as still some partial use of my right hand. Now I want you to imagine all of the features that you show in your advertisements making life easier, safer, attentive, at home, in the country and so on. Now take those powerful situations and apply them to the disabled ( WOW RIGHT ). I know you are thinking that some of my disabilities must have made it to my head. However they did not, I just wanted to gain some kind of purposes with my new found freedom as well as doing some good in the world we live in. So I have trained myself to something that could keep some of our overwhelming consumer waste out of landfills and third world country's streets and empty lots. Again you're thinking that maybe my disability is more in my head then in my limbs. Well it is not, I wanted to repair as many consumer electronic devices that I could with my remaining abilities. Cell Phones was a perfect one for me to tackle, do to their abundance and size for handling with my hands. I know you are thinking how I could pontificate this much and still not giving you the WOW factor. Well its this simple, UPS, FEDEX, USPS will not deliver a lot of today's most popular consumer devices WITHOUT A SIGNATURE OF THE RECIPIENT. Do you see it now, I am able to be in my bed for whatever the reason is that day and not lose out on an important delivery. My delivery people would rather wait a minute or two for me to get transferred into my power wheelchair, so that I can accept my delivery that is important to me and them. This way they won't have to make the three days attempt to deliver it and wast there time oppose to getting it to me on the first day because I was able to answer RING and my front door. I can tell you that I can speak for all of my delivery people, in saying that none of them had never seen such a product as well as they all think it is great. Because they don't have any missed deliveries here. I have so many other great stories and situations but I don't like to be long winded. Also let me please apologize for my spelling and composition that you had to just Indore from me to get my point across. Please feel free to contact me if you would like to hear of the other ways your great modern doorbell (RING) is helping the disabled as well as the abled.KC

title: I love the Ring video doorbell!
body: I have had the Ring video doorbell for a few months now and have had an excellent experience with it. I have found that the number one feature actually has nothing to do with the device itself, but rather the amazing team at Ring! (Don't get me wrong, the device is wonderful!) I installed the device in my apartment home while I am awaiting the completion of our new home where I plan to permanently install the device and had a good experience, but the team at Ring could tell the WiFi signal to my doorbell had been low and reached out to me with easy solutions to improve the signal. The solutions worked and my video quality became much better. I can also say the team at Ring are very responsive to feedback and look to implement customer suggestions in their products. Overall I would describe the Ring system as "Customer Centric" in that they truly care about the safety and convince of the customer.The Look: I actually saw a Ring video doorbell in the "Wild" when I was visiting the French Quarter in New Orleans, LA. It caught my. eye as a smart home junkie and I knew I wanted to have one as part of my smart home ecosystem. The device is very well made and looks amazing with my brushed nickel door hardware from Kwikset. The button features lights that interact with the user to inform you on what is happening.Smart Home: I use the Ring Doorbell with my Wink Hub and the Wink app and have been throughly impressed with the integration between the platforms. For example, If USPS rings my doorbell while I am away, I can answer the call in real time, interact with the mailman, and even unlock my connected Kwikset lock to allow my mailman to place the package inside the door. Again I can verify this all in real time and ensure the the safety and security of my home. I also love that Ring can communicate if motion is detected back to my Wink App as I use this to have the front door light turned on when someone approaches the door. I can't say enough how thankful I am for the integration with the Wink system and the extra features partnerships like this unlock.Install: Install was super simple. I am using it with the included battery in my current apartment with plans to hardwire in our new home. I do have a wired doorbell in my apartment I used to test the doorbell when hardwired and loved the look of the dim locator light around the button, but opted to go with battery to make it easier to move to our new home. I also was happy to find that the packaging included everything I needed to install the doorbell including the drill bit and screwdriver.What makes it special: No doubt there are other video doorbells on the market but Ring's amazing support, commitment to the customer, and features make it the simple choice for me. I love that video storage is only $3 a month or $30 for a full year of video storage. The motion sensors and ability to set custom motion detection zones are a huge win for me, especially in the apartment to ensure I am only receiving alerts that are useful to me. On top of all this, I love that Ring continues to introduce features and improve the Application on a consistent basis.I hope you find Ring to be as helpful and reliable as I have. If you have any questions, I would recommend tweeting @Ring on twitter as they are very helpful, responsive, and willing to help!

title: Not a bad unit, but it needs two improvements, especially a programming change for people who live 40 feet from their street
body: [[VIDEOID:dc16c7f3e7cf35fd8e3c37edb65c9faf]] I first bought this unit in 2016 and only gave it three stars at that time because I was unable, even with CustServ help, to get it to both 1) "see" people walking up onto my porch and 2) NOT see cars driving by 40 feet away. I complained to them about battery life and too many car alerts. I told myself if they ever fixed my complaints, I'd revisit this review.Almost 12 months later, I dusted off the box and TRIED again. I again contacted Ring's Customer Service, and this time, a new agent, Jeffrey, was able to get it working AS I WANTED IT TO. Part of his method was to send me a NEW doorbell (lifetime warranty) and introduce me to new products that they invented. (I wonder if they invented those new products because of my 2016 complaints). Spacers now allow me to adjust the doorbell angle downward by 5°, 10° or 15°. They also created a solar charger. They effectively answered all of my complaints.I am very happy to change my rating to FIVE STARS. The 2018 version of the Ring Wi-Fi enabled Video Doorbell is a vast improvement over their 2016 doorbell, and INFINITELY better than my old (boring) doorbell. I would give Jeffrey TEN stars if I could.LATE 2017 SUMMARY:I finally decided to try again, got out the bell, recharged the battery, but it wouldn't work at all this time, so I e-mailed Customer Support. Jeffrey wrote back then called me and held my hand every step of the way. He diagnosed that I had a bad doorbell, sent me a replacement unit for free, even though it had been almost 12 months since purchase, and we got it working. When I continued to have occasional alerts from passing cars, he sent me some wedges to try, which cured that problem; and when I worried about how often I'd have to charge the battery, he made sure I got a solar charger to try.The unit at 15° detected some cats walking across my porch, so I now have it at 10° and it works great. I have it aimed at a 45° angle with a neighbor-made bracket from last year. At 10° and with my car parked where it is, I don't get any passing car alerts. It recognizes people when they get to my steps. This doorbell is well worth FIVE STARS.A year ago, I said that if they could fix these problems, and especially make it less likely to "see" cars and not require me to charge the battery anywhere near as often; that I'd upgrade my rating. Well, they did. The new unit is better quality, they invented angle spacers to not recognize cars as much, and they came up with a solar charger which (even though my house faces north) is getting enough ambient light to not discharge much during winter. I look forward to summer charging when the morning sun will come in under the porch on my north side door.Thank you, Jeffrey, for AMAZING customer service.IMAGES:0. Not shown: When Doorbell was mounted at 0° (default position), I occasionally detected a car on the street 40 feet from the door.1. When I mounted the doorbell at a 15° downward angle, it occasionally gave me motion alerts for cats crossing the porch. Cat's image used without cat's permission.2. Not shown: Since I remounted it at a 10° downward angle, it has not told me about any cats, nor has it told me of any cars. I believe I have struck a happy medium.

title: A good WiFi product
body: Just installed my ring last week and all has been going well. I think the product is better than what earlier reviews have noted. Probably this is due to improvements in the software. According to my conversations with Ring, there have been no major modifications in the hardware although again from earlier reviews, I think they may have made some improvements on sealing the unit from the weather.Components and fit and finish is good. Unit back plate securely mounts and there are no issues on connecting the unit to the mounted backplate. Be sure to look at it carefully and you will see the tabs require you to push the ring onto the tabs and then push the ring down. That allows it to be secured to the back plate. Tighten the two security screws on the bottom and you have solidly mounted unit.I did check my wireless signal at the bell location and it sits about -63db which is a good signal level. The video quality is actually better than I expected. That quality is definitely going to vary based on the strength of your wireless. The better the signal, the better the video quality. Be aware that the lighting conditions can affect the light exposure for the camera. In my case I have a shaded porch which the camera does well with. As you look beyond the porch the front lawn and street tend to be over exposed in sunny weather. Not a big issue, but there is some overexposure of the area beyond the porch. Understand that if the sun beams into a shaded area you could get a situation where the face of the person might be a little dark due to the bright background. In that case you may have to consider remounting the unit to a different angle to avoid that direct sun.Right now I'm still working on adjusting the sensors to avoid picking up vehicle traffic. I've got things down to maybe one or two activations a day from trucks that are doing deliveries. With the weather turning colder it will be interesting to see if heat sources are more readily seen which may require some adjustment of the sensor range.Typically our front door is active with my dogs going out front to do their business. The sensor will pick them up as they leave the porch and come back to the door.I'm glad I have a power coming to the button since I doubt that the battery would hold out long based on active traffic. Yesterday I noticed the battery at 85%. By morning it was back to 100%. After putting the dogs out a few times we are back down to 87% No problem if you have power to the button, but if not and your door is active, I can't see the battery based on my activity for holding out more than 10 days without a recharge.Overall I'm happy with the unit and the security it provides. They are working on software updates that will allow you to turn the camera on and off from the app, rather than the camera coming on only by motion detection. I'm glad I bought this and it definitely is better than some of the early reviews might indicate. Be aware that you need to verify that your WiFi signal is good at your mounting location. Also make sure you use some WiFi detection software to verify the channel that your WiFi is on. Interference from neighbor's WiFi could affect connectivity. You should understand how to change your WiFi's channel if your neighbors WiFi interferes with your signal.

title: Love it! And an honest review you can go by
body: [[VIDEOID:f8b7cac5d9ee3875942b6d77506240dd]]You know I came here and read a ton of reviews on this product and paid most of my attention to the critics to see what flaws and problems existed with this product. What I heard mostly were the following;- It's too laggy or didn't take video in time and only caught the person moving away from the house. My response, well most of my videos are captured when the person was approaching my door or at my door. My internet speed is 50mps up and 50mps down and the device is not far away from my router. I have no problems. Yes there are some cases where I only got the person moving away but I imagine most of the reason has to do with the fact that these videos are stored on the cloud. If the service or your connection isn't able to perform well, this is likely the reason. For me personally, it's fine about 95% of the time. And I even use it on battery power. No issues whatsoever.- Video looks like it's looking out of a fisheye. My response, Uh....yeah....that's what gives you enough of a perspective to see from side to side of the unit in almost an 180 degree angle. But I am not shooting home movies with this product. I am trying to see who's at my door. Some recent reviews said the quality of the video wasn't clear. I have no idea what they are talking about. Mine is clear as can be in 720p. Don't blame poor internet connectivity on the product.- Motion sensor picks up cars that drive by. My response, you have to adjust the sensitivity to get to the happy medium. I had to play with this a bit to get it right and also had to move a flag in the front of my house that was setting it off. After that, all has been fine. I will say one thing, I think a lot of people forget that when they adjust the settings that you have to ring the bell afterwards for the settings to register. That could be part of their confusion.I will say this, I am pleasantly surprised at how well the audio is on this unit. I answer the bell from 35 miles away from my home and it's crystal clear on both sides. No one would ever know I am not in the house.This is a great tool for my kids as well. Now when they are home alone, they can answer the bell from the iPad and see who it is without opening the door. They feel much safer as a result.My only complaints so far;- I also bought the chime as well. I just wish that there were more options for the doorbell sound and that it could be a bit louder.- I wish I could access the camera upon request.If you are guessing whether this would be the right purchase for you and your home for the reasons I mentioned above, you won't be disappointed. And don't worry about the installation. It's a breeze as long as you are capable of drilling straight.** Update **I've attached a video of my mailman that came to my door today. If people are blaming the product for laggy video or it's unclear, just look at the video I snapped just today as an example. If your setup is done properly and you have good wifi strength and upload speeds, there are no issues. Keep in mind that my mailman doesn't walk up my path. He arrives from the left side and walks across my lawn so this snaps the video as he arrives at my mailbox. If he came from anywhere else, it would have started recording sooner.

title: Highly Recommend !!!!!
body: Outstanding Security Enhancement!!! The Ring Video Doorbell is astonishing! This is my first device of this type so I have no other similar doorbell to compare it to. Here is my experience since installation in July 2015:Ring contains all you need in a very nicely presented package/kit and is very simple to install. There is no need to concern yourself about polarity if you decide to hardwire it because it mimics the polarity of your installed doorbell. If you decide to operate on battery only, it is designed to maintain a charge for an insanely long time, which varies according to your usage and settings. It looks great and visitors will notice it right away.My recommendation is to hardwire this if possible. If your wiring needs work it’s a good idea to get it fixed…the features, like being able to hear your doorbell without having your mobile device at the ready, is a huge convenience factor. Also, there is no need to worry about battery charging, as the power is supplied to, and trickle charges the Ring. I keep a spare mobile device on at home so as not to have to always depend on my phone to answer the door. Due to my floor plan, the combination of the spare mobile device, my phone, and the house doorbell, I don’t really require a Ring Chime, which is Ring.Com’s accessory that acts as a bell/chime for really spacious digs, or for those that don’t hardwire the Ring and have to depend on their mobile devices to hear the announcement.I had no issues with WiFi. The notice was less than two seconds. This may be due to the fact that my router is 15 ft from the Ring. The reviews indicate that some are having latency issues and this may be due to router proximity. I turned off iphone WIFi and tested it on LTE (four bars) and there was only about a three second difference in latency. I can certainly live with an extra three seconds, especially knowing that I don't have to worry about racing to the door in time as one does with a traditional doorbell.The first time I answered the Ring I was in a store and was astonished to hear it ring in my shopping cart. I was able to answer in a flash without them even knowing I wasn’t home. You can even answer the door in bed if you want to.The video and audio is amazing. The HD does a great job in presenting the background (love to look at the clouds in the video sometime…. is that a raven or a baby dragon up there?) The audio picked up some visitors whispering about what was growing in the flowerpots by the door. They never did realize they were being recorded. My husband, who is in law enforcement, showed a video to one of his co-workers and without hesitation they said they would order one that night.That brings me to the security features. I’m sure that most of the time the events being recorded…motion detection and rings…are innocent but there may be that one time that it would have been a good idea to have audio and video of a not-so-innocent visit to your property. That video is stored in the cloud, and also available to save to your device to review again later.The developers are very diligent about improving the device and you can expect several welcome enhancements in the near future such as video on demand, video management, web access among others. They seem to want to blow the competition out of the water.

title: Outstanding solution for monitoring entrances
body: I purchased a Ring doorbell to provide better video surveillance of my front door, especially for visitors who came into my front porch but didn't actually ring the doorbell.This unit replaced a traditional mechanical chime button, and I hard wired the Ring into existing low voltage AC wiring. Buyers need to make sure that their AC transformer puts out a voltage within the acceptable range of voltage listed in the installation instructions. Too low and the unit won't recharge properly; too high and you can fry the backplane circuitry (which I did and Ring.com graciously replaced the backplane for me). Check your output voltage BEFORE installing. By har wiring the Ring, I don't have to worry about recharging it, and it also activates my old mechanical doorbell chime.You also need to make sure that you have a good strong wi-fi signal since that helps reduce time lag when the doorbell is activated and improves the quality of the video signal. I have a brick ranch home with aluminum siding and it's tough to get a good signal outside the home, so I installed an outdoor access point on my porch which greatly improved the operation of the Ring unit. I recommend buyers test their unit with their existing access point or wireless router and either move the access point or install a dedicated wi-fi source for the best performance of this product.Installation of the Ring was easy and the instructions were not hard to follow. I set up the online Ring account and subscribed to the annual program for access to recorded videos. This allowed me to go back to older activations when I was away from my phone. I like the ability to receive notifications when away from home for both motion detection and when someone presses the doorbell. Ring implemented an upgrade which sounds a different tone for each (motion detection or doorbell button press) which is very helpful. The motion detection can be very sensitive and I have set it to the closest inner zone to minimize distant motion activations, but I still get alerts when a large vehicle or truck passes by on the street on very sunny days (range of about 60 feet). The video is a nice wide angle lens that clearly captures indentifiable video of visitors.I had a door to door solicitor come by one day who didn't have a permit to solicit. I was able to download the video, burn it to a disk and provide it to the local police who ticketed the solicitor for continuing to sell in the neighborhood after I warned him. The video provided evidence that he was warned about needing a permit and eliminated the "I never was told" defense.I use an iPhone and iPad to view the Ring's alerts using both 4G and wi-fi at home and when traveling. I really appreciate the two-way communication capability of the Ring. Their is a slight time lag on audio, which is understandable given that you're pushing an audio signal through an internal network, out to the Internet and then to your phone. People at my door have said later that they were able to understand what was said to them, and I could hear them clearly, but with some noticeable lag.In summary, make sure you have the necessary electrical infrastructure and wi-fi capability to support a Ring unit and you'll enjoy the connectivity, especially motion detection, that it gives you.

title: The good feeling starts with the packaging
body: This is one of a handful of tech devices I’ve purchased over the years that I can use the word “delighted” to describe how I feel about the investment. The good feeling starts with the packaging. Besides the video doorbell and its mounting bracket, the kit includes various mounting hardware for the different building materials the Ring might be mounted on. It includes a drill bit so you won’t guess wrong on the size of the holes you need, a screwdriver, and a small bubble level to help you mount the doorbell properly.After being amazed at everything included in the package, I was delighted by the amount of information available from the manufacturer about installation, operation, and more. There must be one hundred or more videos from the company with step by step instructions for various types of home siding installations as well as customer videos captured by the doorbell. It was here that I learned that Ring will work just fine whether your doorbell is on the same surface as your door or is, like mine, on a wall perpendicular to the door.The doorbell is very well made. It feels solid and substantial when you hold it. The doorbell uses proprietary screws to connect to the mounting bracket. It is designed for use outside so weather won’t hurt the doorbell. Mine is well sheltered on a porch and I appreciate that the doorbell button is softly lighted. The doorbell is available in four trims to match your décor or taste.Actual installation of the doorbell was as easy as can be. I replaced an existing hard wired low voltage doorbell, which really is the best way to use Ring. The doorbell can also be powered off of its internal battery. Ring says the battery will power the device for a year, but I can’t speak to that.Before you purchase this device for your home, I recommend checking the strength of the Wi-Fi signal at the site of your existing doorbell or wherever you plan to install it. Ring Video Doorbell requires Wi-Fi to work and if your signal is weak at the door, it could result in lag notifying you via your phone or tablet. Ring will still capture the video for later viewing if needed, but timely notification and the ability to talk to the person at the door depends on a good Wi-Fi signal.The Ring app allows you to view live video when someone activates the device either by motion or pushing the button and even talk to person at the door. The app works with iOS, Windows 10, and Android. It is well designed and easy to navigate. It allows you to turn motion notifications off if desired and to adjust the sensitivity of the motion sensor and to turn it off entirely in certain parts of the viewing area. The video camera is 720p resolution with a very wide angle lens. The app also allows you to zoom the view to cutoff the extreme edges of the image and focus more on the area nearest the doorbell. There is not an adjustment that I’ve wished for that isn’t already there.My Ring Video Doorbell has been in place for a while now and works well. The motion sensor uses infrared so blowing leaves and flags won’t set it off. I’ve fine-tuned the sensor for our yard. Doing this assures you don’t become annoyed with too many motion notifications. I’ve yet to have an experience with the Ring Video Doorbell that has been anything but positive.

title: Great product! Love it
body: Great product! Love it. I replaced our "old school" wired doorbell with a Ring Pro video doorbell. I did the wiring myself, although Ring offers a professional installation (at a reasonable price too). The wiring took 15 minutes. The install kit includes everything you’ll need and wiring instructions were simple, complete with pictures and written directions.The software set-up was a little more involved than I expected. This is because I needed a wireless extender. Troubleshooting and remediating the Wi-Fi signal strength took me an hour or so. While the Wi-Fi signal throughout my home is exceptional – including on my front porch – the Ring device is mounted flush against the wall of your house, which can cause signal strength issues. FWIW, we have an old, wood framed house with vinyl siding.The good news is that an inexpensive Wi-Fi signal extender plugged into a power outlet near my front door resolved my signal strength issue. I ordered the ‘NETGEAR N300 Wi-Fi Range Extender, Essentials Edition (EX2700)’ from Amazon – only $28. Took all of 5 minutes to set-up the extender.Once my Ring Pro had both power and a strong Wi-Fi signal, the set-up and configuration only took a few minutes and went very smoothly for me.So if you experience difficulty a.) Connecting to Wi-Fi, b.) Stuck over-the-air software updates, or c.) If Ring features work intermittently, than you most likely need a Wi-Fi Extender. Ring can almost justify bundling an extender with every Ring Plus to avoid the hassle of signal strength issues. In fairness, if you read input on their website, Ring does recommend extender products.I also use Ring’s Chime device, which is an optional Wi-Fi connected device plugged into a regular power outlet that will audibly “chime” when someone rings your doorbell and/or if you want motion detection alerts. This option is awesome. My teenage boys hang out upstairs and don’t always have a their iPhones handy. If the pizza man comes, they will hear the chime and run downstairs. You use the smartphone app to configure the Chime. The Ring smartphone app let’s you configure notification and volume options on the Chime. Pretty slick.Once configured, you will quickly fall in love with this product. The Ring Pro has so many useful features to include: HD quality video; night vision, two-way audio; zone-based motion detection; smartphone alerts; multi-user support; chime; etc.). I also ordered the solar powered Protected by Ring signage, which illuminates in the evening. You obviously put the sign near your door to deter knuckleheads and evildoers.Another huge bonus with Ring is IFTTT support. IFTTT provides Ring users a simple way to virtually connect to other applications (called recipes). For example, I use an IFTTT “recipe” to send a log of all ring alerts (doorbell rings and motion detection) to my email. Other examples include adding logs to your Google calendar, dimming your connected lights (e.g. Philips HUE), or integrate notifications with your existing in-home security system (e.g. ADT Pulse).If like me, you want a useful, cost-effective and reliable smart doorbell; and do not want spend a ton, the Ring Pro is an excellent option.

title: First Line Of Defensive.
body: First Line Of Defense. I Did Not Purchase My Ring Video Doorbell From Amazon. But Through Another Well Known TV /Online Merchant. I Got It On 4 Flex Pays. Let Me Be Honest First. When I Got My Ring Video Doorbell. I Decided To Install First Just Using The Battery Only Option, Just In Case I Didn't Like It Or If It Didn't Work Properly.People Who Are Complaining That The Device Doesn't Work Right. I Can Relate To Them. Because I Had Issues With My First One. Called Customer Service Over 20 Times. I Am Not Exaggerating. It Was Very Frustrating And Time Consuming. I Had To Keep Taking Off And On The Device For The Customer Service Representative Can Troubleshoot. Or Add That Person To My Account For Them Too Look And Change Some Settings.When I Called In Again Because Of Another Issue. They Wanted Me To Do The Same Thing I Have Been Doing Since I Revived It In July, 2016. At That Point I Told The Rep I Am Not Doing That. I Am Tired Of Doing That. And I Am Done With Your Company And Your Products. And When I Was Preparing To Hang Up The Phone.Nevertheless The Rep That I Last Talk To Said Wait. I Don't Want You To Get Off The Phone Feeling That Way. I Want To Help You. Please Let Me Help You. I Said What You Going To Do Differently From Ever Other Rep? He Said I See All The Calls You Have Placed Into Customer Service Since Receiving The Device.So What I Am Going To Do Is Send You Our A New Replacement. Because Sometimes The Firmware Has Problems. And We Just Need To Just Replace The Unit All Together. So He Put The Replacement Order Through. It Took A Few Days To Get Here. I Was Provided A Prepaid Envelope To Return The Old Device In.So I Got The Replacement. I Installed It. And I Noticed The Difference Right Away With The New One. I Didn't Have To Use A WiFi Extender No More Like I Did With The Old One To Get A Good WiFi Connection Signal For The Device To Work Properly. I Didn't Have To Place Anymore Calls To Customer Service Since Having The Replacement Device. Except To Switch My Cloud Subscription Over To The New Device.I Really Like This And The Peace Of Mind It Gives. The Motion Alerts When Someone Is Outside. But They Do Not Ring The Doorbell. For Example The Mailman Or Mail woman. I Get Motion Alerts And Someone Ringing The Doorbell Alerts Anywhere In The World. As Long As I Have A Active Internet Connection On My Smartphone Or Tablet.I Like The Live View Mode So I Can Just See What's Going On Outside At The Time. Just Keep In Mind Live View Only Works When The Doorbell Is Hard Wired To The Wire Connections That Connects To The Existing Doorbell. And The Auto Video Recording Option Is Is A Plus.So If You Are Having Trouble When You Get Yours. Don't Be Like Me And Go Through The Hassle Of Unnecessary Troubleshooting..Not To Say That Every Unit Is Like This. But Some Are. Other Than That It's A Great Line Of Defense Against Potential Crimes And Suspicious Activity Especially If You Have A 3 Month Old Baby Like Me.Also I Notice The Shipping Carriers Put Down My Packages More Gently Since Getting This Lol 😁 They Don't Want To Ring The Doorbell Because They Think It Won't Record Them. How Wrong They Are 😉

title: Love this wireless video doorbell
