body: Love this wireless video doorbell. I researched all the wireless video doorbells and read the reviews before deciding on the Ring. I certainly do not regret my decision. I selected the silver nickel finish that matches my front door hardware. There are several other colors to choose from. One thing I noticed when I received my Ring was that is was smaller then I expected, and this is a good thing. I didn't want some large clunky device near my front door. Setup literally took me about five minutes to install the iOS app and get the Ring on my WPA secured wifi home network. Installation was also easy and you have the option of installing the Ring standalone (which is powered by a long running internal rechargeable battery) or it can hook up to your existing 24VAC wired doorbell. If it is connected to your existing hardwired doorbell it will derive power from the 24VAC transformer. The other advantage of connecting to existing doorbell is that when the Ring is pushed, your phone will give you an audible and visual alert and your existing doorchime will ring. I chose to wire mine to my existing doorbell so I could hear the chime even if I didn't have my phone near me. If you are afraid of connecting wires then you don't have to and installation is a cinch. The internal battery is supposed to last about a year before needing to be recharged (micro USB cable included). The other cool thing is that all the installation tools come with the Ring, screw driver for philips and security bit, masonry drill bit, level, screws and anchors. It can be installed on wood, stucco, concrete, brick.The audio and video quality is great. I had to install my Ring in front of a metal junction box where my existing doorbell button was so I was afraid the metal might limit my wifi range. My wireless access point is about 30' away from my Ring and I have full signal strength so the metal box had little if any impact on my range. The Ring also has motion detection built in and night vision. I can see a person outside the front door even with no outside lights turned on. The motion detection has adjustable range and alert sensitivity so it can notify you if someone walks up to the door and doesnt push the doorbell button. When motion detection sends an alert to your phone, you can answer the Ring and see and hear what is going on without letting anyone know you watching. This is great for security purposes.My favorite part of Ring is the customer service. To me, excellent customer service is a MUST, especially now a days when many companies don't seem to care about the customer. When I received my Ring purchase from Amazon, the picture quality was blurry. I decided to contact Ring customer support before reaching out through Amazon. I explained my problem and Ring sent me an advance replacement. I received my replacement in two days. The replacement video quality was perfect and I have not had a problem since. I returned my original Ring unit in the prepaid padded postal bag/envelope. I am impressed with the speed and ease of dealing with Ring so this is a happy customer! I have been using my video doorbell for about three weeks now and it works great and is very cool.

title: I loved it so much that I got another one for ...
body: I got this unit last month for my front door and also got the chime to go with it from Amazon. I loved it so much that I got another one for the back entrance. The doorbell looks great and the motion sensor is very useful, even when it's nighttime or low lights. In fact, while traveling recently, I got a motion alert during the day and when I looked from 3000 miles away, I realized it's the gardening service man who was doing his job on the front lawn. I realized that I had forgotten to ask him to prune a couple of my trees, so I "accepted" the connection and called out his name. He came closer to the door, although a bit unsure about what was happening, I told him that I was busy and couldn't come to the door, but that I needed him to prune a couple of trees for me. He agreed and went about his work. I thought this was a great use of this device that I hadn't even thought about because mainly and most people think about security issues and uninvited solicitors at the door, which this door bell addresses so nicely. The chime inside the house is also very useful in that many times, my cell phone is in a different part of the house, and I have had to rely on the in-door Chime to let me know someone is at the door. The setup at both front and back entrance was very simple and the videos on their website is thorough and very helpful. The hardest part of the setup for me, was figuring out where in my house, my old doorbell's transformer was located so that I could hookup the video doorbell in the front entrance to existing wiring and never have to worry about charging it. The back entrance, however, has to run on the built-in battery which after I fully charged it, now is down to 85% (after 4 weeks of use). According to the vendor, a future firmware will improve the features of this doorbell including "on-demand" or live view of the camera which would be a great feature to have. A couple of suggestions for future updates: For the doorbell App, it would be nice to have a setting that controls the volume of the Ring app only. Right now the only way to lower the volume, is to use my cell phone volume rocker which affects the whole phone. But if the Ring app interface had a volume control (similar to that of the Chime), it would be great. There is, however, a setting on the App for the door bell which allows you to turn off completely the alert notification, but sometimes I just want the notification to be lowered. Also, when I bought the Chime, on the box it stated that it has multiple chime sounds to select from, but I have not figured out how to do that. Finally, if the doorbell design could be modified in future versions, where the micro USB charging port is located in a more accessible location on the outside of the bell. Even though, it appears that the battery will last a long time on one charge, still when it's time to charge it, you have to unscrew the cover and take the unit off the wall in order to plug it to be charged. Other than these minor improvements, I really feel this unit gives me a great peace of mind when we're not home or when solicitors ring the door bell and I don't feel like dealing with them. Great device, thank you Ring.

title: Worth My Tiny Monthly Social Security, on Which I Must Now Live
body: I can't come up with ANY negatives, and am grateful that I got a donation to help me purchase what I hope might now be a life-saver.Ring is SO cool!I noticed that someone left a review with several comments, and I'd like to address THIS one..."(2) You cannot access your videos with a computer -- only with your Android or iOS device. So if you are at work and forgot your phone, forget seeing any activity or getting any alerts at your door. You also cannot view past videos on your computer or archive video onto your computer. If you need a video clip for legal reasons, like someone breaking into your house, that's not happening. You can SHOW the cops the video on your phone but unless you have some type of app like ScreenShare, no actual video."Are you KIDDING ME?!??!??!?!! I have an Android, but use it almost exclusive for making/receiving calls (not while driving), and that's only occasionally. I'm extremely reliant upon my PC (if only for the monitor size). I can see everything via my computer, and I most definitely CAN view "past videos"! And I've easily sent one or more video "clips," as the reviewer stated, to several people and so, since the Los Angeles County Sheriff's Department has many public email addresses, I know I can do the same, if need be.Anyway, this device is going to improve in ways that most of us may not even think of and, in the meantime, I have zero complaints. Besides -- and this is REALLY important -- Ring WANTS advice/critique/whatever -- from those of us who now are making use of their fine product. THAT, in and of itself, says a whole lot about the worthiness of an up-and-company who deserves lots. In fact, I had a wee bit of a problem deleting a handful of unwanted videos, so I called, spoke with a really nice guy (who totally got what I was saying), and was grateful (as I had expected) that I'd found a small glitch on multiple deletions via my PC. He said he'd bring it up to the techs, which I hope is helping out that team, as well.I really like Ring.__________________________________________UPDATE - 10-19-15I spoke with one of Ring's exceptional representatives about 1-1/2 weeks ago. I'd been having trouble deleting multiple video files with one click on "Delete." But the rep totally understood the small problem and reassured me he would immediately address the issue.As of today -- when I finally had time to check -- he undoubtedly really HAD done his job, and PROMPTLY!So, this is something worth telling you potential buyers: If you get lots and lots and lots of video files each day, you can now click on whichever and HOWEVER MANY video files you may wish to delete, then click on the "Delete" tab at the top of your personal account information, and all of those checked-only files will instantly delete (and only after you're asked if you're "SURE" you mean to delete such-and-such).You can do this on a daily basis, a weekly basis, or whenever you want to just clean up all those many video files.Ring comes through... AGAIN!
